## Findings

### P05-F01: Unused variable created in all files
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variable `al` is created by parsing `access_level` to an integer but is never used in any of the 9 JSP files. This occurs at line 31 in most files (line 46 in print_curr_driv_report.jsp and print_curr_unit_report.jsp, line 30 in print_daily_driver_summary.jsp and print_daily_veh_summary.jsp, line 29 in print_driver_access_abuse.jsp, line 36 in print_dyn_driver_report.jsp and print_dyn_unit_report.jsp).
**Fix:** Remove the unused variable declaration `int al = Integer.parseInt(access_level);` from all files.
**File:** au_print/print_battery.jsp, au_print/print_broadcastmsg.jsp, au_print/print_curr_driv_report.jsp, au_print/print_curr_unit_report.jsp, au_print/print_daily_driver_summary.jsp, au_print/print_daily_veh_summary.jsp, au_print/print_driver_access_abuse.jsp, au_print/print_dyn_driver_report.jsp, au_print/print_dyn_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 31 (or 46, 30, 29, 36 depending on file)

### P05-F02: Commented-out code block in print_curr_driv_report.jsp
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** A body tag is commented out, leaving unclear intent. The comment suggests this was an alternative to the auto-printing onload body tag.
**Fix:** Remove the commented-out code at line 88: `<!-- <body> -->`
**File:** au_print/print_curr_driv_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 88

### P05-F03: Multiple blocks of commented-out code in print_driver_access_abuse.jsp
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Several sections contain commented-out code including an unused ArrayList variable and commented table header/cell elements. These create ambiguity about whether the code should be reinstated or deleted.
**Fix:** Remove all commented-out code blocks: line 62 (ArrayList a_driv_id), lines 84-85 (Driver Id header), lines 90-91 (Model header), and lines 123-124 (Model display cell).
**File:** au_print/print_driver_access_abuse.jsp
**Line(s):** 62, 84-85, 90-91, 123-124

### P05-F04: Large blocks of commented-out code in print_dyn_driver_report.jsp
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Multiple sections of commented-out code exist, including a complete table header row (lines 158-171) and repeated commented sections within the loop (lines 232-233, 239-240). This clutters the codebase and creates confusion about intended functionality.
**Fix:** Remove all commented-out code blocks at lines 158-171, 232-233, and 239-240.
**File:** au_print/print_dyn_driver_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 158-171, 232-233, 239-240

### P05-F05: Large blocks of commented-out code in print_dyn_unit_report.jsp
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Similar to print_dyn_driver_report.jsp, this file contains a complete commented table header row (lines 163-176) and repeated commented sections within the loop (lines 235-236, 242-243).
**Fix:** Remove all commented-out code blocks at lines 163-176, 235-236, and 242-243.
**File:** au_print/print_dyn_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 163-176, 235-236, 242-243

### P05-F06: Deprecated StringTokenizer usage
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** StringTokenizer is a legacy class that has been superseded by String.split(). Both print_dyn_driver_report.jsp and print_dyn_unit_report.jsp use StringTokenizer to parse the comma-delimited do_list parameter.
**Fix:** Replace `StringTokenizer Tok = new StringTokenizer(do_list,delims);` with `String[] tokens = do_list.split(",");` and update the iteration logic to use a for-each loop or array index iteration instead of `hasMoreElements()` and `nextToken()`.
**File:** au_print/print_dyn_driver_report.jsp, au_print/print_dyn_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 85-103 (print_dyn_driver_report.jsp), 86-105 (print_dyn_unit_report.jsp)

### P05-F07: Raw ArrayList types without generics
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** All files except print_broadcastmsg.jsp (line 49) and print_driver_access_abuse.jsp (lines 69-70) use raw ArrayList types without generic type parameters. This generates unchecked compiler warnings and reduces type safety. For example, print_battery.jsp lines 49-56, 78-81 all use raw ArrayList.
**Fix:** Add appropriate generic type parameters to all ArrayList declarations. For example, change `ArrayList vcust_cd = filter.getCust_cd();` to `ArrayList<String> vcust_cd = filter.getCust_cd();` (assuming the getter returns ArrayList<String>).
**File:** au_print/print_battery.jsp, au_print/print_curr_driv_report.jsp, au_print/print_curr_unit_report.jsp, au_print/print_daily_driver_summary.jsp, au_print/print_daily_veh_summary.jsp, au_print/print_driver_access_abuse.jsp, au_print/print_dyn_driver_report.jsp, au_print/print_dyn_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** Multiple lines across all files (e.g., 49-56, 78-81 in print_battery.jsp)

### P05-F08: Inconsistent variable naming convention
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variable naming is inconsistent across files. Some use uppercase prefix (Vloc_cd, Vloc_nm at print_battery.jsp:51-52) while most use lowercase (vloc_cd, vveh_cd). Additionally, some files use vdep_cd while others use vdept_cd for the same concept.
**Fix:** Standardize all collection variable names to use lowercase prefix. Change `ArrayList Vloc_cd` to `ArrayList vloc_cd` and `ArrayList Vloc_nm` to `ArrayList vloc_nm`. Standardize dept variable naming to consistently use either vdep_cd or vdept_cd across all files.
**File:** au_print/print_battery.jsp, au_print/print_curr_driv_report.jsp, au_print/print_curr_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 51-52 (print_battery.jsp), 65-68 (print_curr_driv_report.jsp and print_curr_unit_report.jsp)

### P05-F09: Inconsistent filter setter method naming
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** The Databean_report bean uses `setSet_opcode()` (print_broadcastmsg.jsp:39, print_daily_driver_summary.jsp:38, etc.) while Databean_dyn_reports bean uses `setSet_op_code()` (print_battery.jsp:40). This naming inconsistency is confusing.
**Fix:** Standardize the method name across both bean classes. Recommend using `setOpCode()` following JavaBeans naming conventions, or if the "Set_" prefix is intentional (for a property named "set_op_code"), ensure it's consistent across all beans.
**File:** au_print/print_broadcastmsg.jsp, au_print/print_daily_driver_summary.jsp, au_print/print_daily_veh_summary.jsp, au_print/print_driver_access_abuse.jsp vs au_print/print_battery.jsp, au_print/print_curr_driv_report.jsp, au_print/print_curr_unit_report.jsp, au_print/print_dyn_driver_report.jsp, au_print/print_dyn_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 39 (print_broadcastmsg.jsp), 38 (print_daily_driver_summary.jsp), 40 (print_battery.jsp), 55 (print_curr_driv_report.jsp), 45 (print_dyn_driver_report.jsp)

### P05-F10: Hardcoded external URLs
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** print_battery.jsp contains hardcoded absolute URLs (http://fleetfocus.lindemh.com.au/fms/images/linde/...) for images at lines 92 and 101. These should be configurable like the LindeConfig.externalURL references used in other files.
**Fix:** Replace hardcoded URLs with LindeConfig.externalURL references: `<img src="<%=LindeConfig.externalURL %>/images/linde/linde_long_logo.gif" ...>` and `<img src="<%=LindeConfig.externalURL %>/images/linde/Logo1.gif" ...>`.
**File:** au_print/print_battery.jsp
**Line(s):** 92, 101

### P05-F11: Duplicated access control code across all files
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** All 9 files contain nearly identical access control code (session attribute retrieval, default value assignment, filter setup) spanning approximately 15-18 lines. This violates DRY principle and makes maintenance difficult. Any change to access control logic must be replicated across all files.
**Fix:** Extract access control logic into a separate JSP include file (e.g., includes/access_control.jsp) that sets up the filter with access control parameters. This include can be called after filter instantiation and before filter.init() in each file.
**File:** au_print/print_battery.jsp, au_print/print_broadcastmsg.jsp, au_print/print_curr_driv_report.jsp, au_print/print_curr_unit_report.jsp, au_print/print_daily_driver_summary.jsp, au_print/print_daily_veh_summary.jsp, au_print/print_driver_access_abuse.jsp, au_print/print_dyn_driver_report.jsp, au_print/print_dyn_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 20-38 (print_battery.jsp and similar ranges in other files)

### P05-F12: Magic string literals for default values
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Multiple files use magic string literals such as "5" for default access_level, "1" for div_cd, and various op_code strings. These should be defined as constants for better maintainability.
**Fix:** Define these values as constants in a configuration class or at the top of the JSP files. For example, define `public static final String DEFAULT_ACCESS_LEVEL = "5";` and `public static final String DEFAULT_DIV_CD = "1";` in a constants class.
**File:** au_print/print_battery.jsp, au_print/print_broadcastmsg.jsp, au_print/print_curr_driv_report.jsp, au_print/print_curr_unit_report.jsp, au_print/print_daily_driver_summary.jsp, au_print/print_daily_veh_summary.jsp, au_print/print_driver_access_abuse.jsp, au_print/print_dyn_driver_report.jsp, au_print/print_dyn_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 29 (default "5"), 17-41 (various op_code strings), 41-56 (div_cd "1")

### P05-F13: Missing null safety checks on session attributes
**Severity:** HIGH
**Description:** All files retrieve session attributes (access_level, access_cust, access_site, access_dept) without null checks. If the session has expired or attributes are not set, this will cause NullPointerException at lines like 27 (`if(access_level.equalsIgnoreCase(""))`).
**Fix:** Add null checks before using session attributes: `String access_level = (String)session.getAttribute("access_level"); if(access_level == null || access_level.equalsIgnoreCase("")) { access_level = "5"; }`
**File:** au_print/print_battery.jsp, au_print/print_broadcastmsg.jsp, au_print/print_curr_driv_report.jsp, au_print/print_curr_unit_report.jsp, au_print/print_daily_driver_summary.jsp, au_print/print_daily_veh_summary.jsp, au_print/print_driver_access_abuse.jsp, au_print/print_dyn_driver_report.jsp, au_print/print_dyn_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 22-27 (and similar ranges in other files)

### P05-F14: Unused request parameter 'message'
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Several files retrieve a 'message' request parameter but never use it (print_battery.jsp:18, print_curr_driv_report.jsp:33, print_curr_unit_report.jsp:33, print_dyn_driver_report.jsp:23, print_dyn_unit_report.jsp:23).
**Fix:** Remove the unused parameter retrieval: `String message=request.getParameter("message")==null?"":request.getParameter("message");`
**File:** au_print/print_battery.jsp, au_print/print_curr_driv_report.jsp, au_print/print_curr_unit_report.jsp, au_print/print_dyn_driver_report.jsp, au_print/print_dyn_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 18 (print_battery.jsp), 33 (print_curr_driv_report.jsp and print_curr_unit_report.jsp), 23 (print_dyn_driver_report.jsp and print_dyn_unit_report.jsp)

### P05-F15: Unused ArrayList variables in print_curr_driv_report.jsp and print_curr_unit_report.jsp
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variables vveh_id and vveh_typ_cd are retrieved from the filter but never displayed or used in the output.
**Fix:** Remove unused variable retrievals at line 90 (vveh_id) and line 92 (vveh_typ_cd) if they are truly not needed, or add them to the display if they were intended to be shown.
**File:** au_print/print_curr_driv_report.jsp, au_print/print_curr_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 90, 92

### P05-F16: Unused ArrayList variable in print_driver_access_abuse.jsp
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variable a_ol_end is retrieved from the filter (getA_ol_end_list()) but never used in the output. Only a_ol_start is displayed.
**Fix:** Remove the unused variable retrieval at line 70: `ArrayList<String> a_ol_end = filter.getA_ol_end_list();` or add it to the display if it was intended to be shown.
**File:** au_print/print_driver_access_abuse.jsp
**Line(s):** 70

### P05-F17: Unused request parameters in print_dyn_driver_report.jsp and print_dyn_unit_report.jsp
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Parameters sort_by, sort_asc, and undo_list are retrieved but never used in the print versions of these reports. These parameters appear to be relevant only for the interactive (non-print) versions where users can sort and hide/unhide columns.
**Fix:** Remove unused parameter retrievals at lines 18-19 (sort_by, sort_asc) and line 17 (undo_list) from both files.
**File:** au_print/print_dyn_driver_report.jsp, au_print/print_dyn_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 17-19

### P05-F18: Unused ArrayList vfld_hide in print_dyn_driver_report.jsp and print_dyn_unit_report.jsp
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** An ArrayList vfld_hide is created at line 74 but never used. Only the String array fld_hide[] is actually used throughout the code.
**Fix:** Remove the unused ArrayList declaration: `ArrayList vfld_hide = new ArrayList();`
**File:** au_print/print_dyn_driver_report.jsp, au_print/print_dyn_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 74 (print_dyn_driver_report.jsp), 75 (print_dyn_unit_report.jsp)

### P05-F19: Deprecated HTML attributes throughout all files
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** All JSP files use deprecated HTML attributes including: bgcolor on body/tr/td/table elements, align attributes on tables and cells, border/bordercolor/cellpadding/cellspacing on tables, and language="JavaScript" on script tags. These are deprecated in HTML5 and should be replaced with CSS.
**Fix:** Move all styling to CSS classes. Replace `bgcolor`, `align`, `border`, `cellpadding`, `cellspacing` with CSS equivalents. Change `language="JavaScript"` to `type="text/javascript"` or remove entirely for HTML5.
**File:** au_print/print_battery.jsp, au_print/print_broadcastmsg.jsp, au_print/print_curr_driv_report.jsp, au_print/print_curr_unit_report.jsp, au_print/print_daily_driver_summary.jsp, au_print/print_daily_veh_summary.jsp, au_print/print_driver_access_abuse.jsp, au_print/print_dyn_driver_report.jsp, au_print/print_dyn_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** Multiple throughout all files (e.g., lines 9-14 in print_curr_driv_report.jsp for script language, lines 87, 90+ for bgcolor/align)

### P05-F20: Inconsistent CSS class naming between files
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Some files use class="footernote" (print_daily_veh_summary.jsp:80) while others use class="footerNote" (print_daily_driver_summary.jsp:80, print_dyn_driver_report.jsp:135, print_dyn_unit_report.jsp:137). CSS class names are case-sensitive.
**Fix:** Standardize the CSS class name across all files. Choose either "footernote" or "footerNote" and update all references consistently. Verify the corresponding CSS file uses the same case.
**File:** au_print/print_daily_driver_summary.jsp, au_print/print_daily_veh_summary.jsp, au_print/print_dyn_driver_report.jsp, au_print/print_dyn_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 80 (print_daily_driver_summary.jsp and print_daily_veh_summary.jsp), 135 (print_dyn_driver_report.jsp), 137 (print_dyn_unit_report.jsp)

## Findings

### P06-F01: Commented-out code block in print_impact_report.jsp
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** A commented-out line of code exists at line 152 that appears to perform impact percentage conversion using DataUtil. This commented code should either be removed or reinstated with appropriate justification.
**Fix:** Either remove the commented line entirely if no longer needed, or uncomment and restore the functionality if impact percentage display is required.
**File:** au_print/print_impact_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 152

### P06-F02: Inconsistent use of raw ArrayList types throughout all files
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** All JSP files use raw ArrayList types without generics (e.g., `ArrayList veh_cd` instead of `ArrayList<String> veh_cd`), which generates unchecked type warnings during compilation. This is a deprecated pattern in modern Java and reduces type safety.
**Fix:** Add generic type parameters to all ArrayList declarations throughout the codebase (e.g., `ArrayList<String> veh_cd = filter.getVmachine_cd();`). This requires updating the filter bean methods to return typed collections as well.
**File:** All files
**Line(s):** Multiple occurrences in all 10 files

### P06-F03: Inconsistent collection types (Vector vs ArrayList) for same data pattern
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** The file print_preop_chk.jsp uses Vector for collections (lines 52-60), while print_preop_chk_fail.jsp and print_preop_chk_inc.jsp use ArrayList for the exact same data pattern from the same bean methods. This inconsistency suggests lack of standardization and may indicate an incomplete refactoring.
**Fix:** Standardize on ArrayList throughout all files for consistency, as Vector is a legacy collection type. Update print_preop_chk.jsp to use ArrayList instead of Vector to match the other preop check files.
**File:** au_print/print_preop_chk.jsp
**Line(s):** 52-60

### P06-F04: Duplicate code pattern across utilization report files
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** The files print_key_hour_util.jsp and print_seat_hour_util.jsp contain nearly identical code structure (same initialization pattern, same rendering logic, same table structure). The only differences are the op_code values ("key_hour" vs "seat_hour"). This duplication violates DRY principle and makes maintenance harder.
**Fix:** Refactor the two files to use a common JSP include file with the op_code passed as a parameter, or create a parameterized report template that both can use.
**File:** au_print/print_key_hour_util.jsp, au_print/print_seat_hour_util.jsp
**Line(s):** Entire files (1-234, 1-232 respectively)

### P06-F05: Inconsistent null checking pattern for request parameters
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Most files use inline ternary operators for null checking request parameters (e.g., `request.getParameter("gp_cd")==null?"1":request.getParameter("gp_cd")`), which calls getParameter twice. This is inconsistent and inefficient compared to storing the result first.
**Fix:** Standardize on a consistent pattern: store the parameter value once, then check for null. Example: `String param = request.getParameter("gp_cd"); String gp_cd = param == null ? "1" : param;`
**File:** All files
**Line(s):** Lines 8-16 (approximately) in all files

### P06-F06: Unused variable 'al' in all files
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** All files declare an integer variable `al` from parsing `access_level` (e.g., line 32 in most files), but this variable is never subsequently used in any of the files. This is dead code that serves no purpose.
**Fix:** Remove the unused variable declaration `int al = Integer.parseInt(access_level);` from all files.
**File:** All files
**Line(s):** Approximately line 32 in each file (print_impact_meter_report.jsp:32, print_impact_report.jsp:32, print_key_hour_util.jsp:32, etc.)

### P06-F07: Unused variables in initialization blocks
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Several files retrieve data into variables that are never used. In print_impact_meter_report.jsp and print_impact_report.jsp, variables `veh_cd`, `veh_nm`, and `veh_typ_nm1` are retrieved but never referenced (lines 56-58). In print_key_hour_util.jsp and print_seat_hour_util.jsp, variable `veh_cd` is retrieved but never used (line 50-51).
**Fix:** Remove the unused variable declarations and corresponding filter getter calls to reduce memory overhead and improve code clarity.
**File:** au_print/print_impact_meter_report.jsp, au_print/print_impact_report.jsp, au_print/print_key_hour_util.jsp, au_print/print_seat_hour_util.jsp
**Line(s):** print_impact_meter_report.jsp:56-58, print_impact_report.jsp:56-58, print_key_hour_util.jsp:50, print_seat_hour_util.jsp:50

### P06-F08: Inconsistent naming convention for variable prefixes
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variables use inconsistent prefixing patterns: some use "V" prefix (Vveh_cd, Vhire_no, Vdriver), some use "v" prefix (veh_cd, vutil1, vmachine_nm), and some use "sm_" prefix (sm_ser_st, sm_ser_ed). This inconsistency makes the codebase harder to read and maintain.
**Fix:** Establish and enforce a consistent naming convention. Consider using camelCase without prefixes for local variables (e.g., vehicleCode, hireNumber, driverList) or stick to one prefix style if Hungarian notation is preferred.
**File:** All files
**Line(s):** Multiple occurrences throughout all files

### P06-F09: Inconsistent string comparison methods
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** The codebase uses both `equals()` and `equalsIgnoreCase()` for string comparisons inconsistently. For example, in print_preop_chk_fail.jsp, lines 93-94 use `equals("-")` while line 126 also uses `equals("-")`, but line 117 uses `equalsIgnoreCase("1")` for the same type of string literal comparison.
**Fix:** Standardize on using `equals()` for case-sensitive comparisons of string literals and `equalsIgnoreCase()` only when case-insensitive comparison is semantically required. For comparing against string literals like "-", "1", "0", use `equals()` consistently.
**File:** All files
**Line(s):** Multiple occurrences throughout all files

### P06-F10: Missing HTML table closing tag in print_shock_summary.jsp
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** The file print_shock_summary.jsp has a `</thead>` closing tag on line 67 without a corresponding opening `<thead>` tag. The table header row starts directly with `<tr>` without being wrapped in a `<thead>` element.
**Fix:** Either remove the spurious `</thead>` tag on line 67, or add a proper `<thead>` opening tag before line 56 to properly wrap the header row.
**File:** au_print/print_shock_summary.jsp
**Line(s):** 67

### P06-F11: Inconsistent HTML attribute quotation
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** HTML attributes are inconsistently quoted throughout the files. Some files use `align=center` without quotes (e.g., print_impact_meter_report.jsp:76, 94), while others properly quote attributes. Modern HTML standards require quoted attribute values.
**Fix:** Ensure all HTML attribute values are properly quoted. Change `align=center` to `align="center"`, `bgcolor=<%=bgcol%>` to `bgcolor="<%=bgcol%>"`, etc.
**File:** All files
**Line(s):** Multiple occurrences throughout all files (e.g., print_impact_meter_report.jsp:76, 94, 113)

### P06-F12: Duplicate access level control code pattern across all files
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** All 10 files contain identical access level control code blocks (lines 21-39 approximately in each file) that retrieve session attributes, check for empty string, parse to integer, and set filter properties. This represents significant code duplication that violates DRY principle.
**Fix:** Extract this common access level control logic into a utility method or a JSP include file that can be reused across all report pages. This would reduce maintenance burden and ensure consistent behavior.
**File:** All files
**Line(s):** Approximately lines 21-39 in each file

### P06-F13: Unused variable 'model_cd' in multiple files
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Several files retrieve the 'model_cd' request parameter but never use it. Specifically in print_impact_meter_report.jsp (line 10), print_impact_report.jsp (line 10), print_key_hour_util.jsp (line 11), print_preop_chk.jsp (line 10), print_preop_chk_fail.jsp (line 10), print_shock_summary.jsp (line 10), and print_unit_unlock.jsp (line 13).
**Fix:** Remove the unused model_cd parameter retrieval from files where it's not used, or implement the model filtering functionality if it was intended.
**File:** au_print/print_impact_meter_report.jsp, au_print/print_impact_report.jsp, au_print/print_key_hour_util.jsp, au_print/print_preop_chk.jsp, au_print/print_preop_chk_fail.jsp, au_print/print_shock_summary.jsp, au_print/print_unit_unlock.jsp
**Line(s):** Line 10 (or 11, 13 in some files)

### P06-F14: Inconsistent form element usage
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** All files include a `<form>` element that serves no functional purpose (no action, no method, no submit mechanism). The form appears to be a legacy container with no actual form functionality. File print_serv_maintenance.jsp doesn't even have a form element, while the others do.
**Fix:** Either remove the unnecessary form tags if they serve no purpose, or document why they're required. Ensure consistency across all files - either all should have forms with documented purpose, or none should.
**File:** All files except print_serv_maintenance.jsp
**Line(s):** Form declarations throughout files

### P06-F15: Hardcoded color values instead of CSS classes
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Multiple files use inline bgcolor attributes with hardcoded color values (e.g., `bgcolor="lightgrey"`, `bgcolor="white"`, `bgcolor='red'`, `bgcolor='orange'`, `bgcolor='cyan'`) instead of using CSS classes. This violates separation of concerns and makes theming difficult.
**Fix:** Define CSS classes for different row states and severity levels, then apply these classes instead of inline styles. This improves maintainability and allows for centralized theme management.
**File:** All files
**Line(s):** Multiple occurrences throughout all files (e.g., print_impact_meter_report.jsp:121, 145, 147, 149, 155)

### P06-F16: Missing file in print_impact_meter_report.jsp vs print_impact_report.jsp
**Severity:** INFO
**Description:** print_impact_meter_report.jsp has an additional data field that print_impact_report.jsp lacks. Specifically, print_impact_meter_report.jsp retrieves and displays `Vtmp_imp_data` (line 68, used at line 140), and has an "Impact Data" column (line 87). print_impact_report.jsp does not have this column or data. This suggests the two files serve different purposes but the naming doesn't clearly indicate this distinction.
**Fix:** Consider renaming the files to better reflect their different purposes, or document why one includes meter-specific impact data and the other doesn't. This will help maintainers understand when to use which file.
**File:** au_print/print_impact_meter_report.jsp, au_print/print_impact_report.jsp
**Line(s):** print_impact_meter_report.jsp:68, 87, 140

### P06-F17: Deprecated onload attribute with inline JavaScript
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** All files use the deprecated `onload` attribute in the body tag (e.g., `<body bgcolor="white" onload="print('document');window.close();">`) to trigger print and close actions. This inline JavaScript is not a best practice and mixes behavior with markup.
**Fix:** Move the print and close logic to an external JavaScript file or a script block, and attach it using proper event listeners. This improves separation of concerns and makes the code more maintainable.
**File:** All files
**Line(s):** Line 72 in most files (varies slightly: print_impact_meter_report.jsp:72, print_impact_report.jsp:71, print_key_hour_util.jsp:78, etc.)

### P06-F18: Potential null pointer exception in access_level handling
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** All files check if `access_level.equalsIgnoreCase("")` is true (e.g., line 28 in most files), but this will throw a NullPointerException if `access_level` retrieved from session is null. The check should handle null case before calling methods on the string.
**Fix:** Change the check to handle null explicitly: `if(access_level == null || access_level.equalsIgnoreCase(""))`. Alternatively, use a utility method like `StringUtils.isEmpty()` or `Objects.toString(access_level, "").isEmpty()`.
**File:** All files
**Line(s):** Approximately line 28 in each file (print_impact_meter_report.jsp:28, print_impact_report.jsp:28, etc.)

## Findings

### P07-F01: Commented-Out Code Block
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Lines 177-184 contain a commented-out HTML table that displays raw vehicle data array. This commented code serves no documentation purpose and should either be removed or reinstated if needed. Keeping commented code in production files increases maintenance burden and creates confusion about intended functionality.
**Fix:** Remove the commented-out table block (lines 177-184). If this debug view is still needed, move it to a separate debug page or implement it as a conditional feature flag.
**File:** au_print/print_util_wow.jsp
**Line(s):** 177-184

### P07-F02: Unused Variable - access level
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variable `al` is declared and assigned the parsed integer value of access_level (line 32) but is never used anywhere in the code. This creates dead code and suggests incomplete implementation or leftover code from refactoring.
**Fix:** Remove the unused variable declaration and assignment at line 32: `int al = Integer.parseInt(access_level);`
**File:** au_print/print_util_summary.jsp
**Line(s):** 32

### P07-F03: Unused Variable - access level
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variable `al` is declared and assigned the parsed integer value of access_level (line 49) but is never used anywhere in the code. This is the same issue as F02 in a different file, indicating a copy-paste pattern.
**Fix:** Remove the unused variable declaration and assignment at line 49: `int al = Integer.parseInt(access_level);`
**File:** au_print/print_util_wow.jsp
**Line(s):** 49

### P07-F04: Raw Type Usage Without Generics
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Multiple ArrayList declarations use raw types without generic type parameters (lines 51-59). This generates unchecked compiler warnings and loses type safety. The code retrieves ArrayLists from filter methods but doesn't specify the element type, which can lead to ClassCastException at runtime and makes the code harder to maintain.
**Fix:** Add generic type parameters to ArrayList declarations. For example: `ArrayList<String> vrpt_field_cd = filter.getVrpt_field_cd();`. This requires updating the filter bean's getter methods to return properly typed collections, or at minimum adding @SuppressWarnings("unchecked") with a justification comment.
**File:** au_print/print_util_summary.jsp
**Line(s):** 51-59

### P07-F05: Raw Type Usage Without Generics
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Multiple ArrayList declarations use raw types without generic type parameters (lines 70-81). Same issue as F04, creating unchecked warnings and reducing type safety. The pattern is repeated across multiple files suggesting a systemic codebase issue.
**Fix:** Add generic type parameters to ArrayList declarations. Example: `ArrayList<String> Vgp_cd = filter.getGroup_cd();`. Update filter bean return types to use generics properly.
**File:** au_print/print_util_wow.jsp
**Line(s):** 70-81

### P07-F06: Raw Type Usage Without Generics
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** ArrayList declarations at lines 51-54 use raw types without generic type parameters, generating unchecked warnings. Same pattern as F04 and F05.
**Fix:** Add generic type parameters: `ArrayList<String> Vloc_cd = filter.getLocation_cd();` and similarly for other ArrayList declarations.
**File:** au_print/print_utilisation.jsp
**Line(s):** 51-54

### P07-F07: Potential NullPointerException - Missing Null Check
**Severity:** HIGH
**Description:** Line 28 calls `access_level.equalsIgnoreCase("")` without first checking if access_level is null. If session.getAttribute("access_level") returns null (which is possible if session is invalid or attribute not set), this will throw a NullPointerException. The code then attempts to parse the string on line 32, which would also fail.
**Fix:** Add null check before using access_level: `if(access_level == null || access_level.equalsIgnoreCase("")) { access_level = "5"; }`. This ensures defensive programming against session expiry or missing attributes.
**File:** au_print/print_util_summary.jsp
**Line(s):** 28

### P07-F08: Potential NullPointerException - Missing Null Check
**Severity:** HIGH
**Description:** Line 45 calls `access_level.equalsIgnoreCase("")` without null check. Same vulnerability as F07 - if session attribute is null, this throws NPE. This is a repeated pattern across multiple files indicating a systemic security/stability issue.
**Fix:** Add null check: `if(access_level == null || access_level.equalsIgnoreCase("")) { access_level = "5"; }`
**File:** au_print/print_util_wow.jsp
**Line(s):** 45

### P07-F09: Potential NullPointerException - Missing Null Check
**Severity:** HIGH
**Description:** Line 27 calls `access_level.equalsIgnoreCase("")` without null check. Same vulnerability as F07 and F08. This pattern appears in all three JSP files being audited, suggesting copy-paste programming without proper defensive coding.
**Fix:** Add null check: `if(access_level == null || access_level.equalsIgnoreCase("")) { access_level = "5"; }`
**File:** au_print/print_utilisation.jsp
**Line(s):** 27

### P07-F10: Inconsistent Code Pattern - Access Control
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** The access control initialization block (lines 21-32 in print_util_summary.jsp, lines 38-49 in print_util_wow.jsp, lines 20-31 in print_utilisation.jsp) is duplicated across all three files with identical logic. This violates DRY principle and creates maintenance burden - any fix to the null check issue (F07-F09) must be applied three times.
**Fix:** Refactor the access control initialization into a shared utility method or include file that all JSPs can reference. This centralizes the logic and ensures consistent behavior across all report pages.
**File:** au_print/print_util_summary.jsp, au_print/print_util_wow.jsp, au_print/print_utilisation.jsp
**Line(s):** print_util_summary.jsp:21-32, print_util_wow.jsp:38-49, print_utilisation.jsp:20-31

### P07-F11: Inconsistent Style - Inline Scriptlet Formatting
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Inconsistent formatting of inline scriptlets within HTML. Some use `<%=variable %>` with spaces (e.g., line 81), while table structure uses inconsistent indentation. Lines 76-80 show an if-else block that could be simplified using ternary operator for the bgcolor attribute.
**Fix:** Standardize scriptlet formatting and consider using CSS classes instead of inline bgcolor attributes. Replace the if-else block (lines 76-80) with: `<tr align=center class="<%=i%2==0 ? "" : "grey-row"%>">` and define CSS class for grey-row.
**File:** au_print/print_util_summary.jsp
**Line(s):** 76-80

### P07-F12: Inconsistent Style - Mixed Tag Attribute Quoting
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** HTML attributes inconsistently quoted. Line 65 uses `align=center` without quotes, line 62 uses `onload="print('document');window.close();"` with quotes. Modern HTML standards require quoted attributes.
**Fix:** Add quotes to all HTML attributes for consistency and HTML5 compliance. Change `align=center` to `align="center"`, etc.
**File:** au_print/print_util_summary.jsp
**Line(s):** 62, 64, 65, 73, 77, 79, 92

### P07-F13: Inconsistent Style - Mixed Tag Attribute Quoting
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Same issue as F12 - HTML attributes without quotes throughout the file. Lines 132-166 in the header table use inconsistent quoting.
**Fix:** Quote all HTML attributes consistently.
**File:** au_print/print_util_wow.jsp
**Line(s):** 127, 131, 169

### P07-F14: Inconsistent Style - Mixed Tag Attribute Quoting
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Same issue as F12 and F13 - unquoted HTML attributes in multiple locations.
**Fix:** Quote all HTML attributes consistently.
**File:** au_print/print_utilisation.jsp
**Line(s):** 85, 90, 109, 111, 139

### P07-F15: Deprecated HTML - bgcolor Attribute
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Inline bgcolor attributes used throughout (e.g., lines 62, 64, 79). The bgcolor attribute is deprecated in HTML5 and should be replaced with CSS. This mixes presentation with structure and makes theme changes difficult.
**Fix:** Remove inline bgcolor attributes and use CSS classes. Define classes in the included CSS file and apply them via class attributes. For example, replace `bgcolor="white"` with `class="white-bg"` and `bgcolor="lightgrey"` with `class="alternate-row"`.
**File:** au_print/print_util_summary.jsp
**Line(s):** 62, 64, 79

### P07-F16: Deprecated HTML - bgcolor Attribute
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Same issue as F15 - deprecated bgcolor attributes used in multiple locations including the body tag and table elements.
**Fix:** Replace with CSS classes.
**File:** au_print/print_util_wow.jsp
**Line(s):** 127, 168

### P07-F17: Deprecated HTML - bgcolor Attribute
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Same issue as F15 and F16 - deprecated bgcolor attributes throughout the file.
**Fix:** Replace with CSS classes.
**File:** au_print/print_utilisation.jsp
**Line(s):** 85, 89, 111

### P07-F18: Inconsistent Naming Convention
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variable naming inconsistent - some use camelCase (e.g., `gen_sdttm`, `gen_fdttm` at lines 8-9) while others use underscores (e.g., `gp_cd`, `user_cd`, `loc_cd`). Within the same scriptlet, `Vgp_cd` (line 70) uses capital V prefix while `vdept_cd` (line 76) uses lowercase v prefix.
**Fix:** Standardize on one naming convention. Java convention would be camelCase for local variables (e.g., `gpCode`, `userCode`) or maintain the underscore convention consistently but fix the capital/lowercase prefix inconsistency.
**File:** au_print/print_util_wow.jsp
**Line(s):** 70-81

### P07-F19: Magic String - Hardcoded Default Value
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** The string "5" is used as a magic number default for access_level (lines 30, 47, 29 across the three files). This should be a named constant to clarify what access level 5 represents.
**Fix:** Define a constant: `private static final String DEFAULT_ACCESS_LEVEL = "5"; // Guest/public access` and use this constant instead of the magic string.
**File:** au_print/print_util_summary.jsp, au_print/print_util_wow.jsp, au_print/print_utilisation.jsp
**Line(s):** print_util_summary.jsp:30, print_util_wow.jsp:47, print_utilisation.jsp:29

### P07-F20: Incomplete HTML Structure
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** The JSP file includes `report_export_header.jsp` which presumably contains `<html>` and `<head>` tags, but line 61 shows `</head>` closing tag and line 101 shows `</html>` closing tag without corresponding opening tags visible in this file. Lines 153-190 contain orphaned closing tags (`</td>`, `</tr>`, `</tbody>`, `</table>`) suggesting incomplete table structure or missing context from included files.
**Fix:** Ensure the included header file properly opens HTML structure, or make the structure explicit in this file. Review the architecture of the include files to ensure proper HTML nesting. The orphaned closing tags at lines 153-190 suggest either missing opening tags or unnecessary closing tags that should be removed.
**File:** au_print/print_util_wow.jsp
**Line(s):** 153-190

### P07-F21: Incomplete HTML Structure
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Similar to F20 - orphaned closing tags at lines 153-157 (`</td>`, `</tr>`, `</tbody>`, `</table>`, `</form>`) without corresponding opening tags in the visible code. This indicates structural issues with the include file architecture.
**Fix:** Review and fix the HTML structure. Ensure all opening tags have corresponding closing tags and vice versa. Consider refactoring the include file pattern to make structure clearer.
**File:** au_print/print_utilisation.jsp
**Line(s):** 153-157

### P07-F22: JavaScript Extending Native Prototype
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Lines 208-213 extend the native Array.prototype by adding a max() method. Modifying native prototypes is considered bad practice as it can conflict with other libraries, breaks the principle of least surprise, and may interfere with future JavaScript language features.
**Fix:** Use a standalone utility function instead: `function arrayMax(arr) { if (arr.length == 0) return undefined; return Math.max.apply(null, arr); }`. Replace line 283 usage `trucks.max()` with `arrayMax(trucks)`.
**File:** au_print/print_util_wow.jsp
**Line(s):** 208-213

### P07-F23: String Comparison with == Instead of ===
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** JavaScript comparison at line 209 uses `==` instead of strict equality `===`. Line 211 also uses loose comparison. Modern JavaScript best practice recommends strict equality to avoid type coercion bugs.
**Fix:** Change `if (this.length == 0)` to `if (this.length === 0)` on line 209.
**File:** au_print/print_util_wow.jsp
**Line(s):** 209

### P07-F24: Unreachable Code Pattern
**Severity:** INFO
**Description:** The body onload attribute (line 62) calls `print('document');window.close();` which immediately closes the window after printing. This means the rendered content is only briefly visible before the window closes. While this may be intentional for print-only pages, it makes debugging difficult.
**Fix:** Consider adding a delay or conditional logic to allow viewing the page without auto-close during development: `onload="print('document'); setTimeout(function(){ window.close(); }, 1000);"` or make the auto-close conditional based on a URL parameter.
**File:** au_print/print_util_summary.jsp
**Line(s):** 62

### P07-F25: Unreachable Code Pattern
**Severity:** INFO
**Description:** Same as F24 - auto-close on print makes debugging difficult. This pattern appears in multiple print JSPs.
**Fix:** Same as F24 - add delay or make conditional.
**File:** au_print/print_util_wow.jsp
**Line(s):** 127

### P07-F26: Unreachable Code Pattern
**Severity:** INFO
**Description:** Same as F24 and F25.
**Fix:** Same as F24.
**File:** au_print/print_utilisation.jsp
**Line(s):** 85

### P07-F27: Missing Error Handling for Type Conversion
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Line 32 performs `Integer.parseInt(access_level)` without try-catch. If access_level contains non-numeric data (which could happen if session is corrupted or maliciously modified), this will throw NumberFormatException and crash the page. The parsed value is stored in unused variable `al`, but if the code were to use it, the lack of error handling would be a runtime risk.
**Fix:** Add try-catch around the parseInt: `try { int al = Integer.parseInt(access_level); } catch (NumberFormatException e) { // Log and use default }`. Alternatively, since `al` is unused (see F02), remove this line entirely.
**File:** au_print/print_util_summary.jsp
**Line(s):** 32

### P07-F28: Missing Error Handling for Type Conversion
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Same as F27 - parseInt without error handling.
**Fix:** Same as F27 - add error handling or remove if unused.
**File:** au_print/print_util_wow.jsp
**Line(s):** 49

### P07-F29: Missing Error Handling for Type Conversion
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Line 31 performs `Integer.parseInt(access_level)` without try-catch. Unlike F27 and F28, this variable `al` is actually used at line 62 in a conditional check, making the lack of error handling a real risk. If access_level is corrupted, the page will crash.
**Fix:** Add try-catch: `int al; try { al = Integer.parseInt(access_level); } catch (NumberFormatException e) { al = 5; // default to public access }`
**File:** au_print/print_utilisation.jsp
**Line(s):** 31

---

## Summary
**Total Findings:** 29
- **CRITICAL:** 0
- **HIGH:** 3 (F07, F08, F09 - NPE risk from missing null checks)
- **MEDIUM:** 11 (F01, F04, F05, F06, F15, F16, F17, F20, F21, F22, F27, F28, F29)
- **LOW:** 11 (F02, F03, F10, F11, F12, F13, F14, F18, F19, F23)
- **INFO:** 3 (F24, F25, F26)

**Key Recommendations:**
1. **Immediate action required:** Fix null pointer exception vulnerabilities (F07-F09) in all three files before deploying to production.
2. **High priority:** Add generic types to ArrayList declarations to eliminate unchecked warnings and improve type safety (F04-F06).
3. **Medium priority:** Remove commented code (F01), replace deprecated HTML attributes with CSS (F15-F17), fix incomplete HTML structure (F20-F21), and add error handling for parseInt (F27-F29).
4. **Refactoring opportunity:** Extract common access control logic into shared utility to reduce code duplication (F10).

## Findings

### P08-F01: Potential NullPointerException on request parameters
**Severity:** HIGH
**Description:** In header_vor_status.jsp, lines 3-4 call `.toString()` directly on `request.getParameter()` results without null checking. If the "mnm" or "sub" parameters are not present in the request, this will throw a NullPointerException and crash the page rendering.
**Fix:** Add null checks before calling .toString(), or use default values. Example: `request.getParameter("mnm") != null ? request.getParameter("mnm") : ""`
**File:** dashboard/jsp/header_vor_status.jsp
**Line(s):** 3-4

### P08-F02: Undefined variables referenced in JSP fragment
**Severity:** HIGH
**Description:** The JSP fragment references variables `form_cd` (line 5) and `permision` (lines 65, 68, 72) that are not defined within this file. This creates tight coupling and makes the fragment dependent on specific inclusion contexts. The code will fail if included in contexts where these variables are not defined.
**Fix:** Either define these variables within this fragment with default values, or convert this to a JSP tag file with proper parameters, or document the required context variables at the top of the file.
**File:** dashboard/jsp/header_vor_status.jsp
**Line(s):** 5, 65, 68, 72

### P08-F03: Commented-out session storage code
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Multiple blocks of commented-out code related to sessionStorage functionality exist in summary.jsp (lines 281-286, 410-412). This clutters the codebase and creates ambiguity about whether this functionality should be present or has been intentionally removed.
**Fix:** Either remove the commented code entirely if the functionality is no longer needed, or reinstate it if it should be active. Consider using feature flags if this is experimental functionality.
**File:** dashboard/jsp/summary.jsp
**Line(s):** 281-286, 410-412

### P08-F04: Commented-out HTML option elements
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** HTML comments contain option elements for "All Customers", "All Sites", and "All Departments" (lines 27, 35, 43). These appear to be duplicate or alternative implementations that should either be removed or reinstated.
**Fix:** Remove the commented HTML if the functionality is implemented elsewhere in JavaScript (which it appears to be in lines 295-297). Keeping commented markup creates confusion about the intended UI.
**File:** dashboard/jsp/summary.jsp
**Line(s):** 27, 35, 43

### P08-F05: Large block of commented-out battery chart code
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Lines 796-838 contain a 43-line block of commented-out Highcharts configuration for a battery monitoring chart, along with related commented AJAX calls (lines 919-923). This represents significant dead code that clutters the file.
**Fix:** If battery monitoring functionality is planned for future implementation, move this code to a separate feature branch or documentation file. Otherwise, delete it entirely. Commented code should not be used as version control.
**File:** dashboard/jsp/summary.jsp
**Line(s):** 796-838, 919-923

### P08-F06: Commented-out card click handler
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Lines 512-514 contain commented-out event handler code for a battery card. This is related to the commented battery chart functionality and should be managed consistently.
**Fix:** Remove this commented code along with the battery chart code (F05), or implement the complete battery monitoring feature properly.
**File:** dashboard/jsp/summary.jsp
**Line(s):** 512-514

### P08-F07: Commented-out realtime interval code
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Line 405 contains a commented-out setInterval call that would refresh the dashboard every 5 minutes (300000ms). It's unclear whether auto-refresh is intentionally disabled or if this is temporary.
**Fix:** Either remove the comment and enable auto-refresh with a configuration option, or delete the line entirely if auto-refresh is not desired. Document the decision.
**File:** dashboard/jsp/summary.jsp
**Line(s):** 405

### P08-F08: Commented-out legacy session variable code
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Lines 8-9 contain commented-out code for extracting user and dealer information from session. This appears to be legacy code that has been replaced but not cleaned up.
**Fix:** Remove these commented lines entirely. The session variable extraction pattern is already established for other variables (lines 10-17), so this commented code serves no purpose.
**File:** dashboard/jsp/summary.jsp
**Line(s):** 8-9

### P08-F09: Typo in variable name - "data.longth"
**Severity:** HIGH
**Description:** Line 326 contains a typo: `data.longth` should be `data.length`. This is a logic error that will cause the department selection to fail when there is exactly one department, as the condition will evaluate to undefined instead of 1.
**Fix:** Change `dept_cd = data.longth == 1? data[0].id : dept_cd` to `dept_cd = data.length == 1? data[0].id : dept_cd`
**File:** dashboard/jsp/summary.jsp
**Line(s):** 326

### P08-F10: Misspelling of "permission" as "permision"
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** The variable name `permision` (missing the second 's') is used consistently throughout header_vor_status.jsp. While consistent within this file, this misspelling violates standard English spelling and may indicate a broader naming inconsistency in the codebase.
**Fix:** Refactor the variable name to `permission` throughout the codebase. This will require coordinated changes in the Java backend that provides this variable to the JSP context.
**File:** dashboard/jsp/header_vor_status.jsp
**Line(s):** 65, 68, 72

### P08-F11: Typo in user-facing text - "accurary"
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Line 80 contains a user-facing message with the typo "accurary" which should be "accuracy": "The accurary of reports can vary marginally due to a number of circumtances."
**Fix:** Correct the spelling to "accuracy". Note: The same line also contains "circumtances" which should be "circumstances".
**File:** dashboard/jsp/header_vor_status.jsp
**Line(s):** 80

### P08-F12: Hardcoded customer-specific business logic
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** The code contains hardcoded customer code `BMW_cd = 20` (line 289) with multiple conditional branches throughout the code (lines 507-511, 935-947, 949-963). This creates tight coupling between the dashboard code and a specific customer configuration.
**Fix:** Replace hardcoded customer checks with a configuration-based approach or customer capability flags. Move customer-specific logic to a configuration file or database table that maps customer codes to feature sets.
**File:** dashboard/jsp/summary.jsp
**Line(s):** 289, 507-511, 935-947, 949-963

### P08-F13: Violation of separation of concerns - embedded JavaScript
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** The JSP file contains 695 lines of embedded JavaScript code (lines 270-965), mixing presentation logic with client-side behavior. This makes the code difficult to maintain, test, and reuse. JavaScript cannot be easily minified, cached separately, or unit tested.
**Fix:** Extract JavaScript code to separate .js files. Create modular JavaScript files such as dashboard-init.js, dashboard-charts.js, and dashboard-events.js. Pass server-side variables to JavaScript through data attributes or a single configuration object.
**File:** dashboard/jsp/summary.jsp
**Line(s):** 270-965

### P08-F14: Inconsistent quote style in JavaScript
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** The JavaScript code uses inconsistent quote styles, mixing single quotes (') and double quotes (") for string literals throughout the file. For example, single quotes in line 276 (`var cust_cd = '<%=cust_cd%>';`) and attribute selectors use mixed styles.
**Fix:** Standardize on a single quote style throughout the JavaScript code. Modern JavaScript convention typically uses single quotes for string literals unless the string contains single quotes.
**File:** dashboard/jsp/summary.jsp
**Line(s):** 270-965 (throughout JavaScript section)

### P08-F15: Magic numbers in date calculations
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Date calculations use magic numbers without explanation: 7 for week calculations (line 426), 28 for 4-week calculations (line 427), 300000 for interval timing (line 405). These reduce code readability.
**Fix:** Define named constants at the top of the script: `const DAYS_IN_WEEK = 7`, `const DAYS_IN_4_WEEKS = 28`, `const REFRESH_INTERVAL_MS = 300000`. Use these constants in calculations to improve readability.
**File:** dashboard/jsp/summary.jsp
**Line(s):** 405, 426, 427, 884, 885

### P08-F16: Incomplete form action URL
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** The form action in header_vor_status.jsp (line 2) ends with "part=" with no value: `action="../Servlet/TableServlet?part="`. This suggests the action is incomplete or relies on JavaScript to complete the URL before submission.
**Fix:** Either provide a complete default part value in the action, or document that JavaScript is required to set this value. If JavaScript modifies the action, add a comment explaining this dependency.
**File:** dashboard/jsp/header_vor_status.jsp
**Line(s):** 2

---

## Summary
**Total Findings:** 16
- **Critical:** 0
- **High:** 3
- **Medium:** 9
- **Low:** 4
- **Info:** 0

**Primary Issues:**
1. Null safety concerns with request parameter handling
2. Excessive commented-out code (7 instances across 50+ lines)
3. Undefined variable dependencies creating tight coupling
4. Customer-specific hardcoded logic violating separation of concerns
5. Large embedded JavaScript block (695 lines) violating architectural best practices
6. Logic error (typo in property name "longth" vs "length")

## Findings

### P09-F01: Commented-out code blocks present in multiple files
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Multiple blocks of commented-out code are present across files, including session attribute retrieval code. In email_dyn_unit_report_exc.jsp lines 26-29 contain commented-out session attribute retrievals, and line 45 contains a commented "end of access level control" that doesn't align with the code structure. This clutters the codebase and creates confusion about intended functionality.
**Fix:** Remove all commented-out code blocks. If session-based access control is needed in the future, reimplement it fresh from version control history rather than maintaining commented code.
**File:** dyn_report/email_dyn_unit_report_exc.jsp
**Line(s):** 26-29, 45

### P09-F02: Raw type usage without generics throughout all files
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** All files use raw ArrayList types without generics (e.g., "ArrayList vrpt_veh_cd" instead of "ArrayList<String> vrpt_veh_cd"). This generates unchecked warnings at compile time and removes type safety, allowing runtime ClassCastException errors that could be caught at compile time.
**Fix:** Add generic type parameters to all ArrayList declarations. Example: change "ArrayList vrpt_veh_cd = filter.getVrpt_veh_cd();" to "ArrayList<String> vrpt_veh_cd = filter.getVrpt_veh_cd();" or use the appropriate type.
**File:** All files (email_driver_league.jsp, email_dyn_driver_report.jsp, email_dyn_seen_report.jsp, email_dyn_unit_report.jsp, email_dyn_unit_report_exc.jsp, email_vor_status_report.jsp, excel_driver_league.jsp, excel_dyn_driver_report.jsp, excel_dyn_seen_report.jsp, excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp)
**Line(s):** 59 (email_driver_league.jsp), 45-64 (email_dyn_driver_report.jsp), 55-64 (email_dyn_seen_report.jsp), 51-70 (email_dyn_unit_report.jsp), 54-91 (email_dyn_unit_report_exc.jsp), 48-53 (email_vor_status_report.jsp), 72-77 (excel_driver_league.jsp), 62-79 (excel_dyn_driver_report.jsp), 81-92 (excel_dyn_seen_report.jsp), 177-194 (excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp)

### P09-F03: Unused request parameters consistently declared but never used
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Multiple request parameters are extracted and assigned to local variables but never used in the code: "undo_list", "sort_by", "sort_asc", and "message". This occurs consistently across all files. These unused variables waste processing time and create confusion about the intended functionality.
**Fix:** Remove unused parameter extractions. If these parameters are intended for future functionality, remove them until actually needed. If they're required for form submission compatibility, add a comment explaining why they're extracted but not used.
**File:** All files
**Line(s):** 28-30, 38-39 (email_driver_league.jsp), 15-17, 20 (email_dyn_driver_report.jsp), 15-17, 27 (email_dyn_seen_report.jsp), 15-17, 21 (email_dyn_unit_report.jsp), 19-21, 24 (email_dyn_unit_report_exc.jsp), 15-17, 21 (email_vor_status_report.jsp), 35-37, 45-46 (excel_driver_league.jsp), 22-24, 29 (excel_dyn_driver_report.jsp), 33-35, 46 (excel_dyn_seen_report.jsp), 33-35, 39 (excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp)

### P09-F04: Hardcoded access level values with inconsistent patterns
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Access level control uses hardcoded string values "1" and "5" without named constants or enums. The pattern is inconsistent: some files (email_dyn_driver_report.jsp, email_dyn_seen_report.jsp, email_dyn_unit_report.jsp, email_vor_status_report.jsp) set hardcoded access_level="1" directly, while others retrieve from session. The default fallback of "5" when empty is cryptic. Magic numbers reduce maintainability and make security implications unclear.
**Fix:** Define access level constants (e.g., public static final int ACCESS_LEVEL_ADMIN = 1, ACCESS_LEVEL_DEFAULT = 5) in a central configuration class. Replace all hardcoded values with these named constants. Document the meaning of each access level value.
**File:** email_dyn_driver_report.jsp, email_dyn_seen_report.jsp, email_dyn_unit_report.jsp, email_dyn_unit_report_exc.jsp, email_vor_status_report.jsp, excel_dyn_driver_report.jsp, excel_dyn_seen_report.jsp, excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 22-28 (email_dyn_driver_report.jsp), 28-35 (email_dyn_seen_report.jsp), 22-30 (email_dyn_unit_report.jsp), 30-37 (email_dyn_unit_report_exc.jsp), 22-30 (email_vor_status_report.jsp), 33-41 (excel_dyn_driver_report.jsp), 50-58 (excel_dyn_seen_report.jsp), 43-51 (excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp)

### P09-F05: Duplicate parameter extraction logic across all files
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** The pattern of extracting request parameters using the ternary operator (e.g., "request.getParameter('user_cd')==null?'':request.getParameter('user_cd')") is duplicated across all 10 files with only minor variations. This violates the DRY principle and makes maintenance difficult - any change to parameter handling must be repeated in 10 places.
**Fix:** Extract parameter retrieval into a utility method or use a request wrapper that provides safe parameter access with default values. Example: "String user_cd = RequestUtil.getParameter(request, 'user_cd', '');" This centralizes the null-checking logic.
**File:** All files
**Line(s):** 14-35 (email_driver_league.jsp), 8-18 (email_dyn_driver_report.jsp), 8-18 (email_dyn_seen_report.jsp), 8-19 (email_dyn_unit_report.jsp), 12-22 (email_dyn_unit_report_exc.jsp), 8-18 (email_vor_status_report.jsp), 21-42 (excel_driver_league.jsp), 14-24 (excel_dyn_driver_report.jsp), 26-36 (excel_dyn_seen_report.jsp), 26-36 (excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp)

### P09-F06: Inconsistent spacing and formatting in conditional statements
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Inconsistent spacing around operators and inconsistent use of braces in conditional statements. For example, email_dyn_driver_report.jsp line 28 has "if(access_level.equalsIgnoreCase(''))" without space after 'if', while inline conditionals use different spacing patterns. Email_driver_league.jsp uses mixed formatting for null checks (lines 14-35 with inconsistent spacing).
**Fix:** Apply consistent formatting throughout: use space after control flow keywords (if, for, while), consistent spacing around operators, and follow standard Java/JSP formatting conventions. Consider using a code formatter tool.
**File:** All files
**Line(s):** Throughout all files (particularly evident in conditional statements and loops)

### P09-F07: Inconsistent ArrayList concatenation pattern
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** When building strings from ArrayList elements, some files use the pattern "String mod_cd = ''+vrpt_veh_typ_cd.get(i)" (empty string concatenation) while this serves to convert Object to String, it's not the clearest approach. This pattern appears consistently in nested loops across multiple files.
**Fix:** Use explicit type casting or String.valueOf() for clarity. Replace "String mod_cd = ''+vrpt_veh_typ_cd.get(i)" with "String mod_cd = String.valueOf(vrpt_veh_typ_cd.get(i))" or proper casting if using generics.
**File:** email_dyn_driver_report.jsp, email_dyn_unit_report.jsp, email_dyn_unit_report_exc.jsp, excel_dyn_driver_report.jsp, excel_dyn_seen_report.jsp, excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 100-101, 151-175 (email_dyn_driver_report.jsp), 138-139, 189-211 (email_dyn_unit_report.jsp), 167-168, 202-222 (email_dyn_unit_report_exc.jsp), 123-124, 178-201 (excel_dyn_driver_report.jsp), 161-176 (excel_dyn_seen_report.jsp), 241-242, 298-321 (excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp)

### P09-F08: Unused variable 'al' after parsing access_level
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** In multiple files, the variable 'al' is created by parsing access_level to an integer but is never subsequently used. This appears in email_dyn_driver_report.jsp line 28, email_dyn_seen_report.jsp line 36, email_dyn_unit_report.jsp line 30, email_dyn_unit_report_exc.jsp line 38, email_vor_status_report.jsp line 30, excel_dyn_driver_report.jsp line 42, excel_dyn_seen_report.jsp line 59, and excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp line 52.
**Fix:** Remove the 'al' variable declaration if it's not needed. If integer access level checking is intended for future use, remove it until actually needed.
**File:** email_dyn_driver_report.jsp, email_dyn_seen_report.jsp, email_dyn_unit_report.jsp, email_dyn_unit_report_exc.jsp, email_vor_status_report.jsp, excel_dyn_driver_report.jsp, excel_dyn_seen_report.jsp, excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 28, 36, 30, 38, 30, 42, 59, 52

### P09-F09: Duplicate field hiding logic across six files
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** The field hiding logic using fld_hide array and do_list parameter processing (lines 64-89 in email_dyn_driver_report.jsp) is duplicated almost identically in six files. This includes array initialization, StringTokenizer parsing, and field matching. Code duplication makes maintenance error-prone.
**Fix:** Extract field hiding logic into a utility method or helper class. Example: "FieldHideUtil.createHideArray(vrpt_field_cd, do_list)" that returns the boolean array. This centralizes the logic and makes it testable.
**File:** email_dyn_driver_report.jsp, email_dyn_seen_report.jsp, email_dyn_unit_report.jsp, email_dyn_unit_report_exc.jsp, excel_dyn_driver_report.jsp, excel_dyn_seen_report.jsp, excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 64-89 (email_dyn_driver_report.jsp), 67-92 (email_dyn_seen_report.jsp), 70-96 (email_dyn_unit_report.jsp), 92-120 (email_dyn_unit_report_exc.jsp), 81-111 (excel_dyn_driver_report.jsp), 92-122 (excel_dyn_seen_report.jsp), 196-226 (excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp)

### P09-F10: Inconsistent hardcoded division code "1"
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** All files set division code to hardcoded "1" via filter.setSet_div_cd("1"). This magic number appears without explanation and is set uniformly across all reports, suggesting it may not be necessary or should be configurable.
**Fix:** Define a named constant for division code (e.g., DEFAULT_DIVISION_CD = "1") or make it configurable via application properties. Add documentation explaining what division "1" represents and why it's hardcoded.
**File:** All files
**Line(s):** 48 (email_driver_league.jsp), 35 (email_dyn_driver_report.jsp), 43 (email_dyn_seen_report.jsp), 37 (email_dyn_unit_report.jsp), 45 (email_dyn_unit_report_exc.jsp), 37 (email_vor_status_report.jsp), 53 (excel_driver_league.jsp), 52 (excel_dyn_driver_report.jsp), 69 (excel_dyn_seen_report.jsp), 62 (excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp)

### P09-F11: Inconsistent date format handling across email vs excel files
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Date format handling is inconsistent: email_dyn_seen_report.jsp gets dealerDateFormat from request parameter (lines 20-25) and sets it on filter (line 52), while excel_dyn_seen_report.jsp gets it from session attribute (lines 37-42) and sets it on filter (line 78). This inconsistency could lead to format mismatches between email and Excel exports of the same report.
**Fix:** Standardize date format retrieval across all reports. Use a consistent source (prefer session attribute for consistency) and create a utility method for date format retrieval with proper fallback logic.
**File:** email_dyn_seen_report.jsp, excel_dyn_seen_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 20-25, 52 (email_dyn_seen_report.jsp), 37-42, 78 (excel_dyn_seen_report.jsp)

### P09-F12: Missing null check on DriverLeagueBean list access
**Severity:** HIGH
**Description:** In email_driver_league.jsp line 79 and excel_driver_league.jsp lines 79, 124, 151, the code directly accesses driverLeagueBeans.get(0).getTruckType() without checking if the list is empty. This will throw IndexOutOfBoundsException if no data is returned, causing the report to fail.
**Fix:** Add null and empty checks before accessing the first element. Example: "if (driverLeagueBeans != null && !driverLeagueBeans.isEmpty()) { /* display truck type */ } else { /* show 'No Data' or default */ }"
**File:** email_driver_league.jsp, excel_driver_league.jsp
**Line(s):** 79 (email_driver_league.jsp), 79, 124, 151 (excel_driver_league.jsp)

### P09-F13: Unused import in excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Line 2 imports "java.text.*" but this package is not used anywhere in the file. The DateFormat or SimpleDateFormat classes are not referenced.
**Fix:** Remove the unused import statement: "<%@ page import='java.text.*'%>"
**File:** excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 2

### P09-F14: Unused import in excel_dyn_seen_report.jsp
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Line 2 imports "java.text.*" but this package is not used anywhere in the file.
**Fix:** Remove the unused import statement: "<%@ page import='java.text.*'%>"
**File:** excel_dyn_seen_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 2

### P09-F15: Inline CSS styling mixed throughout JSP files
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Extensive inline CSS styles are embedded directly in HTML tags throughout all files (e.g., email_driver_league.jsp lines 68-69, email_dyn_driver_report.jsp line 179, etc.). This violates separation of concerns, makes styling maintenance difficult, and increases page size.
**Fix:** Move inline styles to CSS classes in the included CSS files (alternate_rows.css) or create report-specific CSS files. Replace inline style attributes with class references.
**File:** All files
**Line(s):** Throughout all files (particularly in table header and cell definitions)

### P09-F16: Inconsistent dealer-specific logic placement
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Dealer-specific logic appears in email_dyn_unit_report.jsp (lines 48-49, 220-237) and excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp (lines 73-74, 330-347) where "isDealerCi" flag changes rendering behavior. This business logic is embedded in presentation layer JSP files, making it difficult to test and maintain. The hardcoded dealer ID "24" is a magic number.
**Fix:** Move dealer-specific rendering logic to a helper class or use a template method pattern. Define dealer ID constants in a central configuration. Consider using separate view templates for different dealers rather than conditional logic.
**File:** email_dyn_unit_report.jsp, excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 48-49, 220-237 (email_dyn_unit_report.jsp), 73-74, 330-347 (excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp)

### P09-F17: Hardcoded URL in email_dyn_unit_report_exc.jsp
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Lines 127 and 136 contain hardcoded URLs to "http://fleetfocus.lindemh.com.au/fms/images/linde/" for logo images. Hardcoded URLs will fail if the domain changes, the application is deployed to a different environment, or HTTPS is required.
**Fix:** Replace hardcoded URLs with configuration properties or use context-relative paths with LindeConfig.externalURL (as used in other files). Example: "<%=LindeConfig.externalURL %>/images/linde/linde_long_logo.gif"
**File:** email_dyn_unit_report_exc.jsp
**Line(s):** 127, 136

### P09-F18: Empty table row serves no purpose
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** In email_vor_status_report.jsp lines 77-78, there is an empty table row "<tr></tr>" that serves no purpose and creates unnecessary whitespace.
**Fix:** Remove the empty table row element.
**File:** email_vor_status_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 77-78

### P09-F19: Inconsistent Excel filename generation
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Excel export files use inconsistent filename patterns: excel_driver_league.jsp line 4 uses variable "f_nme = 'Driver_League_Report.xls'" with PascalCase, excel_dyn_driver_report.jsp line 3 uses "detailed_report_by_driver.xls" with snake_case, excel_dyn_seen_report.jsp line 11 uses "seen_iris_pedestrian.xls", and excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp line 171 uses conditional logic. No consistent naming convention.
**Fix:** Establish and document a consistent filename convention for all Excel exports. Consider using the report name from the database with a standardized format utility method.
**File:** excel_driver_league.jsp, excel_dyn_driver_report.jsp, excel_dyn_seen_report.jsp, excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 4 (excel_driver_league.jsp), 3 (excel_dyn_driver_report.jsp), 11 (excel_dyn_seen_report.jsp), 171 (excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp)

### P09-F20: Potential resource leak in excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp
**Severity:** HIGH
**Description:** Lines 155-165 in excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp open FileInputStream and ServletOutputStream but only close them in the normal flow. If an exception occurs during the read/write loop, the streams will not be closed, causing resource leaks. The exception handling at line 166 catches and prints stack trace but doesn't close resources.
**Fix:** Use try-with-resources statement to ensure streams are properly closed: "try (FileInputStream fis = new FileInputStream(fileToDownload); ServletOutputStream output = response.getOutputStream()) { /* read/write */ } catch (Exception e) { /* handle */ }"
**File:** excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 155-168

### P09-F21: Exception handling only prints stack trace
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** In excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp line 167, exceptions are caught and only e.printStackTrace() is called. This provides no user feedback about the error and doesn't log to a proper logging system. Users will see a failed download with no explanation.
**Fix:** Log the exception using a proper logging framework (Log4j, SLF4J) and provide user feedback via error page or message. Example: "logger.error('Failed to generate Excel report', e); response.sendError(HttpServletResponse.SC_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, 'Report generation failed');"
**File:** excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 166-168

### P09-F22: Complex path manipulation using string operations
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Lines 135-140 in excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp use complex string manipulation to construct file paths, including counting forward slashes and using nthOccurrence method. This is fragile and platform-dependent (won't work on Windows with backslashes). The relative path "../../../../../../../excelrpt/" is particularly brittle.
**Fix:** Use Java Path API (java.nio.file.Paths) or ServletContext.getRealPath() to construct file system paths reliably. Store the Excel report directory in configuration rather than computing it from class location. Example: "String dir = getServletContext().getRealPath('/excelrpt/');"
**File:** excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 135-140

### P09-F23: Unused variable 'cnt' initialization and increment pattern
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Multiple files initialize a 'cnt' variable and increment it for alternating row colors (e.g., email_dyn_unit_report.jsp line 155), but the variable name is not descriptive and the pattern is inconsistent with files that use the loop index directly for the same purpose.
**Fix:** Use the existing loop index for row coloring instead of maintaining a separate counter. Example: replace "if(cnt%2==0)" with "if(j%2==0)" using the existing loop variable. This eliminates an unnecessary variable.
**File:** email_dyn_unit_report_exc.jsp, excel_dyn_driver_report.jsp, excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 155, 182, 223-226 (email_dyn_unit_report_exc.jsp), 140, 203-207 (excel_dyn_driver_report.jsp), 155, 258, 323-327 (excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp)

### P09-F24: Inconsistent use of getFleetNumHeader() method
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Some files use UtilBean.getFleetNumHeader() within i18n message keys (e.g., email_dyn_driver_report.jsp line 127), while others use it directly without i18n (e.g., email_dyn_unit_report_exc.jsp line 188). This creates inconsistent internationalization support across reports.
**Fix:** Standardize the usage pattern. Either always use the method result within i18n keys or always use it directly. Document the intended internationalization approach for fleet number headers.
**File:** email_dyn_driver_report.jsp, email_dyn_unit_report.jsp, email_dyn_unit_report_exc.jsp, excel_dyn_driver_report.jsp, excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 127 (email_dyn_driver_report.jsp), 109, 164 (email_dyn_unit_report.jsp), 188 (email_dyn_unit_report_exc.jsp), 155 (excel_dyn_driver_report.jsp), 273 (excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp)

### P09-F25: Non-standard scriptlet comment placement
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Several files have comments like "//end of access level control" or "//end of do_list." that appear at awkward positions or reference code blocks that have already ended. For example, email_driver_league.jsp line 45 says "//end of access level control" but there's no access level control code in that file.
**Fix:** Remove misleading comments. Ensure comments accurately describe the code they reference. If access level control code was removed, remove the associated comment as well.
**File:** email_driver_league.jsp, email_dyn_driver_report.jsp, email_dyn_seen_report.jsp, email_dyn_unit_report.jsp, email_dyn_unit_report_exc.jsp, email_vor_status_report.jsp, excel_driver_league.jsp, excel_dyn_driver_report.jsp, excel_dyn_seen_report.jsp, excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 45, 60 (email_driver_league.jsp), 33, 90 (email_dyn_driver_report.jsp), 41, 93 (email_dyn_seen_report.jsp), 35, 96 (email_dyn_unit_report.jsp), 43, 120 (email_dyn_unit_report_exc.jsp), 35 (email_vor_status_report.jsp), 50, 65 (excel_driver_league.jsp), 49, 112 (excel_dyn_driver_report.jsp), 66, 123 (excel_dyn_seen_report.jsp), 59, 227 (excel_dyn_unit_report.jsp)
