## Findings

### P23-F01: Deprecated JavaScript language attribute
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** All JSP files use deprecated `language="JavaScript"` or `language="javascript"` attribute on script tags. The HTML5 specification deprecates this attribute in favor of `type="text/javascript"` or omitting it entirely (JavaScript is the default).
**Fix:** Replace all `<script language="JavaScript">` and `<script language="javascript">` with `<script type="text/javascript">` or simply `<script>`.
**File:** All 10 files
**Line(s):** Multiple occurrences in each file (lines 9-17 typical range for external scripts, 14-15 for inline scripts)

### P23-F02: Commented-out code block in frm_mastercode_40slots.jsp (meta tag)
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Commented-out meta tag `<!-- <meta http-equiv="X-UA-Compatible" content="IE=EmulateIE7" /> -->` indicates old IE compatibility code. Commented code should be removed or reinstated.
**Fix:** Remove the commented-out line if IE7 compatibility is no longer needed, or reinstate if still required.
**File:** master/frm_mastercode_40slots.jsp
**Line(s):** 8

### P23-F03: Commented-out CSS in frm_mastercode_40slots.jsp
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Multiple CSS properties are commented out: `/*width:800px*/` (line 139), `/*width:300px;*/` and `/*height:500px;*/` (line 150), `/*width:70px;*/` (line 159). This creates uncertainty about intended styling.
**Fix:** Remove commented CSS if not needed, or reinstate and apply proper values.
**File:** master/frm_mastercode_40slots.jsp
**Line(s):** 139, 150, 159

### P23-F04: Commented-out JavaScript in frm_mastercode_40slots.jsp
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Two lines of JavaScript code commented out in loadInputs function: `//loadInputs();` and `//fetchSites($('#usr').val());` (lines 547-548). This indicates incomplete refactoring or debugging code left behind.
**Fix:** Remove commented lines if functionality is handled elsewhere, or document why they are preserved.
**File:** master/frm_mastercode_40slots.jsp
**Line(s):** 547-548

### P23-F05: Deprecated jQuery.browser usage
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Multiple files use `jQuery.browser.msie` which was deprecated in jQuery 1.9 and removed in jQuery 3.0. The code uses it for XML parsing: `dataType: (jQuery.browser.msie) ? 'text' : 'xml'`.
**Fix:** Replace with feature detection or use modern AJAX response handling. Example: `dataType: 'xml'` and handle parsing errors gracefully.
**File:** master/frm_mastercode_40slots.jsp
**Line(s):** 335, 368, 407, 449

### P23-F06: Deprecated escape() function usage
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Uses deprecated JavaScript `escape()` function for URL encoding. This function is deprecated and should be replaced with `encodeURIComponent()`.
**Fix:** Replace `escape($(this).val())` with `encodeURIComponent($(this).val())`.
**File:** master/frm_mastercode_40slots.jsp
**Line(s):** 559, 562, 610

### P23-F07: Empty CSS rule
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** CSS rule `a.fat { }` is completely empty and serves no purpose.
**Fix:** Remove the empty CSS rule.
**File:** master/frm_mastercode_40slots.jsp
**Line(s):** 200-202

### P23-F08: Commented-out AJAX code block in frm_mastercode_step2.jsp
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Large commented-out AJAX block (lines 20-28) suggests incomplete refactoring. The code includes the actual AJAX call and response handling that appears to have been replaced by later implementation.
**Fix:** Remove commented-out code block since the functionality is implemented later in the file.
**File:** master/frm_mastercode_step2.jsp
**Line(s):** 20-28

### P23-F09: Deprecated escape() in frm_mastercode_step2.jsp
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Uses deprecated `escape()` function for form serialization.
**Fix:** Replace with `encodeURIComponent()`.
**File:** master/frm_mastercode_step2.jsp
**Line(s):** 13, 16

### P23-F10: Dead code - unused chk_time function
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Function `chk_time()` is defined but never called in the page. It validates timezone format but no input field uses the `onBlur="chk_time();"` handler.
**Fix:** Remove the unused function or add the onBlur handler to the timezone input if validation is needed.
**File:** master/frm_new_branch.jsp
**Line(s):** 59-83

### P23-F11: Commented-out validation logic in frm_new_branch.jsp
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Timezone validation commented out in saveform (lines 26-30) and in form HTML (line 172). Also commented-out state input section (lines 182-190).
**Fix:** Remove commented-out code or reinstate if functionality is needed.
**File:** master/frm_new_branch.jsp
**Line(s):** 26-30, 172, 182-190

### P23-F12: Duplicate file - frm_new_branch_old.jsp
**Severity:** HIGH
**Description:** File `frm_new_branch_old.jsp` is nearly identical to `frm_new_branch.jsp` but uses op_code "branch_add" instead of "loc_add". The "_old" suffix indicates this is a legacy file that should be removed or consolidated.
**Fix:** Determine if frm_new_branch_old.jsp is still in use. If not, delete it. If both are needed, document the difference clearly and rename appropriately.
**File:** master/frm_new_branch_old.jsp
**Line(s):** All (entire file is duplicate)

### P23-F13: Dead code - unused chk_time in frm_new_branch_old.jsp
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Same as F10 - chk_time function defined but never called.
**Fix:** Remove the unused function.
**File:** master/frm_new_branch_old.jsp
**Line(s):** 50-74

### P23-F14: Commented-out division selector in frm_new_customer.jsp
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Large block of commented HTML for division selection (lines 204-212). The division is instead hardcoded to value "1" on line 213.
**Fix:** Remove commented-out code if division selection is no longer needed. If needed, reinstate the selector and remove the hardcoded value.
**File:** master/frm_new_customer.jsp
**Line(s):** 204-212

### P23-F15: Commented-out description field in frm_new_customer.jsp
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Description input field commented out (lines 234-241).
**Fix:** Remove commented-out code if description is no longer needed.
**File:** master/frm_new_customer.jsp
**Line(s):** 234-241

### P23-F16: Hardcoded division value
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Division hardcoded to "1" with no explanation: `usr_gp = "1";` on line 160. This overrides any database value and may cause unexpected behavior.
**Fix:** Remove hardcoded assignment or add comment explaining why division must always be 1. Consider making this configurable.
**File:** master/frm_new_customer.jsp
**Line(s):** 160

### P23-F17: Commented-out customer address TEXTAREA
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Address input commented out in favor of TEXTAREA (line 277).
**Fix:** Remove commented-out code.
**File:** master/frm_new_customer.jsp
**Line(s):** 277

### P23-F18: Code duplication - frm_new_department.jsp and frm_new_division.jsp
**Severity:** HIGH
**Description:** Files frm_new_department.jsp and frm_new_division.jsp are nearly identical. They differ only in op_code ("dep_add" vs "div_add") and form labels ("Department" vs "Division"). This is significant code duplication that should be consolidated.
**Fix:** Create a single parameterized form that handles both department and division entry, using a parameter to control the op_code and labels.
**File:** master/frm_new_department.jsp, master/frm_new_division.jsp
**Line(s):** All (entire files are duplicates with minor variations)

### P23-F19: Missing form_cd handling in frm_new_department_win.jsp
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** File frm_new_department_win.jsp doesn't retrieve or use form_cd parameter in the scriptlet (unlike its full-page counterpart), yet expects it to exist based on the pattern. The filter.setSet_form_cd(form_cd) call is missing.
**Fix:** Add form_cd parameter handling or document why it's intentionally omitted for the window version.
**File:** master/frm_new_department_win.jsp
**Line(s):** 54-66

### P23-F20: Missing page structure in frm_new_department_win.jsp
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** The "win" version lacks the HoldingTable/MainTable structure and Linde branding present in the full version. This creates inconsistent UI when comparing the two versions.
**Fix:** Document that this is intentional for popup windows, or add minimal structure for consistency.
**File:** master/frm_new_department_win.jsp
**Line(s):** 69-138

### P23-F21: Commented-out close button in frm_new_group.jsp
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Close button commented out: `<!-- <input type=button name="close_button" value="Close" onClick="closeform();"> -->` on line 202.
**Fix:** Remove commented-out button and the unused closeform() function if close functionality is not needed.
**File:** master/frm_new_group.jsp
**Line(s):** 202

### P23-F22: Large commented-out code block in frm_new_driver.jsp
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Function check_access_menu contains large commented-out block (lines 406-419) with customer-specific keypad options for customers 27 and 28. This indicates business logic that was disabled rather than properly refactored.
**Fix:** Remove the commented-out code and the entire check_access_menu function since it only contains commented code. If customer-specific logic is still needed, implement it properly with configuration rather than hardcoded customer IDs.
**File:** master/frm_new_driver.jsp
**Line(s):** 404-421

### P23-F23: External CDN dependency in production
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Uses external CDN for jQuery UI: `<script language="javascript" src="http://code.jquery.com/ui/1.8.21/jquery-ui.min.js"></script>`. This creates external dependency, uses HTTP (not HTTPS), and references a very old version (1.8.21 from 2012).
**Fix:** Host jQuery UI locally or upgrade to a modern version from a secure CDN (HTTPS).
**File:** master/frm_new_driver.jsp
**Line(s):** 14

### P23-F24: Global event handler pollution
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Global document.onkeypress handler (line 489) captures all keyboard input for card swipe detection. This can interfere with normal page functionality and is a leaky abstraction of the card reader implementation detail.
**Fix:** Scope the event handler to specific elements or use a more targeted approach. Consider using a library for card reader integration.
**File:** master/frm_new_driver.jsp
**Line(s):** 489

### P23-F25: Very long file - poor separation of concerns
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** File frm_new_driver.jsp is 955 lines long with complex JavaScript including AJAX, validation, card swipe detection, and dynamic form manipulation all mixed together. This violates single responsibility principle.
**Fix:** Split JavaScript into separate files (validation.js, card-reader.js, ajax-utils.js) and extract reusable components.
**File:** master/frm_new_driver.jsp
**Line(s):** All (entire file complexity)

### P23-F26: Style inconsistency - mixed quote styles
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Across all files, there's inconsistent use of single quotes vs double quotes in JavaScript and HTML attributes. For example, some onclick handlers use single quotes: `onclick='delete_void(this)'` while others use double quotes.
**Fix:** Adopt a consistent style guide and apply it across all files.
**File:** All 10 files
**Line(s):** Multiple occurrences throughout

### P23-F27: Inconsistent null/empty string checks
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Multiple validation patterns used inconsistently: `name==null||name==""||name==" "||name=="  "` (checks null, empty, single space, double space) vs simpler checks. This pattern appears in multiple files suggesting copy-paste without standardization.
**Fix:** Create a common validation utility function like `isEmpty(value)` and use it consistently.
**File:** master/frm_new_branch.jsp (line 20), master/frm_new_branch_old.jsp (line 20), master/frm_new_customer.jsp (line 23, 28, 33, 38, 43, 48), master/frm_new_department.jsp (line 20), master/frm_new_department_win.jsp (line 20), master/frm_new_division.jsp (line 20), master/frm_new_driver.jsp (line 28, 33, 38), master/frm_new_group.jsp (line 20)
**Line(s):** Multiple occurrences

### P23-F28: Inline event handlers throughout
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** All files use inline event handlers (onclick, onchange, onload, onblur) embedded in HTML. This violates separation of concerns and Content Security Policy best practices, making the code harder to maintain and potentially less secure.
**Fix:** Move all event handlers to external JavaScript using addEventListener or jQuery event binding.
**File:** All 10 files
**Line(s):** Multiple occurrences throughout

### P23-F29: Hardcoded access level default
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Default access level "5" hardcoded in multiple files when session value is empty. The meaning of "5" is not documented inline.
**Fix:** Define access level constants (e.g., ACCESS_LEVEL_NOT_APPLICABLE = "5") and use named constants instead of magic numbers.
**File:** master/frm_mastercode_40slots.jsp (line 47), master/frm_new_driver.jsp (line 600), master/frm_new_group.jsp (line 84)
**Line(s):** 47, 600, 84 respectively

### P23-F30: Inconsistent AJAX error handling
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Some AJAX calls have error handlers (`error:function(err){alert(err)}`), while others have none. Error handling approach is inconsistent - some show alerts, others silently fail.
**Fix:** Implement consistent error handling strategy across all AJAX calls with proper user feedback and logging.
**File:** master/frm_mastercode_40slots.jsp (lines 346, 379, 418, 460)
**Line(s):** Multiple AJAX calls across multiple files

---

## Summary
**Total Findings:** 30
- **CRITICAL:** 0
- **HIGH:** 2 (duplicate files/code)
- **MEDIUM:** 20 (commented code, deprecated APIs, poor separation of concerns)
- **LOW:** 7 (style issues, unused code, documentation)
- **INFO:** 1 (style consistency)

The codebase shows signs of legacy development with deprecated JavaScript features, commented-out code indicating incomplete refactoring, and significant code duplication. Key areas for improvement include:
1. Remove all commented-out code
2. Consolidate duplicate files and forms
3. Upgrade deprecated jQuery APIs
4. Improve separation of concerns (extract JavaScript to separate files)
5. Implement consistent validation and error handling patterns

- Lines 78-149: Initialization, parameter retrieval, filter setup including form_cd, vehicle data loading, default value assignments including "Not Available" fallback for veh_nm

- saveform() (13-54): Validates serial number, mast configuration, model, location, submits form
- closeform() (56-63): Redirects parent to vehicle list, closes popup
- set() (65-70): Sets vehicle type and location dropdowns

**Form action:** ../servlet/Frm_saveuser
**Form parameters:** vt, s_no, vehnm, loc, veh_cd, veh_typ_cd, op_code=vehicle_add_short, loc_cd, st_dt, end_dt, search_crit, gmtp_id, form_cd

## Findings

### P24-F01: Deprecated JavaScript Language Attribute
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** All files use the deprecated `language="javascript"` attribute in script tags. Modern HTML5 standards recommend omitting the language attribute entirely or using `type="text/javascript"` (though type is also optional in HTML5).
**Fix:** Remove the `language="javascript"` attribute from all `<script>` tags. In HTML5, script tags default to JavaScript, so no attribute is needed.
**File:** All 10 files
**Line(s):**
- frm_new_location.jsp: 7-11
- frm_new_question.jsp: 7-11
- frm_new_user.jsp: 8-13
- frm_new_vehicle.jsp: 7-11
- frm_new_vehicle1.jsp: 7-11
- frm_new_vehicle_short.jsp: 7-11
- frm_new_vehicle_type.jsp: 9-13
- frm_new_vehicle_type_pop.jsp: 9-13
- frm_override_codes.jsp: 9-13
- frm_repl_vehiclemod.jsp: 7-11

### P24-F02: Commented-Out HTML Input Field

**Description:** Commented-out HTML code for timezone text input field. This creates ambiguity about whether the feature should exist and clutters the codebase.
**Fix:** Either remove the commented code entirely if the select dropdown is the permanent solution, or restore it with proper conditional logic if both input methods are needed.
**File:** master/frm_new_location.jsp

### P24-F12: Commented-Out TEXTAREA Alternative

### P24-F13: Inconsistent Empty String Validation Pattern
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Most files validate empty strings using the verbose pattern `value==null||value==""||value==" "||value=="  "` which checks for null, empty, single space, and double space. This is inconsistent and inefficient. Modern JavaScript should use trimmed comparison or simple truthy checks.
**Fix:** Replace with a consistent pattern like `!value || !value.trim()` or create a reusable validation function. This would improve readability and reduce code duplication.
**File:** All files with JavaScript validation
**Line(s):** Multiple validation blocks in all files (e.g., frm_new_location.jsp: 19-28, frm_new_question.jsp: 19-23, frm_new_vehicle.jsp: 24-53, etc.)

### P24-F14: Duplicate Variable Declaration
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variable `hire_no` is declared twice in the saveform() function - once at line 16 and again at line 17. This is redundant and may cause confusion.
**Fix:** Remove the duplicate declaration at line 17. Keep only line 16: `var hire_no=document.forms[0].h_no.value;`
**File:** master/frm_new_vehicle.jsp
**Line(s):** 16-17

### P24-F15: Unreachable Alert Statement
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** In saveform() function, the else block at lines 106-110 contains an alert that will never execute. When confirm returns true and a=true, document.forms[0].submit() is called, then there's an else with alert(msg) that can never run because the condition path requires a=true.
**Fix:** Remove the else block (lines 106-110) as it's dead code. The alert(msg) at line 113 already handles the case when flg=false.
**File:** master/frm_new_user.jsp
**Line(s):** 106-110

### P24-F16: Mixed AJAX Implementation Patterns
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** AJAX code uses inconsistent patterns across files. Some files include IE6/IE5 fallback with ActiveXObject("Microsoft.XMLHTTP") while modern browsers don't need this. The GetXmlHttpObject() factory function is duplicated across multiple files with identical implementation.
**Fix:** Extract AJAX functionality to a shared utility file. Remove legacy IE5/IE6 ActiveXObject support unless specifically required. Use modern fetch API or a consistent XMLHttpRequest pattern.
**File:** frm_new_user.jsp, frm_override_codes.jsp
**Line(s):** frm_new_user.jsp: 398-419, frm_override_codes.jsp: 249-270

### P24-F17: Hardcoded Form Action URLs
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** All forms use hardcoded action="../servlet/Frm_saveuser" which creates tight coupling and makes the servlet endpoint difficult to change or configure.
**Fix:** Extract servlet URLs to a JavaScript configuration object or JSP variable that can be defined in one place. Example: `var ENDPOINTS = { saveUser: '../servlet/Frm_saveuser' };`
**File:** All 10 files
**Line(s):** Various (frm_new_location.jsp: 100, frm_new_question.jsp: 107, frm_new_user.jsp: 768, etc.)

### P24-F18: Inconsistent Window Close Behavior
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Some closeform() functions simply call window.close(), while others redirect the opener window first (window.opener.location.href=url) then close. The popup version (frm_new_vehicle_type_pop.jsp) calls window.opener.refresh() which may not exist.
**Fix:** Standardize the close behavior across all forms. Either always refresh the opener with a consistent redirect pattern, or use a postMessage API to notify the parent window. The window.opener.refresh() call should be changed to window.opener.location.reload() or removed.
**File:** Multiple files
**Line(s):** frm_new_location.jsp: 45, frm_new_question.jsp: 41-45, frm_new_user.jsp: 181-185, frm_new_vehicle_type_pop.jsp: 40-41

### P24-F19: Inconsistent Comment Typo in User Messages
**Severity:** INFO
**Description:** Validation message contains typo "swipped" instead of "swiped" in multiple locations. Also "coma" instead of "comma".
**Fix:** Correct spelling: "swipped" → "swiped" and "coma" → "comma"
**File:** frm_new_user.jsp, frm_new_question.jsp
**Line(s):** frm_new_user.jsp: 88, 618, 645; frm_new_question.jsp: 69

### P24-F20: Potential XSS Vulnerability - Unescaped Output
**Severity:** HIGH
**Description:** JSP expressions like `<%=message %>`, `<%=serial_no %>`, `<%=hire_no %>` and many others directly output user-controlled or database values without HTML encoding. This creates cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerabilities where malicious input could execute JavaScript in users' browsers.
**Fix:** Use JSTL `<c:out>` tags or explicitly escape all dynamic output: `<c:out value="${message}" escapeXml="true"/>` or use a dedicated escaping function for all `<%= %>` expressions.
**File:** All 10 files
**Line(s):** Multiple throughout all files (e.g., frm_new_location.jsp: 147, frm_new_user.jsp: 1012, frm_new_vehicle.jsp: 266, frm_repl_vehiclemod.jsp: 92-121, etc.)

### P24-F21: Potential XSS in JavaScript Context
**Severity:** HIGH
**Description:** JSP expressions are embedded directly in JavaScript strings and onclick handlers without proper JavaScript escaping. Examples include onclick="set_editvalues('<%=loc_cd.get(i)%>','<%=loc_nm.get(i)%>',..." where location names containing quotes or special characters could break out of the string context and execute arbitrary JavaScript.
**Fix:** Use proper JavaScript escaping for all JSP values inserted into JavaScript context. Create a JSP function to escape single quotes, double quotes, backslashes, and newlines, or use a JSON encoding library.
**File:** All files with inline JavaScript
**Line(s):** Multiple (e.g., frm_new_location.jsp: 169, frm_new_question.jsp: 159, frm_new_user.jsp: 767, frm_new_vehicle_type.jsp: 126, 162, etc.)

### P24-F22: Missing NULL Check Before String Operations
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Several scriptlet blocks perform string operations like `.equalsIgnoreCase("")` without first checking if the variable is null. This can cause NullPointerException at runtime.
**Fix:** Add null checks before string operations or use safer patterns like: `if(veh_last_serv == null || veh_last_serv.equalsIgnoreCase(""))`. Alternatively, initialize variables with non-null defaults.
**File:** frm_new_vehicle.jsp, frm_new_vehicle_short.jsp
**Line(s):** frm_new_vehicle.jsp: 137, 141, 145; frm_new_vehicle_short.jsp: 122, 126, 130

### P24-F23: Inconsistent Boolean Comparison
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** JavaScript uses negation operator incorrectly in conditions like `if(!ans=="")` and `if(!crit=="")`. The negation operator has higher precedence than equality, so this evaluates as `(!ans)==""`  which is not the intended logic.
**Fix:** Change to proper empty check: `if(ans != "" && ans != null)` or use modern truthy check: `if(ans)`.
**File:** master/frm_new_question.jsp
**Line(s):** 50, 54

### P24-F24: Magic Numbers in Validation
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Timezone validation uses hardcoded numbers (12, 59) without explanation. The checkDigital function checks for length of 6 without context. These magic numbers reduce code readability.
**Fix:** Extract magic numbers to named constants with descriptive names. Example: `const MAX_TIMEZONE_HOUR = 12; const MAX_TIMEZONE_MINUTE = 59; const SMART_CARD_LENGTH = 6;`
**File:** frm_new_location.jsp, frm_new_user.jsp
**Line(s):** frm_new_location.jsp: 69, 74; frm_new_user.jsp: 579

### P24-F25: Inconsistent Operation Code Naming
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Operation codes (op_code) use inconsistent naming conventions: "loc_add", "quest_add", "user_add", "vehicle_add", "vehicle_add1", "vehicle_add_short", "veh_typ_add", "veh_typ_add_pop", "override_codes", "override_codes_del", "mod_repl". Some use underscores throughout, some use abbreviated forms, creating confusion.
**Fix:** Standardize op_code naming to a consistent pattern, such as: "location_add", "question_add", "user_add", "vehicle_add", "vehicle_add_customer", "vehicle_add_short", "vehicle_type_add", "vehicle_type_add_popup", "override_code_add", "override_code_delete", "module_replace".
**File:** All 10 files
**Line(s):** Various hidden input fields and scriptlet assignments

### P24-F26: Tight Coupling to Specific Bean Structure
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** All files use the same bean class "com.torrent.surat.fms6.master.Databean_getuser" regardless of whether they're managing users, vehicles, locations, or questions. This suggests the bean is doing too much and creates tight coupling.
**Fix:** Refactor to use specific bean classes for different entities: Databean_location, Databean_question, Databean_user, Databean_vehicle. This follows single responsibility principle and improves maintainability.
**File:** All 10 files
**Line(s):** All files at their JSP useBean declarations

### P24-F27: Inconsistent Date Initialization Pattern
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Some files check `if(veh_last_serv.equalsIgnoreCase(""))` while others check `if(veh_last_serv==null || veh_last_serv.equalsIgnoreCase(""))`. This inconsistency can lead to NullPointerException in some scenarios.
**Fix:** Standardize to always check for null first: `if(veh_last_serv == null || veh_last_serv.isEmpty())` or use a utility method for safe date initialization.
**File:** frm_new_vehicle.jsp, frm_new_vehicle1.jsp
**Line(s):** frm_new_vehicle.jsp: 137-148; frm_new_vehicle1.jsp: 162-173

### P24-F28: Missing CSRF Protection
**Severity:** HIGH
**Description:** All forms submit to servlets without any visible CSRF token protection. This leaves the application vulnerable to Cross-Site Request Forgery attacks where malicious sites could trigger unwanted actions.
**Fix:** Implement CSRF protection by adding a token to each form. Generate a unique token in the session, include it as a hidden field, and validate it on the server side before processing any state-changing operation.
**File:** All 10 files
**Line(s):** All form submissions

### P24-F29: Mixed HTTP Parameter Handling
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Parameter retrieval uses inconsistent null handling: some use `request.getParameter("x")==null?"":request.getParameter("x")` while others use `request.getParameter("x")==null?dt:request.getParameter("x")` with different defaults. This creates maintenance burden.
**Fix:** Create a utility method for parameter retrieval with default values: `String getParam(String name, String defaultValue)`. This centralizes the null-handling logic and improves consistency.
**File:** All files
**Line(s):** Multiple scriptlet blocks in all files

### P24-F30: Deprecated HTML Attributes
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** HTML uses deprecated attributes like `align=center`, `bgcolor="#FFFFFF"`, `border="1"`, `bordercolor="#635C5F"` which are not valid in HTML5. These should be replaced with CSS.
**Fix:** Move all presentational attributes to CSS classes. Create semantic CSS classes for table styling and replace inline attributes.
**File:** All 10 files
**Line(s):** Throughout all table definitions

### P24-F31: Inconsistent Error Message Construction
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Some validation functions build error messages by concatenating strings (`msg+= "First Name, ";`) while others use complete hardcoded alerts. The concatenated approach leads to grammatically incorrect messages with trailing commas and inconsistent punctuation.
**Fix:** Standardize error messaging to use an array of missing fields, then join them properly with "and" before the last item. Example: `["First Name", "Last Name"].join(", ")` → "First Name, Last Name" or create complete messages per validation failure.
**File:** Multiple files
**Line(s):** frm_new_location.jsp: 22, 27; frm_new_question.jsp: 22; frm_new_user.jsp: 34-82; frm_new_vehicle.jsp: 27-52

### P24-F32: Redundant Comparison in Validation
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Validation checks like `idtype==0` (numeric comparison) and `idtype=="pin id"` (string comparison) suggest the variable type is ambiguous. Line 64 compares to string "pin id" but line 59 compares to number 0.
**Fix:** Ensure consistent data types. If idtype is a string, all comparisons should be string comparisons. If numeric, use consistent numeric codes. Consider using constants or enums.
**File:** master/frm_new_user.jsp
**Line(s):** 59, 64-66

### P24-F33: Implicit Global Variable Declaration
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Variable `xmlHttp` is used throughout AJAX functions but never declared with `var`, `let`, or `const`. This creates an implicit global variable which can cause conflicts and unexpected behavior across different script contexts.
**Fix:** Declare `var xmlHttp;` at the top of the script block or use `let/const` within each function scope. Better yet, use the modern fetch API which doesn't require global state.
**File:** frm_new_user.jsp, frm_override_codes.jsp
**Line(s):** Used throughout AJAX functions in both files (e.g., frm_new_user.jsp: 270, 314, 357, etc.)

### P24-F34: Duplicate GetXmlHttpObject Implementation
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** The GetXmlHttpObject() function is identically implemented in multiple files, violating DRY principle and creating maintenance burden if the implementation needs to change.
**Fix:** Extract GetXmlHttpObject() and all common AJAX utility functions to a shared JavaScript file (e.g., ajax-utils.js) and include it in all pages that need AJAX functionality.
**File:** frm_new_user.jsp, frm_override_codes.jsp
**Line(s):** frm_new_user.jsp: 398-419; frm_override_codes.jsp: 249-270

### P24-F35: Inefficient DOM Access Pattern
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Code repeatedly accesses `document.forms[0]` multiple times within the same function. Each access traverses the DOM which is inefficient.
**Fix:** Cache the form reference at the start of functions: `var form = document.forms[0];` then use `form.loc.value`, `form.tz.value`, etc. This improves performance and readability.
**File:** All files with JavaScript validation
**Line(s):** Throughout validation functions in all files

### P24-F36: Missing Accessibility Attributes
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Form inputs lack proper accessibility attributes like `<label>` elements with `for` attributes, `aria-label`, or `aria-describedby`. Bold text is used adjacent to inputs but not properly associated.
**Fix:** Wrap label text in `<label for="inputId">` tags and add corresponding `id` attributes to inputs. Add `aria-required="true"` to required fields. Add `aria-invalid` and `aria-describedby` for error messaging.
**File:** All 10 files
**Line(s):** Throughout all form field definitions

### P24-F37: Inconsistent Comment Field Implementation
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Three vehicle forms use TEXTAREA for comments with nearly identical implementation, but each has slightly different ROWS/cols values (all use ROWS="3" but cols varies) and positioning. The old commented-out input implementation remains in two files.
**Fix:** Standardize TEXTAREA dimensions and positioning across all vehicle forms. Remove all commented-out alternatives.
**File:** frm_new_vehicle.jsp, frm_new_vehicle1.jsp
**Line(s):** frm_new_vehicle.jsp: 253-254; frm_new_vehicle1.jsp: 287-288

### P24-F38: Unused Parameter in Function
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** The closeform() function in several files accepts multiple parameters (veh_typ_cd, loc_cd, st_dt, end_dt, search_crit, form_cd, cust_cd, dep_cd) but concatenates them all into a URL. However, frm_new_vehicle_short.jsp doesn't have cust_cd or dep_cd parameters, creating inconsistency.
**Fix:** Ensure all forms pass consistent parameters to closeform(), or make the function flexible enough to handle optional parameters properly.
**File:** Multiple files
**Line(s):** Various closeform() function definitions

### P24-F39: Inconsistent Readonly Attribute Spelling
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** HTML uses lowercase `readonly` in TEXTAREA elements (line 568) while JavaScript uses camelCase `readOnly` property (lines 558, 568, 572). While both work, this inconsistency can cause confusion.
**Fix:** Use consistent casing. For HTML attributes use lowercase `readonly`, for JavaScript DOM properties use camelCase `readOnly`.
**File:** frm_new_user.jsp, frm_override_codes.jsp
**Line(s):** frm_new_user.jsp: 558, 568, 572; frm_override_codes.jsp: 568

### P24-F40: String Replace Without Global Flag
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Code uses `code.replace(", ","\n")` to replace comma-space with newline for override codes display. JavaScript's replace() without regex global flag only replaces the first occurrence, potentially leaving subsequent ", " patterns unchanged.
**Fix:** Use global regex replace: `code.replace(/, /g, "\n")` to ensure all comma-space patterns are replaced.
**File:** master/frm_override_codes.jsp
**Line(s):** 239

### P24-F41: Commented Code Block in HTML
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Large commented-out close button in frm_new_vehicle_type.jsp line 138. This creates uncertainty about whether the close functionality should exist.
**Fix:** Either remove the commented line entirely if close button is not needed in this form, or uncomment it if the button should be present.
**File:** master/frm_new_vehicle_type.jsp
**Line(s):** 138

### P24-F42: Inconsistent Vehicle Type Operation Codes
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Two nearly identical vehicle type forms use different operation codes: "veh_typ_add" vs "veh_typ_add_pop", suggesting the servlet treats popup and regular forms differently. This may be unnecessary complexity.
**Fix:** Review if separate operation codes are truly needed. If the forms have identical backend logic, use the same operation code. Otherwise, document why the distinction exists.
**File:** frm_new_vehicle_type.jsp, frm_new_vehicle_type_pop.jsp
**Line(s):** frm_new_vehicle_type.jsp: 172; frm_new_vehicle_type_pop.jsp: 136

## Reading Evidence

### frm_send_canbus_rules.jsp
**Scriptlet blocks:**
- Lines 20-37: Parameter retrieval and filter initialization - gets veh_cd, form_cd, message parameters; sets opcode "send_canbus_rules"; calls filter.init(); retrieves vehicle data (vcan_mod, gmtp_id, hire_no, serial_no, veh_type)
- Lines 46-53: Null check scriptlet for hire_no variable

## Findings

### P25-F01: Duplicate GetXmlHttpObject() function across multiple files
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** The GetXmlHttpObject() function is duplicated identically in frm_service_flag.jsp (lines 112-133), frm_site_hours.jsp (lines 147-168), and frm_upload_questions.jsp (lines 87-108). This is a clear violation of DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself) principle. The function creates XMLHttpRequest objects with cross-browser compatibility for IE6-era browsers.
**Fix:** Extract GetXmlHttpObject() to a shared JavaScript file (e.g., ../js/ajax.js) and include it in pages that need AJAX functionality. This reduces code duplication and makes maintenance easier.
**File:** master/frm_service_flag.jsp, master/frm_site_hours.jsp, master/frm_upload_questions.jsp
**Line(s):** 112-133, 147-168, 87-108

### P25-F02: Commented-out code in frm_service_status.jsp
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Line 78 contains commented-out code: `//setTimeout(closeform(),5000);`. This suggests the developer considered auto-closing the popup after 5 seconds but decided against it. Commented-out code creates clutter and confusion about whether it should be reinstated.
**Fix:** Remove the commented-out setTimeout call. If auto-close functionality is needed in the future, it can be retrieved from version control.
**File:** master/frm_service_status.jsp
**Line(s):** 78

### P25-F03: Commented-out code in frm_upload_questions.jsp
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Line 52 contains commented-out code: `//var cust = document.forms[0].cust.value;`. The variable is never used, suggesting dead code that was partially removed but not fully cleaned up.
**Fix:** Remove the commented-out line completely as it serves no purpose.
**File:** master/frm_upload_questions.jsp
**Line(s):** 52

### P25-F04: Unused variables in AJAX callback functions
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Multiple AJAX callback functions declare `var xmlDoc=xmlHttp.responseXML.documentElement;` but never use the xmlDoc variable. Found in frm_service_flag.jsp (lines 35, 89), frm_site_hours.jsp (lines 36, 86, 128), and frm_upload_questions.jsp (line 65).
**Fix:** Remove the unused xmlDoc variable declarations from all affected functions (stateChanged, stateChanged1, stateChanged3).
**File:** master/frm_service_flag.jsp, master/frm_site_hours.jsp, master/frm_upload_questions.jsp
**Line(s):** 35, 89, 36, 86, 128, 65

### P25-F05: Unused variables in get_assigned_vehicle.jsp
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variables tmp1 (gmtp_id) and tmp2 (serial_no) are retrieved from ArrayLists on lines 32-33 but never used in the XML response. Only tmp (vehicle code) and tmp3 (hire number) are actually used in line 35.
**Fix:** Remove the unused variable assignments for tmp1 and tmp2, or include them in the XML response if they're needed by the client.
**File:** master/get_assigned_vehicle.jsp
**Line(s):** 32-33

### P25-F06: Non-descriptive single-letter variable names in JavaScript
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** JavaScript confirmation dialogs use single-letter variable 'a' to store the result (frm_send_canbus_rules.jsp line 9, frm_sendmsg.jsp line 11, frm_service_status.jsp lines 20-23, frm_setup_driver.jsp lines 20-23, frm_upload_questions.jsp lines 23-24, frm_vehicle_prod.jsp lines 22-23). This reduces code readability.
**Fix:** Rename variable 'a' to 'confirmed' or 'userConfirmed' for better clarity throughout all affected files.
**File:** master/frm_send_canbus_rules.jsp, master/frm_sendmsg.jsp, master/frm_service_status.jsp, master/frm_setup_driver.jsp, master/frm_upload_questions.jsp, master/frm_vehicle_prod.jsp
**Line(s):** 9, 11, 20-23, 20-23, 23-24, 22-23

### P25-F07: Repetitive null check pattern in frm_send_canbus_rules.jsp
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Lines 46-92 repeat the same null-checking pattern four times with only variable names changing (hire_no, serial_no, gmtp_id, veh_type). Each block checks if the variable is null and assigns "-" if so. This is repetitive and violates DRY principle.
**Fix:** Create a JSP helper method or JSTL function to handle null-to-dash conversion, e.g., `<%! String nullToDash(String value) { return (value == null) ? "-" : value; } %>`, then use `<%=nullToDash(hire_no) %>`.
**File:** master/frm_send_canbus_rules.jsp
**Line(s):** 46-92

### P25-F08: Inconsistent null/empty string checking patterns
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Different files use inconsistent patterns for checking null or empty strings. Some use `==null||==""||==" "||=="  "` (frm_service_status.jsp lines 34-65), while others use just `==null` or `.equals("")` or `.equalsIgnoreCase("")`. This inconsistency makes the codebase harder to maintain.
**Fix:** Standardize on a single null/empty checking approach across all JSP files. Consider using Apache Commons StringUtils.isBlank() or creating a utility method for consistent behavior.
**File:** master/frm_send_canbus_rules.jsp, master/frm_service_flag.jsp, master/frm_service_status.jsp
**Line(s):** Multiple locations

### P25-F09: Duplicate AJAX fetch_dept() pattern across files
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** The fetch_dept() function and its corresponding stateChanged callback are duplicated with minor variations across frm_service_flag.jsp (lines 60-110), frm_site_hours.jsp (lines 56-104), and frm_upload_questions.jsp (lines 44-85). This is another DRY violation.
**Fix:** Extract the AJAX department fetching logic to a shared JavaScript file. Use a generic callback function that can populate any specified dropdown element.
**File:** master/frm_service_flag.jsp, master/frm_site_hours.jsp, master/frm_upload_questions.jsp
**Line(s):** 60-110, 56-104, 44-85

### P25-F10: JavaScript loop variables missing 'var' keyword
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** In frm_setup_driver.jsp, the sel_all_dep() (line 42) and sel_all_mod() (line 50) functions use loop variable 'i' without declaring it with 'var'. This creates implicit global variables, which is a JavaScript anti-pattern and can cause bugs.
**Fix:** Add 'var' keyword before 'i' in both loops: `for(var i=0;i<sz;i++)`.
**File:** master/frm_setup_driver.jsp
**Line(s):** 42, 50

### P25-F11: Dead code - always-true flag variable pattern
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Multiple files have a pattern where `var flg=true;` is set, then immediately checked with `if(flg)` without any intervening code that could change it. This appears in frm_setup_driver.jsp (lines 15-18), frm_upload_questions.jsp (lines 15-18), and frm_vehicle_prod.jsp (lines 16-18). The flag serves no purpose in its current form.
**Fix:** Remove the flg variable and the if(flg) wrapper entirely, or implement actual validation logic that could set flg to false.
**File:** master/frm_setup_driver.jsp, master/frm_upload_questions.jsp, master/frm_vehicle_prod.jsp
**Line(s):** 15-18, 15-18, 16-18

### P25-F12: Duplicate two-column checkbox layout pattern
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** In frm_setup_driver.jsp, the department checkbox loop (lines 184-220) and vehicle model checkbox loop (lines 230-266) use nearly identical two-column layout logic. This duplicated pattern makes maintenance harder.
**Fix:** Create a reusable JSP function or tag to render two-column checkbox layouts, parameterized by the data source, input name, and selection state.
**File:** master/frm_setup_driver.jsp
**Line(s):** 184-220, 230-266

### P25-F13: Hard-coded disabled attribute in frm_service_status.jsp
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Line 225 has a hard-coded `disabled` attribute on the accumulated hours input field. This appears inconsistent with the dynamic disabling pattern used for other fields (lines 246, 252) where disableField() method is called.
**Fix:** Either remove the hard-coded disabled attribute if the field should be editable, or document why this field is always disabled while others are conditionally disabled.
**File:** master/frm_service_status.jsp
**Line(s):** 225

### P25-F14: Magic number "0" for default type_cd
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** In frm_site_hours.jsp, type_cd is set to "0" as a default value (lines 291-294), then later checked for empty or "0" (line 356). The magic number "0" lacks semantic meaning and should be a named constant.
**Fix:** Replace magic number "0" with a named constant like DEFAULT_TYPE_CD or use empty string consistently for unselected state.
**File:** master/frm_site_hours.jsp
**Line(s):** 291-294, 356

### P25-F15: Unusual timestamp generation pattern
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Line 177 in frm_site_hours.jsp uses `(new Date()).toISOString().replace(/[^0-9]/g, "")` to generate a timestamp for cache busting. This is an unusual and inefficient approach compared to `new Date().getTime()`.
**Fix:** Replace with simpler and more standard `new Date().getTime()` for cache busting.
**File:** master/frm_site_hours.jsp
**Line(s):** 177

### P25-F16: Typo in user-facing text
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Line 160 in frm_vehicle_prod.jsp displays "Truck Delevered?" with "Delevered" misspelled (should be "Delivered").
**Fix:** Correct the spelling to "Truck Delivered?".
**File:** master/frm_vehicle_prod.jsp
**Line(s):** 160

### P25-F17: Deprecated language attribute in script tags
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Several files use the deprecated `language="javascript"` attribute in script tags (e.g., frm_service_flag.jsp lines 9-13, frm_service_status.jsp lines 7-11, frm_setup_driver.jsp lines 7-11, frm_site_hours.jsp lines 10-14, frm_upload_questions.jsp lines 7-11, frm_vehicle_prod.jsp lines 7-11). The `language` attribute was deprecated in HTML 4.01 in favor of the `type` attribute.
**Fix:** Remove the `language="javascript"` or `language="JavaScript"` attributes from all script tags. The type attribute is already present or can be omitted in HTML5.
**File:** master/frm_service_flag.jsp, master/frm_service_status.jsp, master/frm_setup_driver.jsp, master/frm_site_hours.jsp, master/frm_upload_questions.jsp, master/frm_vehicle_prod.jsp
**Line(s):** 9-13, 7-11, 7-11, 10-14, 7-11, 7-11

### P25-F18: Inconsistent ArrayList spacing style
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** ArrayList declarations show inconsistent spacing. Some use `ArrayList<Type>` (frm_site_hours.jsp line 340), while others use `ArrayList type_name` without generics. This style inconsistency reduces code uniformity.
**Fix:** Standardize ArrayList declarations to use generic type parameters consistently throughout all JSP files: `ArrayList<String>`, `ArrayList<DayhoursBean>`, etc.
**File:** Multiple files
**Line(s):** Various

### P25-F19: Access level default handling without error checking
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** In frm_service_flag.jsp (lines 227-231), frm_service_status.jsp (lines 172-176), frm_setup_driver.jsp (implicit), frm_site_hours.jsp (lines 302-306), and frm_upload_questions.jsp (lines 135-139), access_level is checked for empty string and defaulted to "5", then parsed to int without try-catch. If access_level contains invalid data, Integer.parseInt() will throw NumberFormatException.
**Fix:** Add try-catch around Integer.parseInt(access_level) with appropriate error handling or logging. Alternatively, use a utility method that safely parses with a default value.
**File:** master/frm_service_flag.jsp, master/frm_service_status.jsp, master/frm_site_hours.jsp, master/frm_upload_questions.jsp
**Line(s):** 227-231, 172-176, 302-306, 135-139

### P25-F20: Inconsistent string comparison methods
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** The codebase uses both `.equals("")` and `.equalsIgnoreCase("")` for checking empty strings. Since empty string case is irrelevant (empty string is always lowercase), using equalsIgnoreCase is semantically incorrect and creates inconsistency.
**Fix:** Standardize on `.equals("")` for empty string checks. Reserve `.equalsIgnoreCase()` for actual case-insensitive string comparisons.
**File:** Multiple files
**Line(s):** Various

---
## Findings

### P26-F01: Inconsistent null-safety handling for request parameters
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Most files lack null checks for request.getParameter() calls, which can result in NullPointerException if parameters are missing. Only get_cust_vehicle.jsp uses a ternary operator for null-safety on the "q" parameter (line 8), but even that file is inconsistent with other parameters. This inconsistency creates maintenance issues and potential runtime errors.
**Fix:** Standardize null-safety across all files by adding null checks with default empty string values for all request.getParameter() calls, e.g., `request.getParameter("param")==null?"":request.getParameter("param")`
**File:** master/get_customer.jsp, master/get_customergp.jsp, master/get_dept.jsp, master/get_driver.jsp, master/get_impact.jsp, master/get_mst_cd_exist.jsp, master/get_mst_cd_like.jsp, master/get_site.jsp, master/get_unassigned_vehicle.jsp
**Line(s):** get_customer.jsp:8, get_customergp.jsp:8, get_dept.jsp:8-9, get_driver.jsp:8-9, get_impact.jsp:8, get_mst_cd_exist.jsp:3-5, get_mst_cd_like.jsp:8-11, get_site.jsp:8, get_unassigned_vehicle.jsp:8

### P26-F02: Missing null checks for session attributes causing potential NullPointerException
**Severity:** HIGH
**Description:** Files that retrieve session attributes (access_level, access_cust, access_site, access_dept) perform equalsIgnoreCase() directly on the retrieved value without null checking first. If the session attribute is null, this will throw a NullPointerException at runtime. The current code assumes these session attributes always exist.
**Fix:** Add null checks before calling equalsIgnoreCase(). Example: `if(access_level == null || access_level.equalsIgnoreCase(""))` or use defensive programming with Objects.requireNonNullElse() if Java 9+.
**File:** master/get_dept.jsp, master/get_mst_cd_like.jsp, master/get_site.jsp
**Line(s):** get_dept.jsp:18, get_mst_cd_like.jsp:22, get_site.jsp:17

### P26-F03: Dead code - unused variables parsed from access_level
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variable 'al' is declared and assigned from Integer.parseInt(access_level) but never used in the code. This creates unnecessary processing and code clutter.
**Fix:** Remove the unused variable declaration and assignment: `int al = Integer.parseInt(access_level);`
**File:** master/get_dept.jsp, master/get_mst_cd_like.jsp, master/get_site.jsp
**Line(s):** get_dept.jsp:22, get_mst_cd_like.jsp:26, get_site.jsp:21

### P26-F04: Dead code - unused ArrayList variables retrieved but never used
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variables Vsite_cd and Vsite_nm are retrieved from filter but never used in building the response. This wastes memory and creates confusion about the intent of the code.
**Fix:** Remove the unused ArrayList retrieval calls: `ArrayList Vsite_cd = filter.getLocation_cd();` and `ArrayList Vsite_nm = filter.getLocation_nm();`
**File:** master/get_driver.jsp
**Line(s):** 16-17

### P26-F05: Dead code - unused temporary variables in loop
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** In get_cust_vehicle.jsp, variables tmp1 (Vgmtp_id) and tmp2 (Vveh_serial) are retrieved from ArrayLists but never used in the XML response construction. The response only uses tmp (veh_cd) and tmp3 (hire_no). This suggests either incomplete implementation or leftover code from refactoring.
**Fix:** Remove unused variable assignments or use them if they were intended to be part of the response: `String tmp1= (String)Vgmtp_id.get(i);` and `String tmp2= (String)Vveh_serial.get(i);`
**File:** master/get_cust_vehicle.jsp
**Line(s):** 34-35

### P26-F06: Dead code - unused temporary variables in loop
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** In get_unassigned_vehicle.jsp, variables tmp1 (Vgmtp_id) and tmp3 (Vveh_hire) are retrieved from ArrayLists but never used. The response uses tmp2 (serial_no) instead. This inconsistency with get_cust_vehicle.jsp (which uses hire_no) suggests incorrect implementation.
**Fix:** Review the intended response format. If serial_no is correct, remove tmp1 and tmp3. If hire_no should be used instead (consistent with get_cust_vehicle.jsp), change line 29 to use tmp3.
**File:** master/get_unassigned_vehicle.jsp
**Line(s):** 26, 28

### P26-F07: Commented-out code
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Debug System.out.println statement is commented out but left in the source code. Commented-out code clutters the codebase and creates ambiguity about whether it should be reinstated or removed.
**Fix:** Remove the commented-out debug statement completely: `//System.out.println(resp+"\n");`
**File:** master/get_mst_cd_exist.jsp
**Line(s):** 38

### P26-F08: Dead code - empty else block
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Empty else block serves no purpose and clutters the code. If no action is needed when the condition is false, the else block should be removed entirely.
**Fix:** Remove the empty else block (lines 33-36)
**File:** master/get_mst_cd_exist.jsp
**Line(s):** 33-36

### P26-F09: Style inconsistency - inconsistent empty result handling
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Files handle empty result sets inconsistently. Some files (get_cust_vehicle.jsp, get_dept.jsp, get_driver.jsp, get_site.jsp, get_mst_cd_like.jsp) provide a default "-Select-" or "-void-" option in the else clause, while others (get_customer.jsp, get_customergp.jsp, get_unassigned_vehicle.jsp) have no else clause at all. This creates an inconsistent user experience.
**Fix:** Standardize the empty result handling pattern across all files. Either all files should provide a default option, or none should, depending on the UI requirements.
**File:** master/get_customer.jsp, master/get_customergp.jsp, master/get_unassigned_vehicle.jsp
**Line(s):** get_customer.jsp:28, get_customergp.jsp:28, get_unassigned_vehicle.jsp:32

### P26-F10: Style inconsistency - formatting of JSP directives
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** File get_mst_cd_exist.jsp has all JSP directives compressed onto a single line (line 1) with no spacing, making it extremely difficult to read. All other files properly format directives on separate lines.
**Fix:** Reformat line 1 to place each JSP directive (page language, page import, page errorPage, jsp:useBean) on separate lines consistent with the other 9 files.
**File:** master/get_mst_cd_exist.jsp
**Line(s):** 1

### P26-F11: Style inconsistency - mixed XML response structures
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Different files use different XML root element names and structures without clear justification. Some use `<body>`, some use `<drivers>`, some use `<slots>`. While this may be intentional based on different AJAX consumers, it creates maintenance confusion and violates the principle of consistent API design.
**Fix:** Document the reason for different XML structures or standardize to a consistent pattern with a descriptive root element name that matches the content type (e.g., use `<vehicles>` instead of `<body>` for vehicle responses).
**File:** master/get_cust_vehicle.jsp, master/get_customer.jsp, master/get_customergp.jsp, master/get_dept.jsp, master/get_driver.jsp, master/get_mst_cd_exist.jsp, master/get_mst_cd_like.jsp, master/get_site.jsp, master/get_unassigned_vehicle.jsp
**Line(s):** get_cust_vehicle.jsp:28, get_customer.jsp:18, get_customergp.jsp:18, get_dept.jsp:39, get_driver.jsp:22, get_mst_cd_exist.jsp:20, get_mst_cd_like.jsp:44, get_site.jsp:37, get_unassigned_vehicle.jsp:20

### P26-F12: Style inconsistency - typo in comment
**Severity:** INFO
**Description:** Comment on line 29 contains typo "contro" instead of "control". While minor, typos in comments reduce code professionalism and readability.
**Fix:** Correct the comment from `//end of access level contro` to `//end of access level control`
**File:** master/get_dept.jsp
**Line(s):** 29

### P26-F13: Style inconsistency - missing XML entity escaping
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Only get_cust_vehicle.jsp performs XML entity escaping for ampersands (line 45: `resp.replace("&","&amp;")`). All other files that generate XML responses do not escape special XML characters (&, <, >, ", '). This creates potential XML parsing errors and XSS vulnerabilities if the data contains these characters.
**Fix:** Apply consistent XML entity escaping across all files that generate XML responses. Consider creating a shared utility method for proper XML escaping of all special characters, not just ampersands.
**File:** master/get_customer.jsp, master/get_customergp.jsp, master/get_dept.jsp, master/get_driver.jsp, master/get_impact.jsp, master/get_mst_cd_exist.jsp, master/get_mst_cd_like.jsp, master/get_site.jsp, master/get_unassigned_vehicle.jsp
**Line(s):** All XML output lines in respective files

### P26-F14: Leaky abstraction - use of raw ArrayList without type parameters
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** All files use raw ArrayList types (e.g., `ArrayList Vveh_cd`) without generic type parameters, requiring unsafe casts like `(String)Vveh_cd.get(i)`. This is deprecated since Java 5 and generates unchecked warnings during compilation. Raw types bypass compile-time type safety.
**Fix:** Replace all raw ArrayList declarations with parameterized types: `ArrayList<String> Vveh_cd = filter.getVveh_cd();` This requires updating the filter bean methods to return parameterized types as well.
**File:** master/get_cust_vehicle.jsp, master/get_customer.jsp, master/get_customergp.jsp, master/get_dept.jsp, master/get_driver.jsp, master/get_mst_cd_exist.jsp, master/get_mst_cd_like.jsp, master/get_site.jsp, master/get_unassigned_vehicle.jsp
**Line(s):** get_cust_vehicle.jsp:22-25, get_customer.jsp:14-15, get_customergp.jsp:14-15, get_dept.jsp:35-36, get_driver.jsp:16-19, get_mst_cd_exist.jsp:13-16, get_mst_cd_like.jsp:39-41, get_site.jsp:33-34, get_unassigned_vehicle.jsp:14-17

### P26-F15: Style inconsistency - variable naming conventions mixed
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variable naming is inconsistent. Some variables use camelCase (vcard_id, vdriver_nm in get_driver.jsp lines 18-19) while others in the same files use PascalCase with 'V' prefix (Vsite_cd, Vsite_nm). The 'V' prefix convention itself is non-standard and unclear in meaning.
**Fix:** Standardize on Java naming conventions: use camelCase for all local variables and remove the unclear 'V' prefix. For example: `vehCd`, `gmtpId`, `vehSerial`, `vehHire` instead of `Vveh_cd`, `Vgmtp_id`, `Vveh_serial`, `Vveh_hire`.
**File:** All files
**Line(s):** Throughout all files

### P26-F16: Leaky abstraction - inconsistent parameter naming between files
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Different files use different parameter names for semantically equivalent data. For example, "q" and "r" are used inconsistently: in get_dept.jsp "q" is loc_cd and "r" is cust_cd, but in get_driver.jsp "q" is loc_cd and "r" is cust_cd with reversed semantic usage. This tight coupling to parameter names makes the code fragile and hard to maintain.
**Fix:** Standardize parameter names across all files to use descriptive names that match their semantic meaning (e.g., always use "cust_cd", "site_cd", "dept_cd" as parameter names instead of single-letter codes).
**File:** master/get_dept.jsp, master/get_driver.jsp
**Line(s):** get_dept.jsp:8-9, get_driver.jsp:8-9

---
## Summary

**Total findings:** 16

**By severity:**
- CRITICAL: 0
- HIGH: 1
- MEDIUM: 6
- LOW: 8
- INFO: 1

**Major issues identified:**
1. Missing null checks for session attributes (HIGH risk of NullPointerException)
2. Inconsistent parameter null-safety handling (MEDIUM)
3. Missing XML entity escaping in 9 of 10 files (MEDIUM - XSS risk)
4. Raw ArrayList types without generics (MEDIUM - deprecated API)
5. Multiple instances of dead/unused code (LOW-MEDIUM)
6. Inconsistent code style and formatting across files (LOW)

**Recommendations:**
1. Immediately address HIGH severity finding (P26-F02) to prevent runtime exceptions
2. Standardize null-safety patterns and XML escaping across all files
3. Migrate from raw types to generic types for compile-time type safety
4. Remove all dead code and commented-out code
5. Establish and document coding standards for these AJAX endpoint JSP files

- master/get_vehicle_dept.jsp
- master/pic_upload_logo.jsp
- master/register.jsp
- master/rpt_preop_unsync_qustion.jsp

**Branch verified:** multi-customer-sync-to-master

## Findings

### P27-F01: Unused variables retrieved from bean
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variables tmp1 (from Vgmtp_id) and tmp3 (from Vveh_hire) are retrieved from the filter bean but never used in the XML response construction. Only tmp (Vveh_cd) and tmp2 (Vveh_serial) are used. This suggests either incomplete implementation or unnecessary data retrieval.
**Fix:** Either remove the unused variables and their corresponding getter calls (lines 15, 17), or incorporate them into the XML response if they were intended to be included.
**File:** master/get_vehicle.jsp
**Line(s):** 15, 17, 26, 28

### P27-F02: Commented-out debug code
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** An alert statement is commented out (//alert(ext);) which is typical debug code that should be removed rather than commented. Commented-out code clutters the codebase and creates confusion about whether the code should be reinstated.
**Fix:** Remove the commented-out alert statement entirely.
**File:** master/pic_upload_logo.jsp
**Line(s):** 28

### P27-F03: Commented-out HTML meta tag
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** An HTML meta tag for IE compatibility mode is commented out (<!-- <meta http-equiv="X-UA-Compatible" content="IE=EmulateIE7" /> -->). This should either be removed or reinstated based on current requirements.
**Fix:** Remove the commented-out meta tag if IE7 emulation is no longer needed, or reinstate it if backward compatibility is required.
**File:** pic_upload_logo.jsp
**Line(s):** 7 (in update_all_user_weigand.jsp), 7 (in rpt_preop_unsync_qustion.jsp)

### P27-F04: Unused variable dt
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variable dt is assigned from Util.getGen_dt() but never used in the JSP. This represents dead code that unnecessarily executes a method call with no effect.
**Fix:** Remove the declaration and assignment of dt on line 62 if the generated date is not needed.
**File:** master/pic_upload_logo.jsp
**Line(s):** 62

### P27-F05: Hardcoded path with redundant slashes
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** The image path "..//images//pics//"+fnm uses double slashes which, while functionally equivalent in many systems, represents inconsistent path formatting and poor style.
**Fix:** Change to "../images/pics/"+fnm using single slashes for consistency.
**File:** master/pic_upload_logo.jsp
**Line(s):** 50

### P27-F06: Raw type usage for ArrayList
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** ArrayList is used without type parameters (raw types) throughout the file, which generates unchecked warnings in Java 5+ and represents deprecated coding practice. Examples include ArrayList Vuser_cd, ArrayList Vuser_fnm, ArrayList Vuser_lnm.
**Fix:** Use parameterized types such as ArrayList&lt;String&gt; for type safety and to eliminate compiler warnings.
**File:** master/get_user.jsp
**Line(s):** 18, 19, 20

### P27-F07: Raw type usage for ArrayList
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** ArrayList is used without type parameters (raw types) throughout the file. Examples include ArrayList Vveh_cd, ArrayList Vgmtp_id, ArrayList Vveh_serial, ArrayList Vveh_hire.
**Fix:** Use parameterized types such as ArrayList&lt;String&gt; for type safety and to eliminate compiler warnings.
**File:** master/get_vehicle.jsp
**Line(s):** 14, 15, 16, 17

### P27-F08: Raw type usage for ArrayList
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** ArrayList is used without type parameters (raw types). Examples include ArrayList Vvt_cd, ArrayList Vvt.
**Fix:** Use parameterized types such as ArrayList&lt;String&gt; for type safety and to eliminate compiler warnings.
**File:** master/get_vehicle_dept.jsp
**Line(s):** 20, 21

### P27-F09: Raw type usage for ArrayList
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** ArrayList is used without type parameters (raw types) for arrModel on line 59. The code does use ArrayList&lt;EntityBean&gt; on line 59, so this is actually correctly parameterized. However, this finding should be adjusted.
**Fix:** N/A - This file uses parameterized ArrayList correctly.

This represents either incomplete refactoring or obsolete code that should be removed.

### P27-F11: Unused variable dt

### P27-F12: Raw type usage for ArrayList (extensive)
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Extensive use of raw ArrayList types without type parameters throughout the file, generating numerous unchecked warnings. Examples include ArrayList Vveh_typ_cd, ArrayList Vveh_typ_nm, ArrayList vcust_cd, ArrayList Vhire_no, ArrayList vvord_no, ArrayList vvquest, etc.
**Fix:** Use parameterized types such as ArrayList&lt;String&gt; for all ArrayList declarations to eliminate compiler warnings and improve type safety.

### P27-F14: Unused JSP bean declarations

**Description:** Two JSP beans (Databean_report filter and UtilBean Util) are declared on lines 39-40 but are never used in the file. These beans occupy memory unnecessarily.
**Fix:** Remove the unused jsp:useBean declarations for filter and Util.

**File:** master/update_all_user_weigand.jsp
**Line(s):** 7

### P27-F17: Inconsistent variable naming convention
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variable naming is inconsistent throughout get_user.jsp, mixing conventions such as ArrayList names starting with capital V (Vuser_cd, Vuser_fnm) versus lowercase local variables (resp, tmp). While the capital V prefix may indicate a vector/ArrayList, this is not a standard Java naming convention and creates inconsistency.
**Fix:** Standardize variable naming to follow Java conventions: use camelCase for local variables and avoid non-standard prefixes. For example, use userCodes instead of Vuser_cd.
**File:** master/get_user.jsp
**Line(s):** 18-20, 22-31

### P27-F18: Inconsistent variable naming convention
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Similar to get_user.jsp, variable naming mixes capital V prefixes (Vveh_cd, Vgmtp_id) with lowercase variables (vt_cd, resp, tmp). This inconsistency appears across multiple JSP files in the codebase.
**Fix:** Standardize variable naming to follow Java conventions: use camelCase consistently and avoid non-standard prefixes.
**File:** master/get_vehicle.jsp
**Line(s):** 8, 14-17, 19-29

### P27-F19: Inconsistent parameter name abbreviations
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Parameters use inconsistent abbreviations: c_cd (customer code), s_cd (site code), d_cd (department code) versus full names or different patterns in other files. This makes the codebase harder to understand and maintain.
**Fix:** Establish and follow consistent naming conventions for parameter names across all JSP files. For example, always use cust_cd, site_cd, dept_cd or always use c_cd, s_cd, d_cd consistently.
**File:** master/get_vehicle_dept.jsp
**Line(s):** 8-10

## Findings

### P28-F01: Commented-out code block in changePage function
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Lines 12-21 in menu.jsp contain a large commented-out code block within the changePage JavaScript function. This includes URL construction logic with formId parameter and fallback to index1.jsp. Commented code should either be removed or reinstated, as it clutters the codebase and creates confusion about intended behavior.
**Fix:** Either delete the commented code if it's no longer needed, or reinstate it with proper version control if there's a valid reason to keep the alternative implementation.
**File:** menu/menu.jsp
**Line(s):** 12-21

### P28-F02: Commented-out HTML submenu block
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Lines 56-61 in menu-new.jsp contain a commented-out HTML submenu structure. This dead markup clutters the code and should be removed if not needed.
**Fix:** Remove the commented-out submenu HTML block if the functionality is not required. If needed for future use, document it properly in version control instead.
**File:** menu/menu-new.jsp
**Line(s):** 56-61

### P28-F03: Extensive commented-out JavaScript animation configuration
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Lines 116-128 and 141-145 in menu.jsp contain large blocks of commented-out JavaScript code for menu animation configuration (showDelay, switchDelay, hideDelay, cssLitClass, showOnClick, animInSpeed, animOutSpeed, and multiple animation type assignments). This represents significant dead code that should be cleaned up.
**Fix:** Remove all commented-out animation configuration code. If specific animation settings are needed in the future, they can be retrieved from version control.
**File:** menu/menu.jsp
**Line(s):** 116-128, 141-145

### P28-F04: Unused img[] array declaration
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Both files declare a String array 'img[]' (line 50 in menu-new.jsp, line 68 in menu.jsp) containing image paths, but this array is never referenced or used anywhere in either file.
**Fix:** Remove the unused img[] array declarations from both files.
**File:** menu/menu-new.jsp, menu/menu.jsp
**Line(s):** 50 (menu-new.jsp), 68 (menu.jsp)

### P28-F05: Unused ArrayList declarations
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Lines 44-47 in menu-new.jsp and lines 62-65 in menu.jsp declare four ArrayList objects (mform_id, mform_name, mform_type, mapplication_path) that are never populated or used. These variables are created but serve no purpose.
**Fix:** Remove the unused ArrayList declarations from both files.
**File:** menu/menu-new.jsp, menu/menu.jsp
**Line(s):** 44-47 (menu-new.jsp), 62-65 (menu.jsp)

### P28-F06: Empty conditional block for administration module
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Lines 85-87 in menu-new.jsp contain an if-statement that filters out the "administration" module but has an empty body. This is confusing as it appears to intentionally skip administration modules without explanation.
**Fix:** Either add a comment explaining why administration modules are excluded, or remove this filtering logic if it's not needed. Consider using 'continue' statement for clarity: `if(mdn.toLowerCase().equals("administration")) { continue; }`
**File:** menu/menu-new.jsp
**Line(s):** 85-87

### P28-F07: String comparison using == instead of equals()
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Line 7 in menu-new.jsp and line 28 in menu.jsp use the == operator to compare strings (session.getAttribute("username")==""). In Java, == compares object references, not content. This can lead to incorrect behavior.
**Fix:** Replace `session.getAttribute("username")==""` with `"".equals(session.getAttribute("username"))` or use null-safe comparison methods.
**File:** menu/menu-new.jsp, menu/menu.jsp
**Line(s):** 7 (menu-new.jsp), 28 (menu.jsp)

### P28-F08: Raw type usage for ArrayList (deprecated)
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Both files use raw ArrayList types without generic type parameters throughout (lines 32-34, 44-47, 78-82 in menu-new.jsp; lines 50-52, 62-65 in menu.jsp). This generates unchecked warnings at compile time and is deprecated practice in modern Java.
**Fix:** Add type parameters to all ArrayList declarations, e.g., `ArrayList<String> mmodule_nm = menu1.getModuleName();`
**File:** menu/menu-new.jsp, menu/menu.jsp
**Line(s):** 32-34, 44-47, 78-82 (menu-new.jsp); 50-52, 62-65 (menu.jsp)

### P28-F09: Inconsistent variable naming
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** The files use inconsistent variable names for the same concept. menu.jsp uses 'user_cd2' (line 37) while menu-new.jsp uses 'user_cd' (line 16). This inconsistency makes the code harder to maintain.
**Fix:** Standardize on a single variable name across both files. Recommend using 'user_cd' consistently.
**File:** menu/menu-new.jsp, menu/menu.jsp
**Line(s):** 16 (menu-new.jsp), 37 (menu.jsp)

### P28-F10: Inconsistent path structure between menu files
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** The two menu files use different base paths for navigation. menu.jsp uses paths like "../home/index4.jsp", "../home/index3.jsp", "../home/home1.jsp" while menu-new.jsp uses "../pages/index.jsp", "../pages/admin.jsp". This inconsistency suggests the files may be for different parts of the application but creates confusion.
**Fix:** Document the intended usage of each menu file and ensure the path structure is consistent within each file's context. Consider consolidating to a single menu implementation if both serve the same purpose.
**File:** menu/menu-new.jsp, menu/menu.jsp
**Line(s):** 38, 41, 67 (menu-new.jsp); 56, 59, 84 (menu.jsp)

### P28-F11: Unused parameter retrieval
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Line 21 in menu-new.jsp retrieves a request parameter 'mnm' into variable 'mdn1' but this variable is never used anywhere in the file.
**Fix:** Remove the unused parameter retrieval: `String mdn1 = request.getParameter("mnm")==null?"":request.getParameter("mnm");`
**File:** menu/menu-new.jsp
**Line(s):** 21

### P28-F12: Unused variable x in menu loop
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Line 69 in menu-new.jsp declares String variable 'x' and assigns it a value (line 72), but this variable is never subsequently used for any purpose.
**Fix:** Remove the unused variable declaration and assignment (lines 69, 72).
**File:** menu/menu-new.jsp
**Line(s):** 69, 72

### P28-F13: Unused icon path variable
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Both files retrieve icon_path values into variable 'ipth' (line 68 in menu-new.jsp, line 85 in menu.jsp) but never use these values. The icon paths are retrieved but not rendered in the UI.
**Fix:** Either use the icon paths in the menu rendering (add img tags with src="<%=ipth %>"), or remove the unused variable assignments.
**File:** menu/menu-new.jsp, menu/menu.jsp
**Line(s):** 68 (menu-new.jsp), 85 (menu.jsp)

### P28-F14: Inconsistent whitespace and indentation
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Both files exhibit inconsistent indentation and spacing patterns. For example, tabs vs spaces are mixed, and there's irregular spacing around operators and in conditional statements.
**Fix:** Apply consistent formatting throughout both files. Use a consistent indentation scheme (either tabs or spaces, not both) and standardize spacing around operators and braces.
**File:** menu/menu-new.jsp, menu/menu.jsp
**Line(s):** Throughout both files

### P28-F15: Session attribute access not encapsulated
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Both files directly access session attributes multiple times throughout the code (getAttribute calls scattered across lines). This creates tight coupling to the session implementation and violates encapsulation principles, making the code harder to test and maintain.
**Fix:** Create a helper class or method to encapsulate session attribute retrieval with proper type safety and null handling. Example: `UserContext.getFromSession(session)` that returns a typed object with all needed user attributes.
**File:** menu/menu-new.jsp, menu/menu.jsp
**Line(s):** 7-19, 36 (menu-new.jsp); 28-40, 54 (menu.jsp)

### P28-F16: Hardcoded path strings throughout
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Both files contain numerous hardcoded relative paths ("../pages/", "../home/", "../images/") scattered throughout. This creates a leaky abstraction where presentation logic is tightly coupled to the application's directory structure.
**Fix:** Extract path constants to a configuration file or constants class. Use a base path variable that can be configured centrally: `String BASE_PATH = request.getContextPath(); String PAGES_PATH = BASE_PATH + "/pages/";`
**File:** menu/menu-new.jsp, menu/menu.jsp
**Line(s):** 38, 41, 67, 102 (menu-new.jsp); 56, 59, 84, 97, 99, 101 (menu.jsp)

### P28-F17: Inconsistent module filtering logic
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** menu-new.jsp has logic to filter out the "administration" module (lines 85-87), but menu.jsp does not have equivalent filtering logic. This inconsistency suggests the two menu implementations may have different intended behaviors that are not clearly documented.
**Fix:** Document why administration modules are filtered in menu-new.jsp but not menu.jsp. If both menus should have the same behavior, make the filtering consistent. If they serve different purposes, add clear comments explaining the difference.
**File:** menu/menu-new.jsp
**Line(s):** 85-87

### P28-F18: Deprecated HTML font tag usage
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** menu.jsp uses deprecated HTML <font> tags for styling (lines 75, 77, 89, 92, 97, 99, 101). The <font> tag has been deprecated since HTML 4 and should not be used in modern web applications.
**Fix:** Replace <font> tags with CSS classes or inline styles. Example: Replace `<font color="#002037">Home</font>` with `<span class="menu-active">Home</span>` and define the color in CSS.
**File:** menu/menu.jsp
**Line(s):** 75, 77, 89, 92, 97, 99, 101

---
**End of P28 Code Quality Audit**
