## Findings

### P64-F01: Large blocks of commented-out code
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Multiple files contain large blocks of commented-out code that should either be removed or reinstated. Commented code clutters the codebase, makes maintenance harder, and creates risk of accidental reactivation. In email_daily_driver_summary.jsp, 17 lines of commented code (lines 57-73) relate to alternative hour calculation fields. In email_driver_impact_report.jsp, lines 25-40 contain a large commented-out access control section. In email_driver_access_abuse.jsp, lines 50, 71-72, 77-78, and 121-122 contain commented-out column definitions.
**Fix:** Review each commented block with the development team. If the code is truly obsolete, delete it (version control preserves history). If it represents alternative functionality that may be needed, document the requirement properly and either implement it with feature flags or remove it until actually needed. Remove lines 57-73 from email_daily_driver_summary.jsp, lines 25-40 from email_driver_impact_report.jsp, and lines 50, 71-72, 77-78, 121-122 from email_driver_access_abuse.jsp.
**File:** reports/email_daily_driver_summary.jsp, reports/email_driver_impact_report.jsp, reports/email_driver_access_abuse.jsp
**Line(s):** email_daily_driver_summary.jsp: 57-73; email_driver_impact_report.jsp: 25-40; email_driver_access_abuse.jsp: 50, 71-72, 77-78, 121-122

### P64-F02: Hardcoded external URLs violate abstraction
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Multiple JSP files contain hardcoded URLs to "http://fms.fleetiq360.com//images/linde/" which creates tight coupling between presentation and deployment infrastructure. This leaky abstraction makes it difficult to migrate environments, test locally, or deploy to different domains. The URLs appear in email_curr_unit_report.jsp (lines 124, 171), email_driver_impact_report.jsp (lines 109, 119, 162, 174, 211), and email_driver_licence_expiry_detail.jsp (lines 57, 92). Note the double slashes in the path suggesting poor URL construction.
**Fix:** Extract all hardcoded URLs to a configuration property or constant (similar to existing SessionVar.reportLogo and LindeConfig.reportHead patterns). Create a configuration property like LindeConfig.imageBaseUrl = "http://fms.fleetiq360.com/images/linde/" and reference it as <%= LindeConfig.imageBaseUrl %>left-round-e.jpg. This centralizes URL management and enables environment-specific configuration.
**File:** reports/email_curr_unit_report.jsp, reports/email_driver_impact_report.jsp, reports/email_driver_licence_expiry_detail.jsp
**Line(s):** email_curr_unit_report.jsp: 124, 171; email_driver_impact_report.jsp: 109, 119, 162, 174, 211; email_driver_licence_expiry_detail.jsp: 57, 92

### P64-F03: Syntax error - extra double quote in inline style
**Severity:** HIGH
**Description:** Multiple files have a syntax error in HTML style attributes where an extra double quote appears at the end of the style value, creating malformed HTML. For example, in email_curr_unit_report.jsp line 197: `style="height: 40px;background-color: rgb(194, 232, 102);font-family:MyCustomFont, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size:0.82em; font-weight:bold; color:#222; text-decoration:none;""` has an extra quote before the closing `>`. This pattern repeats in 5 files.
**Fix:** Remove the extra double quote at the end of each affected style attribute. Change `text-decoration:none;""` to `text-decoration:none;"` at the following locations: email_curr_unit_report.jsp line 197, email_daily_driver_summary.jsp line 109, email_daily_veh_summary.jsp line 95, email_driver_access_abuse.jsp line 99, email_driver_licence_expiry_detail.jsp line 113, email_hour_counter.jsp line 96.
**File:** reports/email_curr_unit_report.jsp, reports/email_daily_driver_summary.jsp, reports/email_daily_veh_summary.jsp, reports/email_driver_access_abuse.jsp, reports/email_driver_licence_expiry_detail.jsp, reports/email_hour_counter.jsp
**Line(s):** email_curr_unit_report.jsp: 197; email_daily_driver_summary.jsp: 109; email_daily_veh_summary.jsp: 95; email_driver_access_abuse.jsp: 99; email_driver_licence_expiry_detail.jsp: 113; email_hour_counter.jsp: 96

### P64-F04: Duplicate access control initialization code
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Eight of ten files contain nearly identical access control initialization code (typically lines 20-41), where access_level, access_cust, access_site, and access_dept are hardcoded to the same values ("1", "", "", ""), then immediately set on the filter bean. This represents significant code duplication and leaky abstraction - access control logic is embedded in presentation layer files. The duplication appears in email_daily_driver_summary.jsp, email_daily_veh_summary.jsp, email_driver_access_abuse.jsp, email_curr_unit_report.jsp, email_driver_util.jsp, email_equipment_bypass_alert_report.jsp, and email_hour_counter.jsp.
**Fix:** Extract access control initialization to a common include file (e.g., includes/init_access_control.jsp) or add an initialization method to the filter bean itself (e.g., filter.initAccessControlFromSession(session)). This centralizes the logic and makes it easier to modify access control behavior. Remove duplicated code from individual JSP files and replace with a single include or method call.
**File:** reports/email_daily_driver_summary.jsp, reports/email_daily_veh_summary.jsp, reports/email_driver_access_abuse.jsp, reports/email_curr_unit_report.jsp, reports/email_driver_util.jsp, reports/email_equipment_bypass_alert_report.jsp, reports/email_hour_counter.jsp
**Line(s):** Approximately lines 20-41 in each file

### P64-F05: Unused local variables
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Multiple files declare a local variable `al` (parsed integer from access_level) that is never subsequently used. This variable is declared at line 31 in email_daily_driver_summary.jsp, line 28 in email_daily_veh_summary.jsp, line 28 in email_driver_access_abuse.jsp, line 36 in email_driver_util.jsp, line 31 in email_equipment_bypass_alert_report.jsp, line 30 in email_hour_counter.jsp, and line 47 in email_curr_unit_report.jsp. Additionally, email_hour_counter.jsp line 48 declares `dsum_no_sesst` which is never used.
**Fix:** Remove all unused variable declarations. Delete the `int al = Integer.parseInt(access_level);` lines from the affected files, and remove `int dsum_no_sesst = filter.getDsum_no_sesst();` from email_hour_counter.jsp line 48.
**File:** reports/email_daily_driver_summary.jsp, reports/email_daily_veh_summary.jsp, reports/email_driver_access_abuse.jsp, reports/email_curr_unit_report.jsp, reports/email_driver_util.jsp, reports/email_equipment_bypass_alert_report.jsp, reports/email_hour_counter.jsp
**Line(s):** email_daily_driver_summary.jsp: 31; email_daily_veh_summary.jsp: 28; email_driver_access_abuse.jsp: 28; email_curr_unit_report.jsp: 47; email_driver_util.jsp: 36; email_equipment_bypass_alert_report.jsp: 31; email_hour_counter.jsp: 30, 48

### P64-F06: Inconsistent variable naming conventions
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variable naming conventions are inconsistent across the JSP files, mixing different prefixing styles and case conventions. Some files use camelCase prefixes (vcust_cd, Vloc_cd with capital V, vveh_cd with lowercase v), while others use underscore-prefixed patterns (a_driv_cd, dir_driv_nm). Some variables inexplicably start with capital letters (Vloc_cd, Vuser_cd, Vgp_cd) while most use lowercase. For example, email_curr_unit_report.jsp uses vcust_cd, Vloc_cd, vdept_cd, vveh_cd all with different capitalization patterns. This inconsistency makes the code harder to read and maintain.
**Fix:** Establish and document a consistent variable naming convention for the project. Recommended approach: use lowercase prefixes consistently (e.g., vCustCd or v_cust_cd, not Vloc_cd). Apply the convention systematically across all JSP files. Consider prefixes like 'list' or 'arr' instead of single letters to improve readability.
**File:** All files
**Line(s):** Throughout - examples at email_curr_unit_report.jsp: 65-70; email_driver_impact_report.jsp: 63-71; email_driver_util.jsp: 56-67

### P64-F07: Inconsistent HTML attribute quoting
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** HTML attributes are inconsistently quoted throughout the files. Some attributes use quotes (align="center"), while others omit them (align=center). For example, email_driver_access_abuse.jsp uses both `<tr align=center` (line 99) and `<th align=center>` (lines 104, 105), while email_driver_league.jsp uses `<tr align=left` (line 75) and `<td align=left>` (lines 106-108). While HTML5 allows unquoted attributes in some cases, consistent quoting is a best practice for maintainability and compatibility.
**Fix:** Standardize on always quoting HTML attribute values. Update all instances of unquoted attributes (align=center, align=left, etc.) to use quoted values (align="center", align="left"). This can be done with a search-and-replace operation across all JSP files.
**File:** All files
**Line(s):** Throughout - examples at email_driver_access_abuse.jsp: 99, 104-106; email_driver_league.jsp: 75, 106-108; email_hour_counter.jsp: 95, 102

### P64-F08: No-op conditional assignment
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** In email_driver_impact_report.jsp lines 98-101, there is a conditional block that checks if model_nm equals "all" and then assigns "all" to model_nm, which is a no-operation. The code reads: `if (model_nm.equals("all")) { model_nm = "all"; }`. This serves no functional purpose and suggests incomplete or misunderstood logic.
**Fix:** Remove the useless conditional block entirely (lines 98-101 of email_driver_impact_report.jsp). If the intent was to normalize or validate the model_nm value, implement the actual validation logic. If this was meant to handle a default case, the logic should be corrected.
**File:** reports/email_driver_impact_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 98-101

### P64-F09: Inconsistent character encoding declaration
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Most JSP files that declare DOCTYPE use UTF-8 encoding implicitly through included headers, but email_driver_util.jsp explicitly declares iso-8859-1 encoding in the meta tag at line 4: `<meta http-equiv="Content-Type" content="text/html; charset=iso-8859-1" />`. Additionally, email_curr_unit_report.jsp also uses iso-8859-1 at line 6. This encoding inconsistency can lead to character rendering issues, especially for internationalized content given that the application uses ResourceBundle for i18n.
**Fix:** Standardize on UTF-8 encoding across all JSP files. Change the charset declaration in email_driver_util.jsp line 4 and email_curr_unit_report.jsp line 6 from "charset=iso-8859-1" to "charset=UTF-8". Ensure that the web.xml and server configuration also specify UTF-8 encoding.
**File:** reports/email_driver_util.jsp, reports/email_curr_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** email_driver_util.jsp: 4; email_curr_unit_report.jsp: 6

### P64-F10: Potential XSS vulnerability from unescaped output
**Severity:** HIGH
**Description:** All JSP files use direct expression output (`<%=variable%>`) for displaying data retrieved from request parameters and database queries without any HTML escaping or encoding. This creates potential Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerabilities if malicious content is stored in the database or passed through request parameters. Examples include `<%=get_user%>`, `<%=dsum_driver_nm.get(i)%>`, `<%=a_driv_nm.get(i)%>` throughout all files. While some values come from controlled sources, defense-in-depth requires output encoding.
**Fix:** Implement consistent output encoding for all user-controlled or database-derived data. Options include: (1) Use JSTL's `<c:out value="${variable}" />` instead of `<%=variable%>` which provides automatic HTML escaping, or (2) create and use a custom escaping utility function like `<%=HtmlUtils.escape(variable)%>`. Apply encoding especially to fields that display user-provided content like driver names, vehicle names, and any text that originates from request parameters or external data sources.
**File:** All files
**Line(s):** Throughout - pervasive pattern in all data output expressions

### P64-F11: Missing CSS class name standardization
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** CSS class names are inconsistent across files. Some files use "email_header" (with underscore), others use different variations. For example, email_driver_access_abuse.jsp line 68 has a typo: `class="email_heade tbl-report-row"` (missing 'r' in header), while other files use `class="email_header tbl-report-row"`. This inconsistency suggests copy-paste errors and makes CSS maintenance difficult.
**Fix:** Standardize CSS class naming across all JSP files. Fix the typo in email_driver_access_abuse.jsp line 68 by changing "email_heade" to "email_header". Review and standardize all CSS class names to use a consistent naming convention (e.g., always use underscores or always use hyphens, but not both).
**File:** reports/email_driver_access_abuse.jsp
**Line(s):** 68

### P64-F12: Inconsistent null-check and ternary operator formatting
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Null checks and ternary operators for request parameters show inconsistent spacing and formatting. Some use `request.getParameter("x")==null?"":` (no spaces), others use `request.getParameter("x") == null ? "" :` (with spaces). For example, email_daily_driver_summary.jsp line 9 uses `==null?"1":` while email_driver_impact_report.jsp lines 11-22 use `== null ? "" :`. This formatting inconsistency reduces code readability.
**Fix:** Standardize ternary operator formatting across all JSP files. Recommended format with spaces for readability: `request.getParameter("x") == null ? "default" : request.getParameter("x")`. Apply consistent spacing in all parameter retrieval blocks.
**File:** All files
**Line(s):** Throughout - examples at email_daily_driver_summary.jsp: 9-16; email_driver_impact_report.jsp: 11-22

## Findings

### P65-F01: Raw type ArrayList usage throughout all files
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** All 10 JSP files use raw ArrayList types without generic type parameters (e.g., `ArrayList` instead of `ArrayList<String>`). This is a deprecated practice that generates unchecked warnings during compilation and eliminates compile-time type safety. Examples found throughout all files when retrieving data from filter bean methods.
**Fix:** Add generic type parameters to all ArrayList declarations. For example, change `ArrayList Vgp_cd = filter.getGroup_cd();` to `ArrayList<String> Vgp_cd = filter.getGroup_cd();`. This requires updating the Databean_report class method signatures to return properly typed collections.
**File:** All 10 files
**Line(s):** Throughout (e.g., email_hourmeter_exception.jsp:47-54, email_impact_meter_report.jsp:52-74, email_impact_report.jsp:52-72, email_impact_report_exc.jsp:59-106, email_inspection_report.jsp:45-60, email_key_hour_util.jsp:49-63, email_monthly_online.jsp:53, email_per_driver_summary.jsp:55-96, email_per_veh_summary.jsp:52-93, email_preop_chk.jsp:49-58)

### P65-F02: Unused variable 'al' declared but never used
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** The variable `al` is declared and assigned the parsed access_level value but is never used in the code. This appears in 9 out of 10 files (all except email_inspection_report.jsp). The variable serves no purpose and should be removed.
**Fix:** Remove the line `int al = Integer.parseInt(access_level);` from all affected files.
**File:** email_hourmeter_exception.jsp, email_impact_meter_report.jsp, email_impact_report.jsp, email_impact_report_exc.jsp, email_key_hour_util.jsp, email_monthly_online.jsp, email_per_driver_summary.jsp, email_per_veh_summary.jsp, email_preop_chk.jsp
**Line(s):** email_hourmeter_exception.jsp:28, email_impact_meter_report.jsp:32, email_impact_report.jsp:32, email_impact_report_exc.jsp:38, email_key_hour_util.jsp:29, email_monthly_online.jsp:33, email_per_driver_summary.jsp:36, email_per_veh_summary.jsp:34, email_preop_chk.jsp:30

### P65-F03: Commented-out code should be removed
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Line 212 in email_impact_report.jsp contains commented-out code: `// tmp = tmp + " (" + DataUtil.convertToImpPerc((String) tmpMeter.get(j)) + "%)";`. This appears to be old logic for converting meter details that was replaced. Commented code clutters the codebase and creates confusion about whether it should be reinstated or was intentionally disabled.
**Fix:** Remove the commented line 212. If the conversion logic might be needed in the future, it should be tracked in version control history, not left as a comment.
**File:** email_impact_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 212

### P65-F04: Hardcoded image URLs instead of configuration
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Hardcoded URLs "http://fms.fleetiq360.com//images/linde/left-round-e.jpg" and "http://fms.fleetiq360.com//images/linde/right-round-e.jpg" appear in multiple files. These URLs are environment-specific and should use configuration properties like LindeConfig.externalUrl. Note the double slashes in the path which is also a minor bug.
**Fix:** Replace hardcoded URLs with configuration-based URLs, e.g., `<%=LindeConfig.externalUrl%>/images/linde/left-round-e.jpg`. This ensures consistency and makes environment changes easier.
**File:** email_hourmeter_exception.jsp, email_inspection_report.jsp
**Line(s):** email_hourmeter_exception.jsp:109, 156; email_inspection_report.jsp:126, 155

### P65-F05: Inconsistent naming conventions for variables
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variable naming is inconsistent throughout the files. Some use camelCase (e.g., `vmachine_nm`, `sm_hour_meter`), others use snake_case (e.g., `gp_cd`, `user_cd`, `loc_cd`), and some mix both styles. This violates Java naming conventions which recommend camelCase for local variables.
**Fix:** Standardize all variable names to use camelCase convention. For example, rename `gp_cd` to `gpCd`, `user_cd` to `userCd`, `loc_cd` to `locCd`, etc. This is a large refactoring that should be done systematically across all JSPs.
**File:** All 10 files
**Line(s):** Throughout all files (parameter and local variable declarations)

### P65-F06: Access level hardcoded to "1" instead of retrieving from session
**Severity:** HIGH
**Description:** In all files, access control variables are hardcoded: `String access_level = "1"; String access_cust=""; String access_site=""; String access_dept="";`. The comment on line 18 (or similar) states "Session variables for the access level control" but no session retrieval occurs. This is a security issue as it bypasses proper access control, potentially allowing users to see data they shouldn't access.
**Fix:** Retrieve access control values from session attributes rather than hardcoding them. For example: `String access_level = (String) session.getAttribute("access_level");` with appropriate null checks and defaults. Alternatively, if these reports are only accessed through controlled pathways, document why hardcoded values are acceptable.
**File:** All files except email_inspection_report.jsp (which has comment but no hardcoded values)
**Line(s):** email_hourmeter_exception.jsp:20-23, email_impact_meter_report.jsp:23-26, email_impact_report.jsp:23-26, email_impact_report_exc.jsp:29-32, email_key_hour_util.jsp:25-28, email_monthly_online.jsp:25-28, email_per_driver_summary.jsp:27-30, email_per_veh_summary.jsp:26-29, email_preop_chk.jsp:22-25

### P65-F07: Empty form tags with no action attribute
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Four files contain empty `<form>` tags with no action attribute, method, or form elements. These forms serve no functional purpose and should be removed. They appear to be remnants from copy-paste operations.
**Fix:** Remove the empty `<form>` and `</form>` tags from these files as they serve no purpose.
**File:** email_impact_report_exc.jsp, email_per_driver_summary.jsp, email_per_veh_summary.jsp, email_preop_chk.jsp
**Line(s):** email_impact_report_exc.jsp:113-231, email_per_driver_summary.jsp:103-233, email_per_veh_summary.jsp:101-233, email_preop_chk.jsp:63-136

### P65-F08: Inconsistent conditional logic for empty string checks
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Multiple files use `if(access_level.equalsIgnoreCase(""))` to check for empty strings (lines 24-27 in most files). However, the access_level is hardcoded to "1" just above, so this condition will never be true. This is dead code that should be removed or fixed if session retrieval is implemented.
**Fix:** If implementing proper session retrieval (per F06), keep this logic but fix it to handle null values: `if(access_level == null || access_level.isEmpty())`. If not implementing session retrieval, remove this dead conditional block.
**File:** email_hourmeter_exception.jsp, email_impact_meter_report.jsp, email_impact_report.jsp, email_impact_report_exc.jsp, email_monthly_online.jsp, email_per_driver_summary.jsp, email_per_veh_summary.jsp, email_preop_chk.jsp
**Line(s):** email_hourmeter_exception.jsp:24-27, email_impact_meter_report.jsp:28-31, email_impact_report.jsp:28-31, email_impact_report_exc.jsp:34-37, email_monthly_online.jsp:29-32, email_per_driver_summary.jsp:32-35, email_per_veh_summary.jsp:30-33, email_preop_chk.jsp:26-29

### P65-F09: Inconsistent HTML attribute quoting in inline styles
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** HTML attributes use inconsistent quoting styles. Some use double quotes for align attribute (`align="center"`), others don't quote it at all (`align=center`). This appears in table rendering loops across multiple files. Modern HTML standards require quoted attribute values.
**Fix:** Consistently quote all HTML attribute values. Change `align=center` to `align="center"` throughout.
**File:** email_hourmeter_exception.jsp, email_impact_meter_report.jsp, email_impact_report.jsp, email_key_hour_util.jsp, email_monthly_online.jsp, email_preop_chk.jsp
**Line(s):** email_hourmeter_exception.jsp:178, 183; email_impact_meter_report.jsp:139, 141; email_impact_report.jsp:138, 140; email_key_hour_util.jsp:109, 115, 121, 231, 237, 244; email_monthly_online.jsp:116, 122, 128; email_preop_chk.jsp:90, 94, 111, 118

### P65-F10: Duplicate logic between email_impact_meter_report.jsp and email_impact_report.jsp
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Files email_impact_meter_report.jsp and email_impact_report.jsp contain nearly identical code with only minor differences. email_impact_meter_report.jsp includes tmp_imp_data display (line 184) and impact meter details (line 212), while email_impact_report.jsp has these commented out or removed. This duplication makes maintenance difficult.
**Fix:** Consolidate these two files into a single JSP with a parameter to control whether to show impact meter data. This reduces code duplication and simplifies maintenance. Alternatively, use JSP includes for the common rendering logic.
**File:** email_impact_meter_report.jsp, email_impact_report.jsp
**Line(s):** email_impact_meter_report.jsp:1-272, email_impact_report.jsp:1-271

### P65-F11: Missing bean class imports in files that use them
**Severity:** INFO
**Description:** Only email_monthly_online.jsp explicitly imports the FleetCheckBean class it uses. However, the pattern suggests other files may rely on implicit imports or classes made available through includes. While this works, it makes dependencies unclear and can cause issues if include order changes.
**Fix:** Add explicit import statements for all Java classes used in JSP scriptlets to make dependencies clear and prevent potential ClassNotFoundException errors.
**File:** email_monthly_online.jsp (positive example), potentially others
**Line(s):** email_monthly_online.jsp:3 (has import), others missing explicit imports for util classes

### P65-F12: Inline CSS styles instead of external stylesheets
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Multiple files use extensive inline CSS styles in table rows and cells (e.g., `style="height: 40px;background-color: rgb(194, 232, 102);font-family:MyCustomFont, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size:0.82em; font-weight:bold; color:#222;"`). This creates maintainability issues and increases page size. While alternate_rows.css is included, inline styles override it.
**Fix:** Move repeated inline styles to CSS classes in alternate_rows.css or a dedicated email report stylesheet. Apply classes instead of inline styles.
**File:** All email_* JSP files
**Line(s):** Throughout rendering sections (e.g., email_hourmeter_exception.jsp:178-183, email_impact_meter_report.jsp:139-141, email_key_hour_util.jsp:115-121)

### P65-F13: Inconsistent date handling in email_key_hour_util.jsp
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Lines 18-19 in email_key_hour_util.jsp manually split date strings: `st_dt = st_dt.split(" ")[0]; to_dt = to_dt.split(" ")[0];`. This is a fragile approach that assumes a specific date format and could throw ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException if the format differs.
**Fix:** Use proper date parsing with SimpleDateFormat or Java 8 DateTimeFormatter to extract the date portion safely. For example: `SimpleDateFormat formatter = new SimpleDateFormat("yyyy-MM-dd"); String dateOnly = formatter.format(formatter.parse(st_dt));`
**File:** email_key_hour_util.jsp
**Line(s):** 18-19

### P65-F14: Commented System.out.println debug statement
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Line 20 in email_key_hour_util.jsp contains a commented-out debug statement: `//System.out.println(user_cd+","+loc_cd+","+dept_cd);`. Debug statements should be removed before production deployment, even if commented out.
**Fix:** Remove the commented debug line. If debugging is needed, use a proper logging framework like Log4j or SLF4J instead of System.out.
**File:** email_key_hour_util.jsp
**Line(s):** 20

### P65-F15: Unused variable declarations in loops
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Several files declare and immediately reassign ArrayList variables in loops, creating unnecessary object allocations. For example, in email_impact_meter_report.jsp lines 106-122, ArrayLists are declared with `new ArrayList()` then immediately reassigned from Vdriver.get(i). The `new ArrayList()` serves no purpose.
**Fix:** Remove the `new ArrayList()` initializations. Change `ArrayList dnm = new ArrayList(); dnm = (ArrayList)Vdriver.get(i);` to `ArrayList dnm = (ArrayList)Vdriver.get(i);`
**File:** email_impact_meter_report.jsp, email_impact_report.jsp, email_impact_report_exc.jsp
**Line(s):** email_impact_meter_report.jsp:106-122, email_impact_report.jsp:106-120, email_impact_report_exc.jsp:178-184

### P65-F16: Missing null checks for request parameters
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** All files retrieve request parameters with null coalescing (e.g., `request.getParameter("gp_cd")==null?"1":request.getParameter("gp_cd")`), but this pattern calls getParameter twice, which is inefficient. Additionally, there are no checks for empty strings vs null, which could lead to unexpected behavior.
**Fix:** Store parameter value in a variable first, then check for null or empty: `String gpCdParam = request.getParameter("gp_cd"); String gp_cd = (gpCdParam == null || gpCdParam.isEmpty()) ? "1" : gpCdParam;`
**File:** All 10 files
**Line(s):** Throughout parameter retrieval sections (e.g., email_hourmeter_exception.jsp:9-14, email_impact_meter_report.jsp:9-18)

### P65-F17: Duplicate column header in email_key_hour_util.jsp
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Line 89 contains a duplicate "Model" column header: `<th class="tbl-report-col">Model</th>` appears after line 88 which already has `<th><fmt:message key="rpt.tbl.header.model" /></th>`. This creates two adjacent "Model" columns in the table header.
**Fix:** Remove the duplicate column header on line 89 or verify if two model columns are intentional and add distinct labels.
**File:** email_key_hour_util.jsp
**Line(s):** 88-89

### P65-F18: Duplicate column header in email_key_hour_util.jsp (second table)
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Similar to F17, lines 210-212 in the second table (Un Utilised Units) also have duplicate "Model" headers.
**Fix:** Remove duplicate "Model" header on line 212.
**File:** email_key_hour_util.jsp
**Line(s):** 210-212

### P65-F19: Double bold tag in email_impact_report.jsp
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Line 89 contains nested bold tags: `<b><b><fmt:message key="global.drivername"/></b></th>`. The double `<b>` tag is redundant and indicates a copy-paste error.
**Fix:** Remove one of the `<b>` tags: `<b><fmt:message key="global.drivername"/></b>`
**File:** email_impact_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 89

### P65-F20: Inconsistent use of fmt:message vs hardcoded text
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Some files mix internationalized messages using `<fmt:message key="..."/>` with hardcoded English text. For example, email_per_driver_summary.jsp uses hardcoded "Reporting System" on line 108 and "Note:" on line 137, while other labels use fmt:message. This breaks internationalization consistency.
**Fix:** Ensure all user-facing text uses fmt:message tags for proper internationalization support. Replace hardcoded text with message keys.
**File:** email_per_driver_summary.jsp, email_per_veh_summary.jsp, email_inspection_report.jsp
**Line(s):** email_per_driver_summary.jsp:108, 137, 142, 146-161; email_per_veh_summary.jsp:108, 139; email_inspection_report.jsp:143-144

### P65-F21: Unused hidden input field
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Line 191 in email_hourmeter_exception.jsp contains a hidden input field `<input type="hidden" name="form_cd" value="<%=form_cd %>"/>` but there's no form to submit this data. Since there's no form tag in this file, this input serves no purpose.
**Fix:** Remove the hidden input field on line 191.
**File:** email_hourmeter_exception.jsp
**Line(s):** 191

### P65-F22: Inconsistent table styling approach
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Some files use CSS classes from alternate_rows.css (email_header, tbl-report-row, tbl-report-col), while others use inline bgcolor attributes and styles. email_impact_report_exc.jsp, email_per_driver_summary.jsp, and email_per_veh_summary.jsp use the alternate_rows class but also inline bgcolor, mixing approaches.
**Fix:** Standardize on one approach - preferably CSS classes with email_header for headers and appropriate classes for data rows. Remove inline bgcolor and style attributes where CSS classes can be used.
**File:** email_impact_report_exc.jsp, email_per_driver_summary.jsp, email_per_veh_summary.jsp, email_preop_chk.jsp
**Line(s):** Throughout table rendering sections

### P65-F23: Magic number for severity default value
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Three impact report files use "10" as the default severity value: `String sev = request.getParameter("sev")==null?"10":request.getParameter("sev");` This magic number should be a named constant or configuration value to make its meaning clear.
**Fix:** Define a constant like `private static final String DEFAULT_SEVERITY = "10";` and use it, or retrieve from configuration. Add a comment explaining what severity level 10 represents.
**File:** email_impact_meter_report.jsp, email_impact_report.jsp, email_impact_report_exc.jsp
**Line(s):** email_impact_meter_report.jsp:16, email_impact_report.jsp:16, email_impact_report_exc.jsp:22

### P65-F24: Missing LoadBundle initialization in some files
**Severity:** INFO
**Description:** email_hourmeter_exception.jsp declares and uses the bundle bean (line 4: `<jsp:useBean class="com.torrent.surat.fms6.util.LoadBundle" id="bundle" scope="request" />`) but most other files don't declare it yet still use fmt:message tags. This suggests bundle is set up in included headers, but inconsistent declaration makes dependencies unclear.
**Fix:** Either declare the bundle bean in all files that use it, or ensure the include files properly document that they provide this bean. Add comments explaining the dependency.
**File:** email_hourmeter_exception.jsp (has it), others (missing)
**Line(s):** email_hourmeter_exception.jsp:4

---

**Summary:**
- Total findings: 24
- Critical: 0
- High: 1 (F06 - hardcoded access control)
- Medium: 5 (F01, F03, F10, F16, F06)
- Low: 17
- Info: 1

**Primary concerns:**
1. Hardcoded access control bypasses security (F06)
2. Raw type usage creates compile warnings (F01)
3. Code duplication between impact report files (F10)
4. Commented code should be removed (F03)
5. Inconsistent coding standards throughout

**Recommendations:**
1. Address security issue (F06) immediately
2. Implement generic types for ArrayList usage (F01)
3. Consolidate duplicate impact report JSPs (F10)
4. Standardize coding conventions and naming
5. Consider migrating from scriptlets to JSTL/EL for better maintainability

  - LindeConfig.reportHead

### reports/email_unit_unlock_impact.jsp

## Findings

### P66-F01: Hardcoded access level credentials
**Severity:** HIGH
**Description:** All files contain hardcoded access level control variables (access_level="1", access_cust="", access_site="", access_dept="") that are never populated from session or authentication context. This suggests broken or bypassed access control logic. The variables are set to empty strings and then immediately assigned default values, indicating dead authentication code.
**Fix:** Remove dead access level control code (lines 22-40 in most files) or implement proper session-based access control using session.getAttribute() for authentication credentials.
**File:** reports/email_preop_chk_detailed.jsp, reports/email_preop_chk_fail.jsp, reports/email_preop_chk_inc.jsp, reports/email_seat_hour_util.jsp, reports/email_serv_maintenance.jsp, reports/email_unit_unlock.jsp, reports/email_unit_unlock_impact.jsp
**Line(s):** email_preop_chk_detailed.jsp:22-40, email_preop_chk_fail.jsp:24-40, email_preop_chk_inc.jsp:26-42, email_seat_hour_util.jsp:20-36, email_serv_maintenance.jsp:18-33, email_unit_unlock.jsp:24-40, email_unit_unlock_impact.jsp:26-42

### P66-F02: Unused variable 'al' after parsing access_level
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variable 'al' is declared and assigned from Integer.parseInt(access_level) but never used in the code. This represents dead code that adds no functionality.
**Fix:** Remove the unused variable declaration: "int al = Integer.parseInt(access_level);"
**File:** reports/email_preop_chk_detailed.jsp, reports/email_preop_chk_fail.jsp, reports/email_preop_chk_inc.jsp, reports/email_seat_hour_util.jsp, reports/email_serv_maintenance.jsp, reports/email_unit_unlock.jsp, reports/email_unit_unlock_impact.jsp
**Line(s):** email_preop_chk_detailed.jsp:33, email_preop_chk_fail.jsp:34, email_preop_chk_inc.jsp:36, email_seat_hour_util.jsp:30, email_serv_maintenance.jsp:27, email_unit_unlock.jsp:34, email_unit_unlock_impact.jsp:35

### P66-F03: Inconsistent null-safe parameter retrieval patterns
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Request parameters are retrieved using inconsistent null-safe patterns. Some files use "1" as default for gp_cd, others use "" (empty string). The pattern request.getParameter("X")==null?"default":request.getParameter("X") is repeated many times, creating maintenance burden and inconsistency.
**Fix:** Standardize parameter retrieval using a utility method like getParameterOrDefault(request, "param_name", "default_value") to ensure consistency and reduce code duplication.
**File:** All JSP files
**Line(s):** email_preop_chk_detailed.jsp:12-19, email_preop_chk_fail.jsp:12-19, email_preop_chk_inc.jsp:13-22, email_restricted_access_usage_report.jsp:13-21, email_seat_hour_util.jsp:9-16, email_serv_maintenance.jsp:10-15, email_shock_summary.jsp:11-18, email_super_master_auth_report.jsp:12-21, email_unit_unlock.jsp:12-21, email_unit_unlock_impact.jsp:13-22

### P66-F04: Duplicate path manipulation code for Excel directory
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Multiple files contain identical code for constructing the Excel report directory path using getProtectionDomain().getCodeSource().getLocation() and substring manipulation. This code is duplicated across 7 files with only minor variations in the final path suffix.
**Fix:** Extract this directory calculation logic into a utility method in a shared utility class to eliminate duplication and ensure consistent path handling.
**File:** reports/email_preop_chk_detailed.jsp, reports/email_preop_chk_fail.jsp, reports/email_preop_chk_inc.jsp
**Line(s):** email_preop_chk_detailed.jsp:78-83, email_preop_chk_fail.jsp:81-85, email_preop_chk_inc.jsp:109-114

### P66-F05: Hardcoded image URLs with domain names
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Multiple files contain hardcoded absolute URLs with specific domain names (http://fms.fleetiq360.com//images/linde/..., http://fleetfocus.lindemh.com.au/fms/images/linde/...). Note the double slashes in some URLs. These URLs will break if the domain changes or in different deployment environments.
**Fix:** Replace hardcoded URLs with configuration properties or relative paths. Use LindeConfig properties consistently (like LindeConfig.reportHead) instead of hardcoded URLs. Fix double slashes in URLs.
**File:** reports/email_preop_chk_inc.jsp, reports/email_restricted_access_usage_report.jsp, reports/email_serv_maintenance.jsp, reports/email_super_master_auth_report.jsp, reports/email_unit_unlock.jsp, reports/email_unit_unlock_impact.jsp
**Line(s):** email_preop_chk_inc.jsp:151,200, email_restricted_access_usage_report.jsp:90,100, email_serv_maintenance.jsp:121,160, email_super_master_auth_report.jsp:88,98, email_unit_unlock.jsp:132,187, email_unit_unlock_impact.jsp:119,176

### P66-F06: Inconsistent use of raw ArrayList types
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Most ArrayList declarations use raw types without generics (ArrayList instead of ArrayList<String>), which generates unchecked type warnings at compile time. This is inconsistent with some declarations that do use generics (e.g., ArrayList<ArrayList<String>> detailedQuesList, ArrayList<RestrictedAccessUsageBean>).
**Fix:** Add proper generic type parameters to all ArrayList declarations to eliminate compiler warnings and improve type safety. Example: change "ArrayList veh_nm = filter.getVmachine_nm();" to "ArrayList<String> veh_nm = filter.getVmachine_nm();"
**File:** All JSP files
**Line(s):** email_preop_chk_detailed.jsp:52-60, email_preop_chk_fail.jsp:56-66, email_preop_chk_inc.jsp:56-86, email_restricted_access_usage_report.jsp:41-49, email_seat_hour_util.jsp:49-64, email_serv_maintenance.jsp:44-93, email_shock_summary.jsp:33-34, email_super_master_auth_report.jsp:39-47, email_unit_unlock.jsp:56-63,97-100, email_unit_unlock_impact.jsp:57-63,80-83

### P66-F07: Inconsistent formatting and indentation in HTML attributes
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Inline styles in table rows have inconsistent formatting with double quotes inside double quotes, extra spaces, and trailing double quotes. Example: style="height: 40px;background-color: rgb(194, 232, 102);font-family:MyCustomFont, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size:0.82em; font-weight:bold; color:#222; text-decoration:none;"" (note the double quote at the end).
**Fix:** Standardize HTML attribute formatting, remove trailing double quotes, and consider extracting repeated inline styles to CSS classes defined in alternate_rows.css.
**File:** All JSP files with table rendering
**Line(s):** email_preop_chk_detailed.jsp:141,153,256, email_preop_chk_fail.jsp:153,155,213, email_preop_chk_inc.jsp:256,258, email_seat_hour_util.jsp:115,213, email_serv_maintenance.jsp:193, email_shock_summary.jsp:63, email_unit_unlock.jsp:220, email_unit_unlock_impact.jsp:207

### P66-F08: Mixed string concatenation patterns for building messages
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Different files use different patterns for building display strings. Some use concatenation with variables (form_nm + " - " + lockoutype), others use separate display logic. This creates inconsistency in how report titles and filters are displayed.
**Fix:** Standardize on a single pattern for building display strings, preferably using String.format() or StringBuilder for complex concatenations to improve readability and maintainability.
**File:** reports/email_unit_unlock.jsp
**Line(s):** 183

### P66-F09: Redundant conditional assignment in model_cd default logic
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** In email_restricted_access_usage_report.jsp and email_super_master_auth_report.jsp, there is a redundant if-block that checks if model_cd.equals("all") and then assigns it to "all" again (lines 71-76 and 71-74 respectively). This is a no-op statement.
**Fix:** Remove the redundant conditional block that reassigns model_cd to "all" when it already equals "all".
**File:** reports/email_restricted_access_usage_report.jsp, reports/email_super_master_auth_report.jsp
**Line(s):** email_restricted_access_usage_report.jsp:73-76, email_super_master_auth_report.jsp:71-74

### P66-F10: Inconsistent import statement ordering
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Import statements and page directives appear in different orders across files. Some files have imports before jsp:useBean, others have them after, and some mix @ page imports with regular JSP directives.
**Fix:** Standardize the order of directives: @ page imports first, then @ include directives, then jsp:useBean declarations, for consistent file structure across all JSP files.
**File:** All JSP files
**Line(s):** email_preop_chk_detailed.jsp:1-7, email_preop_chk_fail.jsp:1-7, email_preop_chk_inc.jsp:1-8, email_restricted_access_usage_report.jsp:1-6, email_seat_hour_util.jsp:1-4, email_serv_maintenance.jsp:1-5, email_shock_summary.jsp:1-4, email_super_master_auth_report.jsp:1-5, email_unit_unlock.jsp:1-6, email_unit_unlock_impact.jsp:1-6

### P66-F11: Inconsistent naming convention for boolean show variable
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** In email_preop_chk_inc.jsp, a boolean variable is named "show" (line 237) while most other variables use underscore-separated naming. This breaks the naming convention used throughout the codebase.
**Fix:** Rename the variable to follow the underscore naming convention used elsewhere, e.g., "show_row" or use camelCase consistently if that's the preferred style.
**File:** reports/email_preop_chk_inc.jsp
**Line(s):** 237-250

### P66-F12: Hardcoded string literals for site name comparison
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Site name comparisons use hardcoded string literals "AU" and "MLA" directly in conditional statements (LindeConfig.siteName.equalsIgnoreCase("AU") || LindeConfig.siteName.equalsIgnoreCase("MLA")). These appear multiple times and should be centralized.
**Fix:** Define site name constants in a configuration class or enum to avoid hardcoded string literals and enable easier maintenance if site codes change.
**File:** reports/email_preop_chk_fail.jsp, reports/email_preop_chk_inc.jsp
**Line(s):** email_preop_chk_fail.jsp:125,165, email_preop_chk_inc.jsp:220,268

### P66-F13: Unused variable 'q' for questions concatenation
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** In email_preop_chk_detailed.jsp, variable 'q' is created by concatenating question strings with "* " prefix (line 159) but is only used in a hidden table cell (line 164 with style="display:none;"). This suggests dead code or debugging leftover.
**Fix:** If the hidden cell serves no purpose, remove both the variable 'q' and the hidden cell. If it's used by JavaScript, add a comment explaining its purpose.
**File:** reports/email_preop_chk_detailed.jsp
**Line(s):** 157-164

### P66-F14: Duplicate logic for filter display string determination
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** email_unit_unlock.jsp and email_unit_unlock_impact.jsp both contain nearly identical code blocks for determining optFilter display string based on rptFilter parameter (lines 79-87 and 65-73 respectively). The logic is duplicated rather than shared.
**Fix:** Extract the filter display logic into a utility method or scriptlet include file to eliminate duplication.
**File:** reports/email_unit_unlock.jsp, reports/email_unit_unlock_impact.jsp
**Line(s):** email_unit_unlock.jsp:79-87, email_unit_unlock_impact.jsp:65-73

### P66-F15: Inconsistent session attribute retrieval patterns
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Some files retrieve session attributes using request.getSession().getAttribute() (lines 82, 91-94 in email_restricted_access_usage_report.jsp) while others use session.getAttribute() directly (line 83). This inconsistency suggests unclear understanding of when session object is available.
**Fix:** Standardize on using the implicit 'session' object directly since it's always available in JSP pages, or document why request.getSession() is needed in specific cases.
**File:** reports/email_preop_chk_fail.jsp, reports/email_restricted_access_usage_report.jsp, reports/email_super_master_auth_report.jsp
**Line(s):** email_preop_chk_fail.jsp:91-94, email_restricted_access_usage_report.jsp:82-83, email_super_master_auth_report.jsp:80-82

### P66-F16: Missing column header for Super Master Code
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** In email_super_master_auth_report.jsp line 145, the table header shows "<fmt:message key="global.supermastercode"/>" but the corresponding data cell at line 165 only shows custName, not the super master code value. There appears to be a mismatch between column header and data.
**Fix:** Verify if the column header is correct or if the data being displayed in that column is the intended value. Update either the header label or the data field to match.
**File:** reports/email_super_master_auth_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 145, 159, 165

### P66-F17: Inconsistent approach to "total" row colspan calculation
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Different files use different colspan values for their total/summary rows. Some use fixed numbers (colspan=7, colspan=8), others dynamically calculate based on whether optional columns are shown. This creates inconsistency and potential layout breaks if columns change.
**Fix:** Standardize the approach to calculating colspan for summary rows, preferably using a calculated value or variable that adjusts based on actual column count.
**File:** reports/email_preop_chk_detailed.jsp, reports/email_preop_chk_fail.jsp, reports/email_preop_chk_inc.jsp, reports/email_seat_hour_util.jsp
**Line(s):** email_preop_chk_detailed.jsp:181, email_preop_chk_fail.jsp:204, email_preop_chk_inc.jsp:304, email_seat_hour_util.jsp:149

### P66-F18: Variable 'failed' used only for hidden cell
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** In email_preop_chk_fail.jsp and email_preop_chk_inc.jsp, variable 'failed' is built by concatenating failure strings but only used in a hidden table cell (style="display:none;"). Similar to finding F13, this appears to be dead code or debugging leftover.
**Fix:** Remove the 'failed' variable and hidden cell if they serve no purpose, or document their JavaScript usage if needed.
**File:** reports/email_preop_chk_fail.jsp, reports/email_preop_chk_inc.jsp
**Line(s):** email_preop_chk_fail.jsp:178-193, email_preop_chk_inc.jsp:281-295

### P66-F19: Incomplete or misleading message key usage
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** In email_preop_chk_fail.jsp line 126, there's a malformed message key with hardcoded "Driver I" prefix: "Driver I<fmt:message key="global.driverid"/>". This suggests incomplete refactoring or incorrect message key usage.
**Fix:** Either remove the hardcoded "Driver I" prefix and use the complete message key, or verify that this concatenation is intentional and document why.
**File:** reports/email_preop_chk_fail.jsp
**Line(s):** 126

### P66-F20: Inconsistent typo in "A" prefix before message key
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** In email_preop_chk_fail.jsp line 204, there's a stray "A" before the message key: "A<fmt:message key="global.averageinittime"/>". This appears to be a typo or incomplete edit.
**Fix:** Remove the stray "A" character before the message key.
**File:** reports/email_preop_chk_fail.jsp
**Line(s):** 204

### P66-F21: Complex nested logic for worst driver/vehicle selection
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** In email_shock_summary.jsp lines 75-83 and 92-99, there's complex nested conditional logic to determine which element from the list to display for "worst" entries. The logic checks for usize>10, usize<=10 && usize > i+5, with multiple similar conditions. This is hard to understand and maintain.
**Fix:** Simplify the logic by calculating the "worst" index once before the loop: int worstUserIndex = Math.max(0, usize - i - 1) and use a helper method to determine if the index is valid.
**File:** reports/email_shock_summary.jsp
**Line(s):** 75-83, 92-99

### P66-F22: @page import directives placed after scriptlets
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** In email_unit_unlock.jsp and email_unit_unlock_impact.jsp, @page import directives appear at lines 94-95 and 77-78 respectively, after the main scriptlet blocks. Page directives should appear at the top of the file before any scriptlets.
**Fix:** Move the @page import statements to the top of the file with other page directives.
**File:** reports/email_unit_unlock.jsp, reports/email_unit_unlock_impact.jsp
**Line(s):** email_unit_unlock.jsp:94-95, email_unit_unlock_impact.jsp:77-78

### P66-F23: Missing else-if causes conditional logic error
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** In email_unit_unlock.jsp and email_unit_unlock_impact.jsp, line 85 and 71 respectively, there's a missing 'else' before the second 'if' statement, causing the third condition to be evaluated even when the second is true.
**Fix:** Change "if( rptFilter.equalsIgnoreCase("both"))" to "else if( rptFilter.equalsIgnoreCase("both"))" to ensure proper conditional logic flow.
**File:** reports/email_unit_unlock.jsp, reports/email_unit_unlock_impact.jsp
**Line(s):** email_unit_unlock.jsp:85, email_unit_unlock_impact.jsp:71

- **StringTokenizer usage:** Lines 76-92 for parsing comma-delimited do_list

### reports/email_vor_unit_report.jsp
- **Scriptlet lines 5-54:** Simplified parameter extraction, access level setup, filter configuration with op_code="vor_unit_report", data retrieval (4 ArrayLists only)
- **Scriptlet lines 77-92:** Simple loop displaying vorUnitHireNo, vorUnitModel, vorUnitSerialNo, vorUnitDateEnabled

### reports/excel_battery.jsp
- **Lines 3-4:** Duplicate Content-disposition headers (inline and attachment)
- **Scriptlet lines 8-90:** Parameter extraction, response.setContentType on line 21, access level retrieved from SESSION (different pattern - lines 28-31), filter configuration with op_code="rpt_battery", data retrieval

### reports/excel_battery_change.jsp
- **Lines 5-6:** Duplicate Content-disposition headers (inline and attachment)
- **Scriptlet lines 10-60:** Parameter extraction, access level from SESSION, filter configuration with op_code="battery_change_rpt", data retrieval using ArrayList<BatteryBean>
- **Scriptlet lines 101-130:** Loop displaying battery change data with conditional unit display based on isTruck1120() (lines 113, 123 show "%" or " minutes")

- **Includes:** report_export_header.jsp, report_excel.jsp

## Findings

### P67-F01: Dead Code - Hardcoded Access Level Always True
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Access level is hardcoded to "1" on lines 19/23, then checked if empty on lines 28/23-26. Since the value is hardcoded, the condition `if(access_level.equalsIgnoreCase(""))` will never be true. This creates unreachable code (lines 25/30 setting access_level = "5") and misleading logic that suggests dynamic access control when it's actually static.
**Fix:** Either remove the hardcoded assignment and retrieve access_level from session/request, OR remove the dead conditional check entirely if hardcoding is intentional. Recommend retrieving from session like excel_battery.jsp does.
**File:** reports/email_unit_unlock_question.jsp
**Line(s):** 19, 23-31

### P67-F02: Dead Code - Hardcoded Access Level Always True
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Same issue as F01. Access level is hardcoded to "1", making the empty string check always false and the fallback assignment unreachable.
**Fix:** Remove hardcoded assignment and retrieve access_level from session, OR remove the dead conditional check.
**File:** reports/email_users_login_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 19, 23-27

### P67-F03: Dead Code - Hardcoded Access Level Always True
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Same issue as F01. Access level is hardcoded to "1", making the empty string check always false.
**Fix:** Remove hardcoded assignment and retrieve access_level from session, OR remove the dead conditional check.
**File:** reports/email_util_wow.jsp
**Line(s):** 47, 51-55

**Fix:** Remove hardcoded assignment and retrieve access_level from session, OR remove the dead conditional check.

**Fix:** Remove the line `int al = Integer.parseInt(access_level);` as the variable is never referenced.
**File:** reports/email_unit_unlock_question.jsp
**Line(s):** 32

### P67-F17: Dead Code - Unused Variables
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variables `dsum_driver_cd` and `dsum_driver_nm` are retrieved from filter bean but never used anywhere in the display logic. Based on the naming pattern (should be vehicle-related for this report), this appears to be copy-paste code from another file.
**Fix:** Remove lines 36-37: `ArrayList dsum_driver_cd = filter.getDsum_veh_cd();` and `ArrayList dsum_driver_nm = filter.getDsum_veh_nm();`
**File:** reports/email_util_summary.jsp
**Line(s):** 36-37

### P67-F18: Dead Code - Unused Variable
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variable `j` is incremented in the loop (line 148) but never used. The loop index `i` is sufficient for the iteration.
**Fix:** Remove the `j` variable declaration and increment statement: `for (int i = 0, j = 0; i < arrUnitUtil.size(); i++) { ... j++; }`
**File:** reports/email_utilisation.jsp
**Line(s):** 116, 148

### P67-F19: Dead Code - Empty HTML Element
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** An empty table row `<tr></tr>` serves no purpose and adds unnecessary markup to the DOM.
**Fix:** Remove line 75-76: `<tr></tr>`
**File:** reports/email_vor_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 75-76

### P67-F20: Commented-Out Code
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Response header code is commented out (lines 41-43). Commented code should either be removed or reinstated. If this functionality is needed for Excel export, it should be active; otherwise it creates clutter and confusion about intended behavior.
**Fix:** Either remove the commented lines entirely, or uncomment if the functionality is needed for proper Excel file handling.
**File:** reports/email_util_wow.jsp
**Line(s):** 41-43

### P67-F21: Commented-Out Code
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Response header code is commented out (lines 41-43). Same issue as F20.
**Fix:** Either remove the commented lines entirely, or uncomment if the functionality is needed.
**File:** reports/email_util_wow_pivot.jsp
**Line(s):** 41-43

### P67-F22: Style Inconsistency - Hardcoded Access Level Mapping
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Access level codes (1-5) are mapped to display names using a series of if-else statements with hardcoded strings (lines 152-171). This business logic should be externalized to a constants class, enum, or configuration file for maintainability. The same mapping logic would need to be duplicated across multiple reports.
**Fix:** Create an AccessLevel enum or constants class with a method like `getDisplayName(String code)`, then replace the if-else chain with a single method call.
**File:** reports/email_users_login_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 152-171

### P67-F23: Style Inconsistency - Hardcoded URLs
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Image URLs are hardcoded to "http://fms.fleetiq360.com/" in multiple locations (lines 78, 118). This creates maintenance issues if the domain changes and prevents environment-specific configuration (dev/staging/prod). Other files use SessionVar.reportLogo and LindeConfig references which are the correct pattern.
**Fix:** Replace hardcoded URLs with configuration properties similar to SessionVar.reportLogo or LindeConfig.reportHead used elsewhere in the same file.
**File:** reports/email_users_login_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 78, 118

### P67-F24: Style Inconsistency - Hardcoded URLs
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Multiple hardcoded URLs to "http://fms.fleetiq360.com/" (lines 13, 18, 148, 199, 240). Same issue as F23.
**Fix:** Replace hardcoded URLs with configuration properties.
**File:** reports/email_util_wow.jsp
**Line(s):** 13, 18, 148, 199, 240

### P67-F25: Style Inconsistency - Hardcoded URLs
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Hardcoded URLs to "http://fms.fleetiq360.com/" (lines 8, 13). Same issue as F23.
**Fix:** Replace hardcoded URLs with configuration properties.
**File:** reports/email_util_wow_pivot.jsp
**Line(s):** 8, 13

### P67-F26: Style Inconsistency - Mixed Access Control Patterns
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** This file has NO access level control code, while all other email report JSPs include access level setup (hardcoded or from session). This inconsistency suggests either missing security controls or intentional design that's not documented.
**Fix:** Add consistent access level control matching other reports, or document why this report doesn't require access control.
**File:** reports/email_util_summary.jsp
**Line(s):** N/A (missing code)

### P67-F27: Style Inconsistency - Mixed Access Control Patterns
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Excel reports (excel_battery.jsp, excel_battery_change.jsp) retrieve access_level from session attributes, while email reports hardcode it to "1". This inconsistency indicates different security models for the same application and creates maintenance confusion.
**Fix:** Standardize access control across all reports - either all should use session attributes or all should use a consistent service/method to retrieve access context.
**File:** reports/excel_battery.jsp, reports/excel_battery_change.jsp
**Line(s):** excel_battery.jsp:28-31, excel_battery_change.jsp:31-34

### P67-F28: Style Inconsistency - Malformed HTML
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Inline style attribute contains double quotes within double quotes causing malformed HTML: `style="..."" >` (note the double quote before the closing bracket). This should use single quotes or proper escaping.
**Fix:** Change to `style="..." >` (remove extra quote) or use proper quote escaping.
**File:** reports/email_unit_unlock_question.jsp
**Line(s):** 90

### P67-F29: Style Inconsistency - Malformed HTML
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Same malformed HTML issue - double quotes in style attribute: `style=""">`
**Fix:** Remove the extra double quote before the closing bracket.
**File:** reports/email_util_summary.jsp
**Line(s):** 63

### P67-F30: Style Inconsistency - Typo in JSP Tag
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Extra character "s" after closing JSP useBean tag: `</jsp:useBean>s`. This is malformed JSP syntax that may cause parser issues.
**Fix:** Remove the trailing "s": `</jsp:useBean>`
**File:** reports/email_util_wow.jsp
**Line(s):** 7

### P67-F31: Style Inconsistency - Variable Name Typo
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Parameter name has typo "condf_valaue" instead of "condf_value" (missing 'u', extra 'a'). While this won't break functionality if used consistently, it indicates a spelling error that reduces code quality.
**Fix:** Rename variable to correct spelling: `String condf_value = request.getParameter("condf_value")==null?"10":request.getParameter("condf_value");`
**File:** reports/email_util_wow_pivot.jsp
**Line(s):** 39

### P67-F32: Style Inconsistency - Possible Display Error
**Severity:** INFO
**Description:** Line 111 has "T" character before fmt:message tag: `<th class="tbl-report-col">T<fmt:message key="global.tractionhourspercent"/>`. This appears to be unintentional and would display as "T..." instead of just the message.
**Fix:** Remove the "T" character if unintentional: `<th class="tbl-report-col"><fmt:message key="global.tractionhourspercent"/>`
**File:** reports/email_utilisation.jsp
**Line(s):** 111

### P67-F33: Style Inconsistency - Possible Display Error
**Severity:** INFO
**Description:** Line 112 has extra "s" after closing tag: `<fmt:message key="global.hydraulichours"/>s`. This would display as "...s" with a trailing 's' character.
**Fix:** Remove the trailing "s": `<fmt:message key="global.hydraulichours"/>`
**File:** reports/email_utilisation.jsp
**Line(s):** 112

### P67-F34: Style Inconsistency - Hardcoded Text Instead of i18n
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** The word "Note:" is hardcoded in English instead of using fmt:message tag for internationalization. Other parts of the same line use `<fmt:message key="global.note"/>` properly.
**Fix:** Replace hardcoded "Note:" with `<fmt:message key="global.note"/>` to match the pattern used in other files.
**File:** reports/email_vor_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 101

### P67-F35: Style Inconsistency - Duplicate Content-Disposition Headers
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Two Content-disposition headers are set with conflicting values - one as "inline" and one as "attachment" (lines 3-4). Only the second one will take effect, making the first line dead code. This creates confusion about intended browser behavior.
**Fix:** Remove one of the duplicate headers. Typically "attachment" is correct for Excel file downloads.
**File:** reports/excel_battery.jsp
**Line(s):** 3-4

### P67-F36: Style Inconsistency - Duplicate Content-Disposition Headers
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Same issue as F35 - duplicate Content-disposition headers with conflicting values.
**Fix:** Remove the "inline" header on line 5, keep only the "attachment" header.
**File:** reports/excel_battery_change.jsp
**Line(s):** 5-6

### P67-F37: Style Inconsistency - Empty Tag
**Severity:** INFO
**Description:** Line 119 has an empty bold closing tag `<b></b>` that serves no purpose.
**Fix:** Remove the empty `<b>` tag.
**File:** reports/excel_battery.jsp
**Line(s):** 119

### P67-F38: Leaky Abstraction - Potential NPE on Session Attribute
**Severity:** HIGH
**Description:** Access level is retrieved from session attribute with `.equalsIgnoreCase("")` check on line 33, but if the session attribute is null, this will throw NullPointerException. The code assumes the attribute exists, exposing internal session management details without null safety.
**Fix:** Add null check before .equalsIgnoreCase(): `if(access_level == null || access_level.equalsIgnoreCase("")) { access_level = "5"; }`
**File:** reports/excel_battery.jsp
**Line(s):** 28, 33

### P67-F39: Leaky Abstraction - Potential NPE on Session Attribute
**Severity:** HIGH
**Description:** Same issue as F38 - access_level retrieved from session without null check before calling .equalsIgnoreCase().
**Fix:** Add null check: `if(access_level == null || access_level.equalsIgnoreCase(""))`
**File:** reports/excel_battery_change.jsp
**Line(s):** 31, 36

### P67-F40: Leaky Abstraction - RuntimeConf Direct Access
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Line 19 directly accesses RuntimeConf static fields (question_lockout, c_question_lockout, s_question_lockout) without null checks or error handling. This creates tight coupling to the RuntimeConf implementation and could cause NullPointerException if the configuration is not initialized.
**Fix:** Add null checks or use a configuration service with proper error handling: `RuntimeConf.getQuestionLockout()` with fallback values.
**File:** reports/email_unit_unlock_question.jsp
**Line(s):** 19

---

## Summary

**Total Findings:** 40
- **CRITICAL:** 0
- **HIGH:** 2 (NPE risks on session attributes)
- **MEDIUM:** 11 (dead code blocks, commented code, inconsistent patterns, missing access control)
- **LOW:** 24 (unused variables, style issues, typos, hardcoded values)
- **INFO:** 3 (minor display/formatting issues)

**Key Issues:**
1. Systematic dead code pattern across 7 files with hardcoded access_level="1" followed by unreachable conditional
2. Unused variable `al` in all 10 files
3. Inconsistent access control patterns (hardcoded vs session-based)
4. Commented-out code in 2 files
5. Potential NPE vulnerabilities in 2 Excel export files
6. Hardcoded URLs and configuration values instead of using centralized config
# Agent P68 — Pass 4 Code Quality Audit
**Files reviewed:**
- reports/excel_battery_charge.jsp
- reports/excel_blacklist_driv.jsp
- reports/excel_broadcastmsg.jsp
- reports/excel_curr_driv_report.jsp
- reports/excel_curr_unit_report.jsp
- reports/excel_daily_driver_summary.jsp
- reports/excel_daily_veh_summary.jsp
- reports/excel_driver_access_abuse.jsp
- reports/excel_driver_league.jsp
- reports/excel_driver_licence_expiry_detail.jsp

**Branch verified:** multi-customer-sync-to-master

## Findings

### P68-F01: Duplicate Content-Disposition headers
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Multiple files set Content-Disposition header twice with conflicting values (inline and attachment). The second header overwrites the first, making the first header redundant. This pattern appears in all files.
**Fix:** Remove the first "inline" header declaration and keep only the "attachment" declaration. For example, change lines 5-6 in excel_battery_charge.jsp from two setHeader calls to a single call: `response.setHeader("Content-disposition","attachment; filename=battery_charge.xls");`
**File:** reports/excel_battery_charge.jsp
**Line(s):** 5-6

### P68-F02: Duplicate Content-Disposition headers
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Duplicate Content-Disposition headers with conflicting values (inline vs attachment).
**Fix:** Remove the first "inline" header declaration and keep only the "attachment" declaration.
**File:** reports/excel_blacklist_driv.jsp
**Line(s):** 11-12

### P68-F11: Malformed URL protocol in link tag
**Severity:** HIGH
**Description:** CSS link href uses "ttp://" instead of "http://" or "https://", which will cause the stylesheet to fail to load.
**Fix:** Change "ttp://fms.fleetiq360.com/css/linde.css" to "http://fms.fleetiq360.com/css/linde.css" (or preferably "https://")
**File:** reports/excel_blacklist_driv.jsp
**Line(s):** 8

### P68-F12: Malformed URL protocol in link tag
**Severity:** HIGH
**Description:** CSS link href uses "ttp://" instead of "http://" or "https://", which will cause the stylesheet to fail to load.
**Fix:** Change "ttp://fms.fleetiq360.com/css/reportTables1.css" to "http://fms.fleetiq360.com/css/reportTables1.css" (or preferably "https://")
**File:** reports/excel_blacklist_driv.jsp
**Line(s):** 9

### P68-F13: Large block of commented-out code
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Lines 61-77 contain commented-out ArrayList declarations and variable assignments related to operational hours, standby hours, hydraulic hours, and their totals. This dead code clutters the file and creates confusion about which fields are actually used.
**Fix:** Remove the commented-out code if it's no longer needed, or reinstate it if it's required. Keeping commented code in version control is not a best practice - use git history to retrieve old code if needed.
**File:** reports/excel_daily_driver_summary.jsp
**Line(s):** 61-77

### P68-F14: Commented-out table header code
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Lines 76-77 and 82-83 contain commented-out table cells for "Driver Id" and "Model" columns. This suggests incomplete refactoring or uncertainty about the data model.
**Fix:** Remove commented-out HTML if these columns are no longer needed, or reinstate them if they should be displayed.
**File:** reports/excel_driver_access_abuse.jsp
**Line(s):** 76-77, 82-83, 113-114

### P68-F15: Inconsistent variable naming convention
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variable names use inconsistent prefixes: some ArrayLists use lowercase "v" prefix (vveh_cd, vdriv_nm), while others use uppercase "V" prefix (Vloc_cd, Vuser_cd). This mixing of naming conventions within the same file reduces code readability.
**Fix:** Standardize on a single naming convention. Recommend using lowercase for all variable names (e.g., change "Vloc_cd" to "vloc_cd", "Vuser_cd" to "vuser_cd").
**File:** reports/excel_blacklist_driv.jsp
**Line(s):** 58-68

### P68-F16: Inconsistent variable naming convention
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variable names mix lowercase "v" prefix (vcust_cd, vveh_cd, vdriv_nm, vdept_cd) with uppercase "V" prefix (Vloc_cd, Vloc_nm). This inconsistency makes the code harder to read and maintain.
**Fix:** Standardize on lowercase for all ArrayList variable names throughout the file.
**File:** reports/excel_curr_driv_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 67-101

### P68-F17: Inconsistent variable naming convention
**Severity:** LOW

### P68-F35: Inconsistent hardcoded department label
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Line 156 contains hardcoded text "Dept." instead of using an i18n message key like other column headers in the same table. This breaks internationalization consistency.
**Fix:** Replace hardcoded "Dept." with `<fmt:message key="global.department"/>` to match the pattern used in other parts of the file.
**File:** reports/excel_curr_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 156

### P68-F36: Hardcoded string instead of i18n key
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Line 137 uses hardcoded "Total:" instead of using the i18n message key. Line 145 shows the correct pattern using `<fmt:message key="global.total"/>`.
**Fix:** Replace `<b>Total:</b>` with `<b><fmt:message key="global.total"/></b>` to maintain i18n consistency.
**File:** reports/excel_daily_driver_summary.jsp
**Line(s):** 137

### P68-F37: Inconsistent null/empty string handling
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** The file uses repeated if-statements to check if each field equals empty string and replace with "-". This pattern is verbose and inconsistent with other files that may use different approaches. Lines 92-125 show this pattern repeated for each field.
**Fix:** Create a helper method to handle null/empty string replacement, e.g., `displayValue(String value)` that returns "-" if empty, otherwise the value. This would reduce code duplication.
**File:** reports/excel_driver_access_abuse.jsp
**Line(s):** 92-125

### P68-F38: Raw type usage on ArrayList
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Multiple ArrayList declarations use raw types without generic type parameters (e.g., `ArrayList Vuser_cd = filter.getUser_cd();`). This causes unchecked type warnings during compilation and reduces type safety.
**Fix:** Add generic type parameters to all ArrayList declarations. Example: change `ArrayList Vuser_cd` to `ArrayList<String> Vuser_cd` (or appropriate type).
**File:** reports/excel_blacklist_driv.jsp
**Line(s):** 58-68

### P68-F39: Raw type usage on ArrayList
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** ArrayList declarations use raw types without generic type parameters, causing compiler warnings.
**Fix:** Add generic type parameters to all ArrayList declarations, e.g., `ArrayList<String> vcust_cd = filter.getCust_cd();`
**File:** reports/excel_curr_driv_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 67-101

**Description:** The onload attribute contains incorrect JavaScript syntax: `print('document')`.
**Fix:** Change to `onload="window.print();"`.
**File:** reports/excel_daily_veh_summary.jsp
**Line(s):** 71

### P68-F52: Inconsistent inline onload attribute

### P68-F54: Empty onload attribute
**Severity:** INFO
**Description:** The body tag has an empty onload attribute (`onload=""`), which serves no purpose and should be removed.
**Fix:** Either remove the onload attribute entirely, or add the print functionality if needed: `onload="window.print();"`.
**File:** reports/excel_blacklist_driv.jsp
**Line(s):** 75

### P68-F55: Style inconsistency - inline CSS in conditional expression
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Lines 70-71 embed conditional CSS color selection based on LindeConfig.siteName directly in the style attribute. This mixes business logic with presentation and makes the code harder to maintain.
**Fix:** Move the color logic to a scriptlet variable at the top of the page, then reference that variable in the style attribute. Example: `<% String borderColor = LindeConfig.siteName.equalsIgnoreCase("hiremech") ? "#ddd" : "#0E76AF"; %>` then use `style="border-bottom:1px solid <%=borderColor %>"`.
**File:** reports/excel_battery_charge.jsp
**Line(s):** 70-71

### P68-F56: Inconsistent conditional display patterns
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** The file uses conditional JSP blocks (lines 128-132, 136-140, 155-157, 170-172) to control display of content based on `get_dep.equalsIgnoreCase("all")` and `get_loc.equalsIgnoreCase("all")`. Some conditionals wrap entire table cells while others are inline, creating inconsistent patterns.
**Fix:** Standardize the conditional display pattern. Use consistent block structure for all conditional elements.
**File:** reports/excel_curr_driv_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 128-132, 136-140, 155-157, 170-172

### P68-F57: Potentially null session attributes without null check
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Session attributes (access_level, access_cust, access_site, access_dept) are retrieved and cast to String without null checks before calling methods on them. While line 36-39 checks if access_level is empty, a NullPointerException would occur if the attribute doesn't exist in the session. This pattern is repeated across all files.
**Fix:** Add null checks before the equalsIgnoreCase() call: `if(access_level == null || access_level.equalsIgnoreCase(""))`. Alternatively, use Objects.toString(session.getAttribute("access_level"), "") to safely handle null values.
**File:** reports/excel_battery_charge.jsp
**Line(s):** 31-36

### P68-F58: Potentially null session attributes without null check
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Session attributes are cast to String without null checks before method calls.
**Fix:** Add null checks: `if(access_level == null || access_level.equalsIgnoreCase(""))` or use `Objects.toString()` for safe null handling.
**File:** reports/excel_blacklist_driv.jsp
**Line(s):** 32-37

**Description:** Session attributes are cast to String without null checks before method calls.

### P68-F65: Missing conditional check before ArrayList access
**Severity:** HIGH
**Description:** Line 124 accesses `driverLeagueBeans.get(0).getTruckType()` without first checking if the ArrayList is non-empty. If there are no driver league records, this will throw an IndexOutOfBoundsException.
**Fix:** Add a null/empty check before accessing index 0: `<%=driverLeagueBeans != null && !driverLeagueBeans.isEmpty() ? driverLeagueBeans.get(0).getTruckType() : "N/A" %>`. Also apply the same fix to line 149.
**File:** reports/excel_driver_league.jsp
**Line(s):** 124, 149

### P68-F66: Inconsistent use of deprecated script language attribute
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Script tags use the deprecated "language" attribute instead of the "type" attribute. Modern HTML5 doesn't require either, but "type" is the standard if specified.
**Fix:** Remove the `language="JavaScript"` and `language="javascript"` attributes, or replace with `type="text/javascript"` if compatibility with older browsers is needed. Modern browsers assume JavaScript by default.
**File:** reports/excel_curr_driv_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 9-13

### P68-F67: Inconsistent use of deprecated script language attribute
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Script tags use the deprecated "language" attribute.
**Fix:** Remove the `language` attributes or replace with `type="text/javascript"`.
**File:** reports/excel_curr_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 9-13
