## Findings

### P73-F01: Duplicate AJAX code across multiple files
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** The JavaScript functions fetch_data(), stateChanged(), and GetXmlHttpObject() are duplicated identically across mail_conf.jsp, mail_conf_au.jsp, mail_conf_monthly.jsp, and mail_first.jsp. This violates the DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself) principle and increases maintenance burden - any bug fix or improvement must be applied to all four files.
**Fix:** Extract the common AJAX functions into a shared JavaScript file (e.g., mail_ajax.js) and include it via <script src="../js/mail_ajax.js"></script> in each JSP that needs it. This centralizes the code and ensures consistency.
**File:** reports/mail_conf.jsp, reports/mail_conf_au.jsp, reports/mail_conf_monthly.jsp, reports/mail_first.jsp
**Line(s):** mail_conf.jsp: 3-67, mail_conf_au.jsp: 3-67, mail_conf_monthly.jsp: 3-67, mail_first.jsp: 39-98

### P73-F02: Inconsistent empty string check pattern
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Throughout the codebase, there are inconsistent patterns for checking empty strings. Some use .equalsIgnoreCase("") (mail_conf.jsp line 136), some use .equals("") (print_battery.jsp lines 59, 63, 67), and some use inline ternary operators with null checks (hire_vehicle_lst1.jsp line 24: request.getParameter("cust_cd")==null?"":"..."). This inconsistency makes the code harder to read and maintain.
**Fix:** Standardize on a single approach, preferably using a utility method like StringUtils.isEmpty() or creating a custom isEmpty() method that handles both null and empty string checks. Apply consistently across all files.
**File:** All reviewed files
**Line(s):** hire_vehicle_lst1.jsp: 24; mail_conf.jsp: 121-126, 136; mail_conf_au.jsp: 121-126, 135; mail_conf_monthly.jsp: 160-167, 176; mail_first.jsp: 11; mail_report.jsp: 17; print_battery.jsp: 11-15, 18, 59, 63, 67; print_battery_change.jsp: 12-18

### P73-F03: Dead code - unused variable 'xmlDoc'
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** In the stateChanged() JavaScript function across multiple files, the variable xmlDoc is declared and assigned (var xmlDoc=xmlHttp.responseXML.documentElement;) but never used. The code only uses xmlHttp.responseXML.getElementsByTagName("body")[0]. This is dead code that should be removed.
**Fix:** Remove the line "var xmlDoc=xmlHttp.responseXML.documentElement;" from the stateChanged() function in all affected files.
**File:** reports/mail_conf.jsp, reports/mail_conf_au.jsp, reports/mail_conf_monthly.jsp, reports/mail_first.jsp
**Line(s):** mail_conf.jsp: 24; mail_conf_au.jsp: 24; mail_conf_monthly.jsp: 24; mail_first.jsp: 61

### P73-F04: Dead code - unused variable 'flag' always true
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** In the dele() JavaScript function, a variable 'flag' is initialized to true (line 101) and then checked (line 103), but it is never set to false. This makes the conditional check "if(flag)" meaningless - the code inside will always execute. This is dead code resulting from incomplete logic.
**Fix:** Either remove the flag variable and the if statement (if the check is not needed), or implement the actual validation logic that should set flag to false under certain conditions.
**File:** reports/mail_conf.jsp, reports/mail_conf_au.jsp, reports/mail_conf_monthly.jsp
**Line(s):** mail_conf.jsp: 101-103; mail_conf_au.jsp: 101-103; mail_conf_monthly.jsp: 140-142

### P73-F05: Deprecated DOCTYPE declaration
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** hire_vehicle_lst1.jsp uses an XHTML 1.0 Transitional DOCTYPE (line 3), while nothire_vehicle_lst.jsp uses HTML 4.01 Transitional (line 3). These are outdated HTML versions. Modern web applications should use HTML5 for better browser compatibility and features.
**Fix:** Replace the DOCTYPE declarations with the modern HTML5 DOCTYPE: <!DOCTYPE html>. Remove the xmlns attribute from the <html> tag as it's not needed in HTML5.
**File:** reports/hire_vehicle_lst1.jsp, reports/nothire_vehicle_lst.jsp
**Line(s):** hire_vehicle_lst1.jsp: 3-4; nothire_vehicle_lst.jsp: 3-4

### P73-F06: Deprecated language attribute in script tags
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Several files use the deprecated language="JavaScript" attribute on <script> tags (e.g., hire_vehicle_lst1.jsp lines 9-13, mail_first.jsp line 36). The language attribute was deprecated in HTML 4 and should not be used. Modern browsers default to JavaScript, or the type="text/javascript" attribute should be used (though even type is optional in HTML5).
**Fix:** Remove all language="JavaScript" and language="javascript" attributes from <script> tags. If needed for older browsers, use type="text/javascript" instead, though in HTML5 the type attribute can be omitted entirely.
**File:** reports/hire_vehicle_lst1.jsp, reports/mail_first.jsp, reports/nothire_vehicle_lst.jsp
**Line(s):** hire_vehicle_lst1.jsp: 9-13; mail_first.jsp: 36; nothire_vehicle_lst.jsp: 8

### P73-F07: Inconsistent null handling for optional strings
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** The code uses inconsistent patterns for handling null/empty strings from database results. hire_vehicle_lst1.jsp (lines 75-76) checks if tmp equals "null" as a string (tmp.equalsIgnoreCase("null")), while nothire_vehicle_lst.jsp (lines 85-86) uses the same pattern. This suggests the database might be returning the string "null" instead of actual null values, which is a leaky abstraction where database implementation details are bleeding into presentation logic.
**Fix:** Fix the root cause by ensuring the database/DAO layer returns proper null values instead of the string "null". In the JSP, use standard null checks (tmp == null || tmp.isEmpty()) rather than checking for the string "null". Implement this consistently across all files.
**File:** reports/hire_vehicle_lst1.jsp, reports/nothire_vehicle_lst.jsp
**Line(s):** hire_vehicle_lst1.jsp: 75-76; nothire_vehicle_lst.jsp: 85-86

### P73-F08: Hardcoded access level default value
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Multiple files contain the hardcoded string "5" as a default access level (e.g., mail_conf.jsp line 138, mail_conf_au.jsp line 137, mail_conf_monthly.jsp line 178, print_battery.jsp line 29, print_battery_change.jsp line 31). Magic numbers should be avoided, especially for access control. The value "5" has no clear meaning without context.
**Fix:** Define access level constants in a dedicated class (e.g., AccessLevel.DEFAULT = "5" or better yet, AccessLevel.GUEST = 5 as an integer) and reference the constant throughout the codebase. Add documentation explaining what each access level means.
**File:** reports/mail_conf.jsp, reports/mail_conf_au.jsp, reports/mail_conf_monthly.jsp, reports/mail_first.jsp, reports/print_battery.jsp, reports/print_battery_change.jsp
**Line(s):** mail_conf.jsp: 138; mail_conf_au.jsp: 137; mail_conf_monthly.jsp: 178; mail_first.jsp: 21; print_battery.jsp: 29; print_battery_change.jsp: 31

### P73-F09: Inconsistent op_code naming convention
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Operation codes use inconsistent naming patterns: some use underscores (e.g., "hire_cust1", "mail_group_add", "rpt_battery", "battery_change_rpt"), some mix underscores and positions of type indicators differently. For example, "rpt_battery" puts "rpt" first while "battery_change_rpt" puts "rpt" last. This inconsistency makes the codebase harder to navigate and understand.
**Fix:** Establish and document a consistent naming convention for op_codes, such as always using "<entity>_<action>" (e.g., "battery_report", "battery_change_report") or "<action>_<entity>" (e.g., "report_battery", "report_battery_change"). Refactor all op_codes to follow the chosen convention.
**File:** reports/hire_vehicle_lst1.jsp, reports/mail_conf.jsp, reports/mail_conf_au.jsp, reports/mail_conf_monthly.jsp, reports/mail_first.jsp, reports/nothire_vehicle_lst.jsp, reports/print_battery.jsp, reports/print_battery_change.jsp
**Line(s):** hire_vehicle_lst1.jsp: 26; mail_conf.jsp: 150; mail_conf_au.jsp: 148; mail_conf_monthly.jsp: 190; mail_first.jsp: 31; nothire_vehicle_lst.jsp: 24; print_battery.jsp: 17; print_battery_change.jsp: 20

### P73-F10: Raw ArrayList types without generics
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Multiple files declare ArrayList variables without generic type parameters (e.g., mail_conf.jsp lines 153-163, print_battery.jsp lines 49-56). This is a raw type usage that generates compiler warnings and loses type safety. Modern Java code should always use generics with collections to prevent ClassCastException at runtime and enable compile-time type checking.
**Fix:** Add generic type parameters to all ArrayList declarations. For example, change "ArrayList grp_cd" to "ArrayList<String> grp_cd" or whatever the appropriate type is. Review and update all ArrayList declarations throughout the codebase.
**File:** reports/hire_vehicle_lst1.jsp, reports/mail_conf.jsp, reports/mail_conf_au.jsp, reports/mail_conf_monthly.jsp, reports/mail_first.jsp, reports/nothire_vehicle_lst.jsp, reports/print_battery.jsp
**Line(s):** hire_vehicle_lst1.jsp: 32-36; mail_conf.jsp: 153-163; mail_conf_au.jsp: 151-161; mail_conf_monthly.jsp: 194-204; mail_first.jsp: 33-34; nothire_vehicle_lst.jsp: 30-40; print_battery.jsp: 49-56, 78-81

### P73-F11: Missing type attribute on HTML form inputs
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Many input elements are missing the type attribute, relying on the browser default (text). For example, mail_conf.jsp line 231: "<input type=text size=30 maxlength=100 name=subject value="">". While browsers default to type="text", it's better practice to be explicit, especially for consistency and clarity.
**Fix:** Explicitly add type="text" to all input elements that are meant to be text inputs. Also note that attribute values should be quoted (type="text" not type=text) for proper XHTML/HTML5 compliance.
**File:** reports/mail_conf.jsp, reports/mail_conf_au.jsp, reports/mail_conf_monthly.jsp, reports/mail_first.jsp
**Line(s):** mail_conf.jsp: 200, 231; mail_conf_au.jsp: 198, 234; mail_conf_monthly.jsp: 285; mail_first.jsp: 133, 139

### P73-F12: Unquoted HTML attribute values
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Throughout the JSP files, many HTML attributes have unquoted values, particularly in older-style HTML. For example, "<td align=center>" instead of "<td align=\"center\">". While this works in HTML, it's poor practice and violates XHTML standards. Some files claim to use XHTML DOCTYPE but don't follow XHTML syntax rules.
**Fix:** Quote all HTML attribute values using double quotes for consistency and standards compliance. This can be done with a find-replace operation for common patterns like align=center, bgcolor=white, border=1, etc.
**File:** All reviewed JSP files
**Line(s):** Throughout multiple files - examples: hire_vehicle_lst1.jsp: 44-84; mail_conf.jsp: 176-296; nothire_vehicle_lst.jsp: 49-95; print_battery.jsp: 88-187

### P73-F13: Debug/development code in production file
**Severity:** INFO
**Description:** param_test.jsp (lines 33-42) contains code that constructs directory paths, prints debug information to console with System.out.println(), and appears to be a test/development file. The file name "param_test.jsp" confirms this. Debug files should not be deployed to production environments.
**Fix:** If this file is used for testing/debugging during development, move it to a separate test directory that is excluded from production builds. If it's no longer needed, delete it. Never deploy test files to production.
**File:** reports/param_test.jsp
**Line(s):** 1-43 (entire file)

### P73-F14: Commented-out code block
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** print_battery.jsp contains a commented-out body tag on line 86: "<!-- <body bgcolor=\"white\" onload=\"print('document'); \"> -->". Commented-out code clutters the codebase and creates confusion about whether the functionality should be active or not. If auto-print is not desired, the code should be removed entirely rather than commented out.
**Fix:** Either remove the commented-out code entirely, or if the auto-print functionality is needed, uncomment it and remove the current body tag on line 87. Commented code should never remain in production.
**File:** reports/print_battery.jsp
**Line(s):** 86

### P73-F15: Inconsistent variable naming - camelCase vs underscore
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variable naming is inconsistent across files. Some use camelCase (arrBattery, batteryBean in print_battery_change.jsp), some use underscore_case (cust_cd, loc_cd, dept_cd, vunit_name, vdriver_name), and some use mixed patterns. Java conventions prefer camelCase for local variables and underscores only for constants.
**Fix:** Establish a consistent naming convention following Java standards: camelCase for variables and methods, UPPER_SNAKE_CASE for constants. While changing existing database column names may not be feasible, at least ensure Java variables follow camelCase consistently.
**File:** All reviewed files
**Line(s):** Throughout - examples of mixed patterns: print_battery_change.jsp: 53 (arrBattery - camelCase), vs line 12 (gp_cd - underscore)

### P73-F16: Potential NPE from unchecked session attributes
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Multiple files retrieve session attributes (access_level, access_cust, user_cd) and use them directly without null checking before calling methods on them. For example, mail_conf.jsp line 136 checks if access_level.equalsIgnoreCase("") - but if session.getAttribute() returns null, this will throw a NullPointerException. The Expire.jsp include may handle session validation, but the code is fragile without explicit null checks.
**Fix:** Add null checks before using session attributes: if (access_level == null || access_level.equalsIgnoreCase("")) { access_level = "5"; }. Alternatively, use a utility method that safely extracts session attributes with defaults.
**File:** reports/mail_conf.jsp, reports/mail_conf_au.jsp, reports/mail_conf_monthly.jsp, reports/mail_first.jsp, reports/print_battery.jsp, reports/print_battery_change.jsp
**Line(s):** mail_conf.jsp: 132-140; mail_conf_au.jsp: 132-139; mail_conf_monthly.jsp: 172-180; mail_first.jsp: 15-23; print_battery.jsp: 22-31; print_battery_change.jsp: 24-33

### P73-F17: Duplicate row color alternation logic
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** The pattern for alternating row background colors is duplicated across multiple files with identical logic: checking if i%2==0 to set bgcol to "#FFFFFF" or "lightgrey". This appears in mail_conf.jsp (lines 264-271), mail_conf_au.jsp (lines 267-274), mail_conf_monthly.jsp (lines 318-325), print_battery.jsp (lines 175-181), print_battery_change.jsp (lines 94-98), and nothire_vehicle_lst.jsp (line 72).
**Fix:** Use CSS classes instead of inline bgcolor attributes for better separation of concerns. Define CSS classes .even-row and .odd-row with appropriate background colors, then use class="<%=i%2==0 ? \"even-row\" : \"odd-row\"%>" in the JSP. This eliminates the Java code for color selection and makes styling easier to maintain.
**File:** reports/mail_conf.jsp, reports/mail_conf_au.jsp, reports/mail_conf_monthly.jsp, reports/nothire_vehicle_lst.jsp, reports/print_battery.jsp, reports/print_battery_change.jsp
**Line(s):** mail_conf.jsp: 264-271; mail_conf_au.jsp: 267-274; mail_conf_monthly.jsp: 318-325; nothire_vehicle_lst.jsp: 72; print_battery.jsp: 175-181; print_battery_change.jsp: 94-98

### P73-F18: Empty script tag
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** hire_vehicle_lst1.jsp contains an empty <script> tag (lines 14-17) with no content. This serves no purpose and should be removed. It suggests incomplete development or forgotten cleanup.
**Fix:** Remove the empty script tag entirely.
**File:** reports/hire_vehicle_lst1.jsp
**Line(s):** 14-17

### P73-F19: Duplicate response.setHeader calls
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** nothire_vehicle_lst.jsp has two response.setHeader calls for "Content-disposition" with different values on consecutive lines (20-21). The second call will overwrite the first, making line 20 dead code. The first sets "inline; filename=..." while the second sets "attachment; filename=...". This is either an error or leftover code from debugging.
**Fix:** Remove the duplicate line. Keep only line 21 with "attachment" disposition if the intent is to download the file, or only line 20 with "inline" if the intent is to display it inline. Based on the Excel download context, "attachment" is likely the correct choice.
**File:** reports/nothire_vehicle_lst.jsp
**Line(s):** 20-21

### P73-F20: Unused variable 'firstRow'
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** nothire_vehicle_lst.jsp declares a Boolean variable "firstRow" and initializes it to true (line 72), but this variable is never used anywhere in the code. This is dead code that should be removed.
**Fix:** Remove the line "Boolean firstRow = true;" as it serves no purpose.
**File:** reports/nothire_vehicle_lst.jsp
**Line(s):** 72

### P73-F21: Inconsistent file naming convention
**Severity:** INFO
**Description:** JSP file names use inconsistent patterns: some use underscores (hire_vehicle_lst1, mail_conf, nothire_vehicle_lst), some indicate versioning (hire_vehicle_lst1 suggests there might be a lst2 or base version), and some are generic (param_test). The "1" suffix in hire_vehicle_lst1 is unclear - does a version without "1" exist?
**Fix:** Review and standardize file naming conventions. Remove version numbers from filenames unless versioning is intentionally managed. Use descriptive names that clearly indicate the file's purpose. Document any naming conventions in a project style guide.
**File:** reports/hire_vehicle_lst1.jsp, reports/param_test.jsp
**Line(s):** N/A (filename itself)

### P73-F22: Inconsistent form naming
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** mail_first.jsp uses form name="xxxx" (line 113), which is a placeholder or debug name that should never appear in production code. Other forms in the codebase have no name attribute at all, which is acceptable in modern HTML.
**Fix:** Either remove the name attribute from the form (it's not required in modern HTML), or use a meaningful name if the form needs to be referenced by JavaScript. Never use placeholder names like "xxxx" in production code.
**File:** reports/mail_first.jsp
**Line(s):** 113

### P73-F23: Mixed use of single and double quotes in JavaScript
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** JavaScript code uses both single quotes and double quotes for strings inconsistently. For example, mail_conf.jsp line 76 uses double quotes ("Kindly enter the proper frequency in no of days") while line 90 uses single quotes ('0'). Modern JavaScript style guides recommend consistency - either always use single quotes or always use double quotes.
**Fix:** Choose one quote style for JavaScript strings and apply it consistently. ESLint or Prettier can enforce this automatically. Single quotes are common in modern JavaScript, but the important thing is consistency.
**File:** All files with JavaScript
**Line(s):** Throughout JavaScript sections in mail_conf.jsp, mail_conf_au.jsp, mail_conf_monthly.jsp, mail_first.jsp

### P73-F24: Hardcoded URLs and paths
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Multiple files contain hardcoded URLs to external resources. For example, print_battery.jsp lines 94, 96, 115 use hardcoded "//fms.fleetiq360.com//images/..." and "http://fms.fleetiq360.com//images/..." URLs. These hardcoded values make the application environment-dependent and difficult to configure for different deployments (dev, staging, production).
**Fix:** Move all URLs to a configuration file or system properties that can be changed per environment. Use LindeConfig or a similar configuration class (already used for emailurl, siteName, reportLogo, reportHead) to centralize URL configuration. Replace hardcoded URLs with references to these configuration properties.
**File:** reports/print_battery.jsp
**Line(s):** 94, 96, 115

### P73-F25: Inconsistent use of LindeConfig vs SessionVar
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** print_battery.jsp uses both LindeConfig (lines 90, 94, 96, 118) and SessionVar (line 102) for configuration values. This inconsistency suggests there may be duplicate or overlapping configuration sources, which could lead to confusion about which source is authoritative for different settings.
**Fix:** Review and consolidate configuration sources. Establish one authoritative configuration class (preferably LindeConfig based on usage patterns) and migrate all configuration to it. Document clearly which configuration values should come from which source and why.
**File:** reports/print_battery.jsp
**Line(s):** 90, 94, 96, 102, 118

### P73-F26: Unused import in mail_report.jsp
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** mail_report.jsp imports javax.naming.*, javax.servlet.*, and javax.servlet.http.HttpSession (lines 3-6) but doesn't use any classes from these packages. The only servlet-related objects used are request and session, which are implicit JSP objects that don't require imports.
**Fix:** Remove unused imports: javax.naming.*, javax.servlet.*, javax.servlet.http.*, and javax.servlet.http.HttpSession. Keep only the imports that are actually used.
**File:** reports/mail_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 3-6

### P73-F27: Security risk - URL manipulation from user input
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Multiple files take URL parameters from request.getParameter("url") and use them directly or with minimal manipulation (url.replaceAll(" ","+")). For example, mail_conf.jsp line 127, mail_report.jsp lines 17-20. This could allow URL injection attacks where malicious users craft URLs that redirect to external sites or inject unexpected content.
**Fix:** Validate and sanitize URL parameters before use. Implement a whitelist of allowed URL patterns or validate that URLs are relative paths within the application. Never trust user input for URL construction. Consider using a URL validation utility that checks for proper encoding and prevents injection.
**File:** reports/mail_conf.jsp, reports/mail_conf_au.jsp, reports/mail_conf_monthly.jsp, reports/mail_first.jsp, reports/mail_report.jsp
**Line(s):** mail_conf.jsp: 121, 127; mail_conf_au.jsp: 121, 129; mail_conf_monthly.jsp: 160, 168; mail_first.jsp: 11; mail_report.jsp: 17-20

### P73-F28: Potential path traversal vulnerability in param_test.jsp
**Severity:** HIGH
**Description:** param_test.jsp constructs file system paths using getProtectionDomain().getCodeSource().getLocation() and performs string manipulation (lines 33-38) without any validation. The resulting path is constructed using string concatenation with hardcoded "../../../../../../../excelrpt/" which is a classic path traversal pattern. If this code is used to access files based on user input, it could allow directory traversal attacks.
**Fix:** If param_test.jsp is a test file, remove it from production deployments. If the path construction logic is needed elsewhere, use Path.normalize() to sanitize paths, validate that the resulting path is within expected boundaries, and never allow user input to influence path construction without strict validation. Consider using Path.resolve() with proper security checks.
**File:** reports/param_test.jsp
**Line(s):** 33-38

---

## Summary
**Total Findings:** 28
- CRITICAL: 0
- HIGH: 1
- MEDIUM: 7
- LOW: 18
- INFO: 2

**Key Issues:**
1. Code duplication (AJAX functions, row coloring logic)
2. Raw ArrayList usage without generics (compiler warnings)
3. Security concerns (URL injection, path traversal, potential NPE)
4. Dead/commented code
5. Inconsistent coding standards (naming, null checks, quoting)
6. Deprecated HTML/JavaScript attributes
7. Debug/test code in production

**Recommendations:**
- Refactor common JavaScript into shared files
- Apply Java generics consistently
- Implement input validation and security best practices
- Remove all dead/commented/test code
- Standardize coding conventions across the codebase
- Migrate to HTML5 and modern JavaScript standards

## Findings

### P74-F01: Extensive commented-out code blocks
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** The file print_daily_veh_summary.jsp contains 17 consecutive lines of commented-out code (lines 57-73) declaring unused ArrayLists and String variables. This includes commented declarations for dsum_oper_hr, dsum_sb_hr, dsum_hyd_hr, dsum_key_hrt, dsum_oper_hrt, dsum_sb_hrt, dsum_hyd_hrt, dsum_oper_hr_nc, dsum_sb_hr_nc, dsum_hyd_hr_nc, dsum_oper_hrtnc, dsum_sb_hrtnc, and dsum_hyd_hrtnc. Commented-out code clutters the codebase, creates confusion about intent, and carries a risk of accidental reactivation.
**Fix:** Remove the commented-out code entirely (lines 57-73). If this functionality might be needed in the future, rely on version control history to recover it. The code should be either active or removed, not left in a commented state.
**File:** reports/print_daily_veh_summary.jsp
**Line(s):** 57-73

### P74-F02: Commented-out code in access abuse report
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** The file print_driver_access_abuse.jsp contains multiple instances of commented-out code: one ArrayList declaration (line 50: "// ArrayList a_driv_id = filter.getA_driv_id();"), two table header elements (lines 75-76, 81-82 for "Driver Id" and "Model"), and one table data cell (lines 112-113). This creates ambiguity about whether Driver ID and Model columns should be displayed and clutters the code.
**Fix:** Remove all commented-out code blocks on lines 50, 75-76, 81-82, and 112-113. If these columns are not needed, delete them completely. If they might be needed in the future, rely on version control to recover them.
**File:** reports/print_driver_access_abuse.jsp
**Line(s):** 50, 75-76, 81-82, 112-113

### P74-F03: Commented-out body tags
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Three files contain commented-out body tags that serve no purpose: print_daily_driver_summary.jsp (line 66: "<!-- <body> -->"), print_daily_veh_summary.jsp (implied), and print_driver_access_abuse.jsp (line 62: "<!-- <body> -->"). Additionally, print_curr_unit_report.jsp has a commented-out body tag on line 91 ("<!-- <body> -->"). These are redundant since proper body tags exist later in each file.
**Fix:** Remove the commented-out body tag lines (line 66 in print_daily_driver_summary.jsp, line 62 in print_driver_access_abuse.jsp, and line 91 in print_curr_unit_report.jsp).
**File:** reports/print_daily_driver_summary.jsp, reports/print_driver_access_abuse.jsp, reports/print_curr_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 66, 62, 91 respectively

### P74-F04: Inconsistent access level control implementation
**Severity:** HIGH
**Description:** Access level control is implemented inconsistently across the report files. Most files (print_battery_charge.jsp, print_broadcastmsg.jsp, print_curr_driv_report.jsp, print_curr_unit_report.jsp, print_daily_driver_summary.jsp, print_daily_veh_summary.jsp, print_driver_access_abuse.jsp, print_driver_impact_report.jsp) implement full access level control by retrieving session attributes and setting them on the filter bean. However, print_detail_operation.jsp has NO access level control implementation at all, and print_driver_league.jsp has a comment "//end of access level control" (line 47) but no actual implementation. This creates a security vulnerability where unauthorized users could access detailed operational data or driver league information.
**Fix:** Implement consistent access level control in print_detail_operation.jsp and print_driver_league.jsp by adding the standard pattern: retrieve access_level, access_cust, access_site, and access_dept from session, provide default value "5" if empty, parse the integer, and call filter.setAccess_level(), setAccess_cust(), setAccess_site(), and setAccess_dept() before filter.init().
**File:** reports/print_detail_operation.jsp, reports/print_driver_league.jsp
**Line(s):** 17-34 (missing implementation in print_detail_operation.jsp), 47 (misleading comment in print_driver_league.jsp)

### P74-F05: Hardcoded absolute URLs
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Two files contain hardcoded absolute URLs to external domains: print_curr_driv_report.jsp uses "//fms.fleetiq360.com//images/linde/logo_rpt.png" (line 96), "//fms.fleetiq360.com//images/linde/header.png" (line 98), "http://fms.fleetiq360.com//images/linde/left-round.jpg" (line 117), and "http://fms.fleetiq360.com//images/linde/right-round.jpg" (line 161). The same URLs appear in print_curr_unit_report.jsp (lines 98, 100, 119, 163). These hardcoded URLs create tight coupling to external resources, will fail if the domain changes, and bypass SSL when using http:// protocol.
**Fix:** Replace hardcoded URLs with configuration variables from LindeConfig or SessionVar (similar to how line 104 uses SessionVar.reportLogo). For example, use LindeConfig.logoUrl, LindeConfig.headerUrl, LindeConfig.leftRoundUrl, and LindeConfig.rightRoundUrl. Alternatively, use relative paths if these images should be served from the application's own domain.
**File:** reports/print_curr_driv_report.jsp, reports/print_curr_unit_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 96, 98, 117, 161 (print_curr_driv_report.jsp); 98, 100, 119, 163 (print_curr_unit_report.jsp)

### P74-F06: Raw ArrayList usage without type parameters
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Multiple files use raw ArrayList declarations without generic type parameters, which generates unchecked warnings during compilation. For example, print_curr_driv_report.jsp declares "ArrayList vcust_cd = filter.getCust_cd();" (line 49) and similar untyped ArrayLists for vcust_nm, Vloc_cd, Vloc_nm, vdept_cd, vdept_nm, veh_fno, vrpt_tm, vveh_cd, vveh_id, vveh_nm, vveh_typ_cd, vveh_typ, vdriv_nm, vst_tm (lines 49-82). This pattern appears in print_curr_unit_report.jsp (lines 48-83), print_daily_driver_summary.jsp (lines 49-60), print_detail_operation.jsp (lines 36-74), print_driver_access_abuse.jsp (lines 48-56), and print_driver_impact_report.jsp (lines 55-83). Only print_battery_charge.jsp, print_broadcastmsg.jsp, and print_driver_league.jsp use proper generic types.
**Fix:** Add generic type parameters to all ArrayList declarations. For example, change "ArrayList vcust_cd = filter.getCust_cd();" to "ArrayList<String> vcust_cd = filter.getCust_cd();" (or appropriate type based on content). This eliminates compiler warnings and provides type safety.
**File:** reports/print_curr_driv_report.jsp, reports/print_curr_unit_report.jsp, reports/print_daily_driver_summary.jsp, reports/print_daily_veh_summary.jsp, reports/print_detail_operation.jsp, reports/print_driver_access_abuse.jsp, reports/print_driver_impact_report.jsp
**Line(s):** Multiple lines throughout each file (49-82 in print_curr_driv_report.jsp, 48-83 in print_curr_unit_report.jsp, 49-60 in print_daily_driver_summary.jsp, 49-75 in print_daily_veh_summary.jsp, 36-74 in print_detail_operation.jsp, 48-56 in print_driver_access_abuse.jsp, 55-83 in print_driver_impact_report.jsp)

### P74-F07: Inconsistent naming conventions for ArrayList variables
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** ArrayList variable naming is inconsistent across files. Some use prefixes like 'V' or 'v' (vcust_cd, Vloc_cd, Vgp_cd, Vuser_cd, Vmodel_cd in print_detail_operation.jsp lines 36-41; vveh_cd, vdriv_nm in print_curr_driv_report.jsp), some use descriptive prefixes like 'dsum_' (dsum_driver_cd, dsum_key_hr in print_daily_driver_summary.jsp), some use 'a_' prefix (a_driv_cd, a_veh_nm in print_driver_access_abuse.jsp), some use 'dir_' prefix (dir_driv_nm, dir_blue_c in print_driver_impact_report.jsp), and the casing is inconsistent ('Vloc_cd' vs 'vcust_cd'). This makes the codebase harder to understand and maintain.
**Fix:** Adopt a consistent naming convention for ArrayList variables across all report JSPs. Recommend using lowercase descriptive names without prefixes (e.g., 'customerCodes', 'locationCodes', 'driverSummaries') or if prefixes are needed, use them consistently (all lowercase 'v' or remove prefixes entirely). Update all ArrayList variable names to follow the chosen convention.
**File:** All reviewed files
**Line(s):** Throughout all files

### P74-F08: Duplicated access level control code pattern
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Eight files contain nearly identical access level control code blocks (approximately 20 lines each): retrieving session attributes for access_level, access_cust, access_site, access_dept, checking if access_level is empty and defaulting to "5", parsing to integer (which is stored but never used as variable 'al'), and setting these values on the filter bean. This represents significant code duplication across print_battery_charge.jsp (lines 22-40), print_broadcastmsg.jsp (lines 27-43), print_curr_driv_report.jsp (lines 20-36), print_curr_unit_report.jsp (lines 19-35), print_daily_driver_summary.jsp (lines 19-35), print_daily_veh_summary.jsp (lines 20-35), print_driver_access_abuse.jsp (lines 18-34), and print_driver_impact_report.jsp (lines 22-37). This is a leaky abstraction where security concerns are scattered across presentation layer files.
**Fix:** Extract the access level control logic into a reusable JSP include file (e.g., ../includes/access_level_control.jsp) or into a utility method on the filter bean that can be called with the session object. Replace all duplicated code blocks with a single include directive or method call. This centralizes security logic and makes it easier to maintain and audit.
**File:** reports/print_battery_charge.jsp, reports/print_broadcastmsg.jsp, reports/print_curr_driv_report.jsp, reports/print_curr_unit_report.jsp, reports/print_daily_driver_summary.jsp, reports/print_daily_veh_summary.jsp, reports/print_driver_access_abuse.jsp, reports/print_driver_impact_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 22-40, 27-43, 20-36, 19-35, 19-35, 20-35, 18-34, 22-37 respectively

### P74-F09: Duplicated request parameter retrieval pattern
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** All files use the same verbose pattern for retrieving request parameters with null-checking and default values: 'request.getParameter("param")==null?"default":request.getParameter("param")'. This pattern is repeated 5-11 times per file, resulting in hundreds of lines of duplicated code across all 10 files. This approach also calls request.getParameter() twice for each parameter, which is inefficient.
**Fix:** Create a utility method (e.g., in UtilBean) that takes the request, parameter name, and default value, and returns the parameter value or default in one call. For example: 'String gp_cd = Util.getParameter(request, "gp_cd", "1");'. This reduces code duplication, improves readability, and eliminates the double getParameter() call.
**File:** All reviewed files
**Line(s):** Throughout all files (e.g., lines 8-18 in print_battery_charge.jsp, lines 8-17 in print_broadcastmsg.jsp, etc.)

### P74-F10: Unused variable 'al'
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Eight files declare a variable 'al' by parsing the access_level string to an integer, but this variable is never used anywhere in the code. The pattern appears in print_battery_charge.jsp (line 33: "int al = Integer.parseInt(access_level);"), print_broadcastmsg.jsp (line 38), print_curr_driv_report.jsp (line 31), print_curr_unit_report.jsp (line 30), print_daily_driver_summary.jsp (line 30), print_daily_veh_summary.jsp (line 31), print_driver_access_abuse.jsp (line 29), and print_driver_impact_report.jsp (line 32). The parsed value is never referenced subsequently.
**Fix:** Remove the unused 'al' variable declaration from all eight files. If integer parsing was meant to validate that access_level is a valid number, move this validation into the access level control utility method recommended in P74-F08, or remove it entirely if the filter bean performs this validation.
**File:** reports/print_battery_charge.jsp, reports/print_broadcastmsg.jsp, reports/print_curr_driv_report.jsp, reports/print_curr_unit_report.jsp, reports/print_daily_driver_summary.jsp, reports/print_daily_veh_summary.jsp, reports/print_driver_access_abuse.jsp, reports/print_driver_impact_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 33, 38, 31, 30, 30, 31, 29, 32 respectively

### P74-F11: Unused retrieved ArrayLists in print_driver_impact_report.jsp
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** The file print_driver_impact_report.jsp retrieves multiple ArrayLists from the filter bean that are never used in the display logic: veh_cd (line 55), veh_nm (line 56), veh_typ_nm1 (line 57), Vveh_cd (line 59), Vhire_no (line 60), Vmodel_no (line 61), Vdriver (line 62), Vcard_no (line 63), Vimp_tm (line 64), Vimp_ene (line 65), Vimp_img (line 67), photoTotal (line 68), dsum_oper_hr (line 70), and dsum_oper_hr_nc (line 71). Only the 'dir_' prefixed variables (lines 75-83) are actually used in the display loop (lines 100-124). This suggests incomplete refactoring or dead code.
**Fix:** Remove the unused ArrayList declarations and corresponding filter bean method calls for veh_cd, veh_nm, veh_typ_nm1, Vveh_cd, Vhire_no, Vmodel_no, Vdriver, Vcard_no, Vimp_tm, Vimp_ene, Vimp_img, photoTotal, dsum_oper_hr, and dsum_oper_hr_nc (lines 55-71). Only keep the variables that are actually used in the display logic.
**File:** reports/print_driver_impact_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 55-71

### P74-F12: Inconsistent styling for table row alternation
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Files use inconsistent approaches for alternating table row colors. Most use inline style attribute with RGB color (style="background-color: rgb(194, 232, 102);" in print_battery_charge.jsp line 101, print_broadcastmsg.jsp line 84, print_curr_driv_report.jsp line 184, etc.), but print_driver_league.jsp uses no explicit class or inline style for alternation (lines 100-105). Additionally, some files have extra spacing and attribute formatting inconsistencies (e.g., 'align=center' without quotes vs 'align="center"', 'class="textblack"' in some places).
**Fix:** Standardize the row alternation approach across all files. Either use a CSS class for alternating rows (e.g., class="even-row" and class="odd-row" defined in a CSS file), or consistently use inline styles. Also standardize HTML attribute quoting - always use quotes around attribute values (align="center" not align=center).
**File:** All reviewed files
**Line(s):** Multiple lines throughout each file

### P74-F13: Inconsistent default value for empty access_level
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** All files that implement access level control use the same pattern to check if access_level is empty and set it to "5" as a default (e.g., lines 29-32 in print_battery_charge.jsp: "if(access_level.equalsIgnoreCase(\"\")){ access_level = \"5\"; }"). However, this check uses equalsIgnoreCase("") which would throw a NullPointerException if access_level from the session is null, which is likely if the user is not authenticated. The proper check should handle null values.
**Fix:** Change the empty check to handle null values: "if(access_level == null || access_level.trim().isEmpty()){ access_level = \"5\"; }". This prevents NullPointerException and also handles whitespace-only values. Apply this fix to all 8 files that implement access level control.
**File:** reports/print_battery_charge.jsp, reports/print_broadcastmsg.jsp, reports/print_curr_driv_report.jsp, reports/print_curr_unit_report.jsp, reports/print_daily_driver_summary.jsp, reports/print_daily_veh_summary.jsp, reports/print_driver_access_abuse.jsp, reports/print_driver_impact_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 29-32, 34-37, 27-30, 26-29, 26-29, 27-30, 25-28, 28-31 respectively

### P74-F14: Inconsistent filter bean classes
**Severity:** INFO
**Description:** The files use two different filter bean classes: most files (7 out of 10) use com.torrent.surat.fms6.reports.Databean_report, but print_curr_driv_report.jsp, print_curr_unit_report.jsp, and print_driver_league.jsp use com.torrent.surat.fms6.reports.Databean_dyn_reports. While this may be intentional based on report type, it creates inconsistency in the codebase and makes it harder to understand which bean to use for new reports. The naming also uses snake_case (Databean_report, Databean_dyn_reports) which violates Java naming conventions (should be DatabeanReport, DatabeanDynReports).
**Fix:** Document when to use each filter bean class (add comments or documentation). Consider consolidating into a single filter bean class if the functionality overlaps significantly, or rename the classes to follow Java naming conventions (PascalCase without underscores). If they serve different purposes, make the naming more descriptive (e.g., StaticReportDatabean vs DynamicReportDatabean).
**File:** All reviewed files
**Line(s):** Line 4 or 15 in each file (jsp:useBean declaration)

### P74-F15: Hardcoded string literal "1" for division code
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Several files set the division code to a hardcoded string literal "1" using filter.setSet_div_cd("1"). This appears in print_curr_driv_report.jsp (line 41), print_curr_unit_report.jsp (line 40), print_driver_impact_report.jsp (line 40), and print_driver_league.jsp (line 50). This magic number "1" lacks context about what division it represents and makes the code harder to understand and maintain.
**Fix:** Replace the hardcoded "1" with a named constant that explains its meaning, such as LindeConfig.DEFAULT_DIVISION_CODE or filter.DEFAULT_DIVISION, or retrieve it from request parameters like the other codes. If "1" always means "all divisions" or "default division", use a descriptive constant.
**File:** reports/print_curr_driv_report.jsp, reports/print_curr_unit_report.jsp, reports/print_driver_impact_report.jsp, reports/print_driver_league.jsp
**Line(s):** 41, 40, 40, 50 respectively

### P74-F16: Inconsistent onload attribute in body tag
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** The body tag onload attribute varies across files. Most use "print('document'); " with a trailing space (print_battery_charge.jsp line 57, print_broadcastmsg.jsp line 61, etc.), but print_driver_impact_report.jsp uses "print('document');window.close();" (line 86) which closes the window immediately after printing, and print_detail_operation.jsp uses "print('document'); " (line 84). The trailing space is inconsistent, and the automatic window.close() in print_driver_impact_report.jsp may not be desired behavior in all contexts.
**Fix:** Standardize the onload attribute across all report files. Remove trailing spaces for consistency. Review whether window.close() is appropriate for print_driver_impact_report.jsp - this may close the window before the user can view or interact with the report if printing is cancelled. Consider removing the automatic close or making it conditional based on print success.
**File:** All reviewed files
**Line(s):** 57, 61, 89, 90, 65, 81, 84, 61, 86, 64 respectively

### P74-F17: Potential XSS vulnerability in unescaped output
**Severity:** HIGH
**Description:** All files output user-controlled data directly to the page using JSP expressions (<%= %>) without HTML escaping. For example, print_battery_charge.jsp outputs batteryBean.getBef_veh_nm(), batteryBean.getBef_dri_nm() (lines 109-111) and other values that likely originate from user input or database records. If this data contains HTML special characters or JavaScript, it could lead to XSS attacks. This pattern appears throughout all 10 files wherever ArrayList data is displayed.
**Fix:** Use JSTL's <c:out> tag with escapeXml="true" (default) for all user-controlled output, or use a utility function to HTML-escape all values before output. For example, replace "<%=batteryBean.getBef_veh_nm() %>" with "<c:out value='${batteryBean.befVehNm}'/>". Alternatively, implement a utility method like UtilBean.escapeHtml() and use "<%=UtilBean.escapeHtml(batteryBean.getBef_veh_nm()) %>". Apply this fix systematically to all user-controlled output across all files.
**File:** All reviewed files
**Line(s):** Throughout all files wherever ArrayList or bean data is displayed

### P74-F18: Mixed use of scriptlet expressions and spacing
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Files inconsistently format scriptlet expressions - some have spaces around the Java code inside <%= %> tags (e.g., "<%=batteryBean.getBat_fleet_num() %>" with no space vs "<%=dt %> <%=tm %>" in print_curr_driv_report.jsp line 138), and some outputs are on the same line as table cells while others are on separate lines. This creates visual inconsistency.
**Fix:** Adopt a consistent formatting style for JSP expressions throughout all files. Recommend: always include a space after <%= and before %>, and place expressions on the same line as their containing HTML element unless it exceeds a reasonable line length. Use a code formatter or linter to enforce this consistently.
**File:** All reviewed files
**Line(s):** Throughout all files

## Summary

**Total findings:** 18

**Severity breakdown:**
- CRITICAL: 0
- HIGH: 2 (P74-F04: Missing access level control, P74-F17: XSS vulnerability)
- MEDIUM: 8 (P74-F01, F02, F03, F05, F06, F08, F13)
- LOW: 7 (P74-F07, F09, F10, F11, F12, F15, F16, F18)
- INFO: 1 (P74-F14)

**Key themes:**
1. Security issues: Missing access control (2 files), potential XSS vulnerabilities (all files), null pointer risks
2. Code quality: Extensive commented-out code, duplicated code patterns, unused variables and imports
3. Consistency: Inconsistent naming conventions, styling, error handling, and bean usage
4. Maintainability: Hardcoded values, raw types without generics, scattered security logic

# Agent P75 — Pass 4 Code Quality Audit
**Files reviewed:**
- reports/print_driver_licence_expiry_detail.jsp
- reports/print_driver_util.jsp
- reports/print_equipment_bypass_alert_report.jsp
- reports/print_hour_counter.jsp
- reports/print_hourmeter_exception.jsp
- reports/print_impact_meter_report.jsp
- reports/print_impact_report.jsp
- reports/print_impact_report_exc.jsp
- reports/print_inspection_report.jsp
- reports/print_key_hour_util.jsp

**Branch verified:** multi-customer-sync-to-master

## Findings

### P75-F01: Hardcoded URLs Throughout Report Pages
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Multiple JSP files contain hardcoded URLs for images and resources (e.g., "http://fms.fleetiq360.com/css/", "//fms.fleetiq360.com//images/"). This creates maintenance issues and prevents environment-specific configuration. If the domain changes or different environments use different URLs, all files must be manually updated.
**Fix:** Replace hardcoded URLs with configuration properties from LindeConfig or a similar configuration bean. Use variables like LindeConfig.baseURL or LindeConfig.cssPath instead of literal strings.
**File:** reports/print_driver_licence_expiry_detail.jsp
**Line(s):** 49, 51, 67, 99

### P75-F02: Hardcoded URLs in Driver Utilization Report
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** The file contains hardcoded URLs for CSS stylesheets (http://fms.fleetiq360.com/css/alternate_rows.css, http://fms.fleetiq360.com/css/reportTables1.css). These should be configurable to support different deployment environments.
**Fix:** Replace hardcoded URLs with configuration properties from LindeConfig or similar configuration bean.
**File:** reports/print_driver_util.jsp
**Line(s):** 8, 9

### P75-F03: Hardcoded URLs in Inspection Report
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** The file contains hardcoded URLs for images (http://fms.fleetiq360.com//images/linde/left-round.jpg, right-round.jpg, header.png). These should be externalized to configuration.
**Fix:** Replace hardcoded URLs with configuration properties from LindeConfig or similar configuration bean.
**File:** reports/print_inspection_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 209, 223, 250

### P75-F04: Raw ArrayList Usage Without Generics
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** All JSP files extensively use raw ArrayList types without generic type parameters (e.g., "ArrayList driver_nm" instead of "ArrayList<String> driver_nm"). This bypasses compile-time type safety and generates unchecked conversion warnings during compilation. Raw types are deprecated practice in modern Java and can lead to ClassCastException at runtime.
**Fix:** Add generic type parameters to all ArrayList declarations. For example, change "ArrayList driver_nm" to "ArrayList<String> driver_nm", "ArrayList veh_cd" to "ArrayList<String> veh_cd", etc. Review each ArrayList usage to determine the appropriate generic type.
**File:** reports/print_driver_licence_expiry_detail.jsp
**Line(s):** 36-38

### P75-F05: Raw ArrayList Usage in Driver Utilization Report
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** The file uses raw ArrayList types throughout without generic type parameters (e.g., line 60-71, 106-107). This generates compiler warnings and reduces type safety.
**Fix:** Add generic type parameters to all ArrayList declarations. For example, "ArrayList<String> Vgp_cd", "ArrayList<String> Vuser_nm", "ArrayList<double[]> arrUtil_driver", etc.
**File:** reports/print_driver_util.jsp
**Line(s):** 60-71, 106-107

### P75-F06: Mixed Raw and Typed ArrayList Usage
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** The file inconsistently uses raw ArrayList (line 50-52, 54-71) alongside properly typed collections (List<EquipmentBypassAlert> on line 52). This mixing of styles creates confusion and inconsistency.
**Fix:** Consistently use generic type parameters for all collection declarations. Change all raw ArrayLists to use appropriate generic types.
**File:** reports/print_equipment_bypass_alert_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 50-71 (in related context, the typed list is a good example to follow)

### P75-F14: Inconsistent Variable Naming Convention
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variable names use inconsistent prefixes and conventions. Some use 'V' prefix for ArrayLists (Vgp_cd, Vuser_cd), some use 'v' prefix (vdept_cd, vvt_cd), some use 'a_' prefix (a_veh_cd), and some have no prefix at all. This makes code harder to read and maintain.
**Fix:** Standardize variable naming conventions across all files. Choose one consistent approach (e.g., camelCase without prefixes, or a consistent prefix scheme) and apply it uniformly.
**File:** reports/print_driver_util.jsp
**Line(s):** 60-71

### P75-F15: Inconsistent Variable Naming in Hour Counter
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variables use 'a_' prefix (a_veh_cd, a_veh_nm, a_veh_sno, a_veh_mnm) which is inconsistent with other files that use 'V' or 'v' prefixes for similar ArrayList variables.
**Fix:** Standardize variable naming to match the project's predominant convention (likely 'V' prefix based on other files).
**File:** reports/print_hour_counter.jsp
**Line(s):** 50, 51, 58, 59

### P75-F16: Inconsistent Variable Naming in Hourmeter Exception
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variables use capital 'V' prefix (Vgp_cd, Vuser_cd) and lowercase 'v' prefix (vdept_cd, vdept_nm) inconsistently for similar ArrayList variables.
**Fix:** Standardize to use consistent capitalization for the prefix (recommend uppercase 'V' to match most variables).
**File:** reports/print_hourmeter_exception.jsp
**Line(s):** 48-55

**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variables use capital 'V' prefix for some ArrayLists (Vveh_cd, Vhire_no, Vdriver) and lowercase for others (veh_cd, veh_nm) without clear distinction in purpose.

### P75-F21: Empty String Comparison for Access Level
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Access level validation uses ".equalsIgnoreCase("")" which can never be true when the value is retrieved from session (line 29). If access_level is null, it will throw NullPointerException before reaching line 29. If it's empty string, the check at line 29 works, but this suggests defensive programming for a case that may not be properly initialized.
**Fix:** Check for null explicitly and handle uninitialized access level more clearly: "if(access_level == null || access_level.isEmpty())". Consider whether "5" is the appropriate default or if missing access level should be an error.
**File:** reports/print_driver_util.jsp
**Line(s):** 29-32

### P75-F22: Empty String Comparison for Access Level
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Same issue as F21 - access level validation uses ".equalsIgnoreCase("")" which may not handle null correctly.
**Fix:** Check for null explicitly: "if(access_level == null || access_level.isEmpty())". Consider proper error handling for missing access level.
**File:** reports/print_equipment_bypass_alert_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 29-32

### P75-F23: Empty String Comparison for Access Level
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Same issue as F21 - access level validation uses ".equalsIgnoreCase("")" pattern.
**Fix:** Check for null explicitly: "if(access_level == null || access_level.isEmpty())".
**File:** reports/print_hour_counter.jsp
**Line(s):** 26-29

**Fix:** Either use the 'al' variable for access level checks or remove the unused assignment.
**File:** reports/print_impact_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 30

### P75-F37: Unused Variable After Assignment
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variable 'al' is assigned the parsed integer value of access_level (line 47) but is never used in the rest of the file.
**Fix:** Either use the 'al' variable for access level checks or remove the unused assignment.
**File:** reports/print_impact_report_exc.jsp
**Line(s):** 47

### P75-F38: Unused Variable After Assignment
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variable 'al' is assigned the parsed integer value of access_level (line 56) but is only used once at line 164 for a simple comparison. The variable adds minimal value.
**Fix:** Consider removing the variable and using Integer.parseInt(access_level) directly at line 164 if only used once, or keep if access level integer checks will be added elsewhere.
**File:** reports/print_inspection_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 56

### P75-F39: Unused Variable After Assignment
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variable 'al' is assigned the parsed integer value of access_level (line 33) but is never used in the rest of the file.
**Fix:** Either use the 'al' variable for access level checks or remove the unused assignment.
**File:** reports/print_key_hour_util.jsp
**Line(s):** 33

### P75-F40: Commented-Out HTML Code
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** HTML comment tag is present as a commented-out line (line 75: "<!--  <body>  -->"). This appears to be debugging code or an alternative implementation that was never removed. Commented-out code creates confusion about intent and clutters the codebase.
**Fix:** Remove the commented-out HTML body tag. If different body configurations are needed, use conditional logic instead of commented code.
**File:** reports/print_impact_meter_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 75

### P75-F41: Commented-Out Code Block
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** A line of code is commented out (line 207: "// tmp = tmp + " (" + DataUtil.convertToImpPerc((String) tmpMeter.get(j)) + "%)";"). This suggests an alternative implementation that was disabled but never removed. Commented code should be deleted or reinstated.
**Fix:** Either remove the commented line entirely if the logic is no longer needed, or reinstate it if it should be active. Do not leave commented code in production files.
**File:** reports/print_impact_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 207

### P75-F42: Commented-Out HTML Code
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** HTML comment tag is present as commented-out line (line 74: "<!--  <body>  -->"). This is dead code that should be removed.
**Fix:** Remove the commented-out HTML body tag.
**File:** reports/print_impact_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 74

### P75-F43: Inconsistent Table Header Markup
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** The table header row uses <td> tags instead of <th> tags (lines 103-109). While <th> is used in the class attribute value ("email_header"), the actual elements are <td>. This is semantically incorrect HTML and reduces accessibility.
**Fix:** Change <td> tags to <th> tags in the header row for proper semantic HTML markup.
**File:** reports/print_hourmeter_exception.jsp
**Line(s):** 102-109

### P75-F44: Duplicate Table Header Structure
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** The file contains two nearly identical table header structures (lines 91-115 for utilized units, lines 189-213 for unutilized units). The headers have identical columns and structure, which violates DRY principle.
**Fix:** Consider refactoring to use a shared header template or component to reduce duplication. Alternatively, if the headers must remain separate for layout reasons, ensure they stay synchronized.
**File:** reports/print_key_hour_util.jsp
**Line(s):** 91-115, 189-213

### P75-F45: Inconsistent Default Parameter Value
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** The gp_cd parameter defaults to "1" (line 10) in print_key_hour_util.jsp while it defaults to "" (empty string) in other similar files. This inconsistency suggests different assumptions about default group codes across reports.
**Fix:** Review the appropriate default value for gp_cd and ensure consistency across all report pages. Document why this report requires a different default if intentional.
**File:** reports/print_key_hour_util.jsp
**Line(s):** 10

### P75-F46: Code Duplication Between Impact Report Files
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Files print_impact_meter_report.jsp and print_impact_report.jsp contain nearly identical code with only minor differences (Vtmp_imp_data ArrayList and its usage). This represents significant code duplication that makes maintenance difficult - any bug fix or enhancement must be applied to both files.
**Fix:** Refactor to consolidate the duplicate code into a single JSP file with conditional logic for the differences, or extract common functionality into an included JSP fragment.
**File:** reports/print_impact_meter_report.jsp, reports/print_impact_report.jsp
**Line(s):** Entire files are duplicated with minor variations

### P75-F47: Inconsistent Form Tag Attributes
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Most files use form tags without any attributes (no action, method, or id), while this creates consistency, the forms appear incomplete. The forms don't actually submit data - they only wrap content for printing.
**Fix:** Since forms are only used for print layout and don't submit data, consider removing unnecessary form tags or add HTML comments explaining their purpose for print formatting.
**File:** Multiple files (print_driver_util.jsp, print_equipment_bypass_alert_report.jsp, print_hour_counter.jsp, etc.)
**Line(s):** Various (examples: print_driver_util.jsp:116, print_equipment_bypass_alert_report.jsp:57)

### P75-F48: Inconsistent Spacing in Conditional Statements
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Throughout the files, spacing around conditional operators is inconsistent. Some use "i%2==0" without spaces, others use "i % 2 == 0" with spaces. Some use "i < Vveh_cd.size() -1" with mixed spacing.
**Fix:** Apply consistent spacing around operators following Java coding conventions. Recommend: "i % 2 == 0" and "i < Vveh_cd.size() - 1" with spaces around all operators.
**File:** Multiple files
**Line(s):** Examples: print_driver_licence_expiry_detail.jsp:119, print_driver_util.jsp:168, print_equipment_bypass_alert_report.jsp:71

### P75-F49: Hard-Coded Default Form Code
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** The form_cd parameter defaults to "62" (line 33) instead of empty string like other files. This hard-coded form code may not be appropriate for all use cases and reduces flexibility.
**Fix:** Review whether form_cd should default to empty string like other reports, or document why "62" is the appropriate default for inspection reports specifically.
**File:** reports/print_inspection_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 33

### P75-F50: Complex Nested Conditional Logic
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** The file contains deeply nested conditional logic for displaying data with multiple levels of loops and conditions (lines 105-247 in print_impact_meter_report.jsp, lines 106-238 in print_impact_report.jsp). This creates high cyclomatic complexity and makes the code difficult to understand, test, and maintain.
**Fix:** Refactor complex nested loops into smaller methods or separate JSP fragments. Extract conditional display logic into helper methods. Consider using JSTL tags (c:forEach, c:if) instead of scriptlets for cleaner separation of logic and presentation.
**File:** reports/print_impact_meter_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 105-247

### P75-F51: Complex Nested Conditional Logic in Impact Report
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Similar to F50, this file has deeply nested conditional logic for data display.
**Fix:** Refactor complex nested loops and conditions into smaller, more maintainable pieces. Consider JSTL tags for cleaner presentation logic.
**File:** reports/print_impact_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 106-238

### P75-F52: Complex Nested Table Structure
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** The file uses a complex nested table structure (tables within table cells, lines 179-238) which is difficult to maintain and understand. This creates brittle layout code.
**Fix:** Refactor to use CSS for layout instead of nested tables. If tables must be used, simplify the structure and add clear comments explaining the layout purpose.
**File:** reports/print_impact_report_exc.jsp
**Line(s):** 179-238

### P75-F53: String Concatenation in Loop
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** In multiple files, string concatenation is performed repeatedly in loops (e.g., line 213 in print_impact_meter_report.jsp: "tmp = tmp + " (" + tmpMeter.get(j) + "%)""). While not a severe issue for small iterations, this is inefficient for large datasets.
**Fix:** If dealing with large datasets, consider using StringBuilder for string concatenation in loops. For small datasets, current approach is acceptable but could be noted for optimization if performance issues arise.
**File:** reports/print_impact_meter_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 213

---
## Findings

### P76-F01: Unused variable 'al' after access level parsing
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Multiple files declare variable 'al' (parsed integer from access_level) but never use it. This occurs after parsing the access level string to int with `int al = Integer.parseInt(access_level);`. The variable is assigned but never referenced in subsequent code.
**Fix:** Remove the unused variable declaration `int al = Integer.parseInt(access_level);` from all affected files to reduce code clutter.
**File:** reports/print_monthly_online.jsp, reports/print_per_driver_summary.jsp, reports/print_per_veh_summary.jsp, reports/print_preop_chk.jsp, reports/print_preop_chk_detailed.jsp, reports/print_preop_chk_fail.jsp, reports/print_preop_chk_inc.jsp, reports/print_restricted_access_usage_report.jsp, reports/print_seat_hour_util.jsp
**Line(s):** 32, 46, 46, 31, 40, 31, 40, 34, 31 (respectively)

### P76-F02: Hardcoded URLs instead of configuration properties
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Hardcoded URLs like "http://fms.fleetiq360.com//images/linde/..." are embedded directly in JSP code. This creates maintenance issues when domains change and inconsistency with other files that use LindeConfig/SessionVar properties. Note the double slashes in the paths indicating improper URL construction.
**Fix:** Replace hardcoded URLs with configuration properties (e.g., LindeConfig.imageBaseUrl) to centralize URL management and enable environment-specific configuration.
**File:** reports/print_monthly_online.jsp
**Line(s):** 73, 86, 119

### P76-F03: Commented-out HTML body tag
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Contains a commented-out body tag `<!-- <body bgcolor="white"> -->` directly above the active body tag. This suggests code was changed but the old version left in place, creating confusion about which version is correct or whether the commented code might be needed.
**Fix:** Remove the commented-out body tag. If the alternate version is needed for some scenarios, implement it as a conditional branch rather than leaving dead code.
**File:** reports/print_monthly_online.jsp, reports/print_preop_chk_fail.jsp, reports/print_seat_hour_util.jsp
**Line(s):** 64, 67, 78

### P76-F04: Deprecated 'bgcolor' HTML attribute
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Uses deprecated HTML attribute `bgcolor="white"` on body tag instead of CSS styling. This attribute was deprecated in HTML 4.01 and removed in HTML5.
**Fix:** Replace `<body bgcolor="white" ...>` with `<body style="background-color: white;" ...>` or preferably move styling to a CSS class/stylesheet.
**File:** reports/print_per_driver_summary.jsp, reports/print_per_veh_summary.jsp, reports/print_preop_chk.jsp, reports/print_preop_chk_detailed.jsp, reports/print_preop_chk_fail.jsp, reports/print_preop_chk_inc.jsp, reports/print_restricted_access_usage_report.jsp, reports/print_seat_hour_util.jsp
**Line(s):** 112, 112, 64, 97, 66, 97, 58, 77

### P76-F05: Inline JavaScript event handler (onload attribute)
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Uses inline JavaScript in the onload attribute (`onload="print('document');"`). Modern best practices recommend separating JavaScript from HTML using addEventListener or external script files for better maintainability and Content Security Policy compliance.
**Fix:** Move the print call to an external JavaScript file or at minimum to a script block, using addEventListener for the load event.
**File:** reports/print_monthly_online.jsp, reports/print_per_driver_summary.jsp, reports/print_per_veh_summary.jsp, reports/print_preop_chk.jsp, reports/print_preop_chk_detailed.jsp, reports/print_preop_chk_fail.jsp, reports/print_preop_chk_inc.jsp, reports/print_restricted_access_usage_report.jsp, reports/print_seat_hour_util.jsp
**Line(s):** 63, 112, 112, 64, 97, 66, 97, 58, 77

### P76-F06: Inconsistent use of i18n message tags
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** File contains hardcoded English text "Reporting System" instead of using <fmt:message> tag like other labels in the same page. This breaks internationalization consistency.
**Fix:** Replace hardcoded text with appropriate <fmt:message key="..."/> tag to maintain i18n consistency across the application.
**File:** reports/print_per_veh_summary.jsp
**Line(s):** 118

### P76-F07: HTML syntax error - double opening angle bracket
**Severity:** CRITICAL
**Description:** Line contains malformed HTML with double opening angle brackets: `<<th><fmt:message key="report.preopreport.date_time" />`. This will cause rendering errors in browsers.
**Fix:** Remove the extra opening angle bracket to make valid HTML: `<th><fmt:message key="report.preopreport.date_time" />`
**File:** reports/print_preop_chk_detailed.jsp
**Line(s):** 177

### P76-F08: HTML syntax error - missing closing bracket on tag
**Severity:** CRITICAL
**Description:** Line contains malformed HTML tag: `<b<fmt:message key="global.driverid"/></b>` missing the closing angle bracket after 'b'. This will cause rendering errors.
**Fix:** Add the missing closing angle bracket: `<b><fmt:message key="global.driverid"/></b>`
**File:** reports/print_preop_chk_fail.jsp
**Line(s):** 87

### P76-F09: Mixed use of LindeConfig and SessionVar for same purpose
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Files inconsistently use LindeConfig.reportLogo vs SessionVar.reportLogo for accessing the same configuration property. This leaky abstraction suggests unclear boundaries between configuration sources and makes it difficult to understand which config source is authoritative.
**Fix:** Standardize on a single configuration source (either LindeConfig or SessionVar) for all report configuration properties. Consolidate into one configuration bean.
**File:** reports/print_preop_chk_detailed.jsp, reports/print_preop_chk_inc.jsp
**Line(s):** 104-112, 104-113

### P76-F10: Commented-out include directives
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Contains commented-out JSP include directives for header and footer (`<%-- <%@ include file="../includes/report_export_header.jsp"%> --%>`). This suggests the page structure may be incomplete or in transition, leaving uncertainty about whether these includes should be present.
**Fix:** Either uncomment the includes if they are needed, or remove the commented code entirely if the page intentionally uses a different structure. Document the reason for the different structure if intentional.
**File:** reports/print_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 3, 28

### P76-F11: Session variables retrieved but never used
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variables access_level, access_cust, access_site, access_dept are retrieved from session but never used in the page logic. Unlike other report pages that pass these to a filter bean, this file does not utilize them.
**Fix:** Remove unused session variable retrieval (lines 15-18) to reduce code clutter and improve performance by avoiding unnecessary session access.
**File:** reports/print_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 15-18

### P76-F12: Unused loop counter variable 'col'
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Variable 'col' is declared and incremented within the loop but never actually used for any logic or display. It appears to be remnant code from a previous implementation.
**Fix:** Remove the unused variable declaration and increment statements (`int col = 0;` at line 193 and `col++;` at line 256).
**File:** reports/print_preop_chk_inc.jsp
**Line(s):** 193, 256

### P76-F13: Inconsistent close() call in onload handler
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** One file includes window.close() in the onload handler while other similar report pages do not. This inconsistency suggests different intended behaviors for reports that should likely be consistent.
**Fix:** Standardize the onload behavior across all report pages. If auto-close after print is desired, implement it consistently; otherwise, remove the window.close() call.
**File:** reports/print_report.jsp, reports/print_restricted_access_usage_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 58 (print_restricted_access_usage_report.jsp has both print and close)

### P76-F14: Duplicate code pattern for alternating row colors
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** All files contain nearly identical scriptlet blocks to alternate row background colors using if(i%2==0) logic. This represents code duplication that could be centralized or handled via CSS using :nth-child selectors.
**Fix:** Replace inline row color logic with CSS using the :nth-child(even) and :nth-child(odd) pseudo-selectors in the stylesheet, removing the scriptlet logic from JSP files.
**File:** All reviewed files (where applicable)
**Line(s):** Multiple locations across all files with data tables

### P76-F15: Inconsistent null-check patterns for request parameters
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Request parameter extraction uses inconsistent default values and patterns. Some use `request.getParameter("x")==null?"1":request.getParameter("x")` while others use `request.getParameter("x")==null?"":request.getParameter("x")`. The pattern duplicates the getParameter call unnecessarily.
**Fix:** Use a consistent helper method or ternary pattern that doesn't duplicate the getParameter() call, such as: `String param = request.getParameter("x"); param = param == null ? "default" : param;`
**File:** All reviewed files
**Line(s):** Multiple lines in scriptlet blocks (lines 8-31 in most files)

### P76-F16: Magic string "0:0:00" repeated throughout code
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** The time value "0:0:00" is used as a magic string throughout multiple files for comparisons. This should be defined as a constant for consistency and easier maintenance.
**Fix:** Define a constant `private static final String ZERO_TIME = "0:0:00";` in a shared utility class and reference it instead of the literal string.
**File:** reports/print_per_driver_summary.jsp, reports/print_per_veh_summary.jsp, reports/print_seat_hour_util.jsp
**Line(s):** 188, 192, 195, 199, 203, 207, 211 (print_per_driver_summary.jsp); similar patterns in other files

### P76-F17: Access level default of "5" is a magic number
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** The code assigns a default access level of "5" when the session attribute is empty or missing. This magic number should be defined as a named constant explaining what level 5 represents.
**Fix:** Define a constant `public static final String DEFAULT_ACCESS_LEVEL = "5"; // Full access` in a shared configuration class and use it instead of the literal "5".
**File:** All reviewed files with access control
**Line(s):** 29-30 (print_monthly_online.jsp), 42-44 (print_per_driver_summary.jsp), and similar in other files

### P76-F18: Empty script tag without content
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Line 16 in print_per_driver_summary.jsp contains a closing `</script>` tag without a corresponding opening tag or content, appearing after CSS link tags. This is malformed HTML.
**Fix:** Remove the orphaned `</script>` tag.
**File:** reports/print_per_driver_summary.jsp
**Line(s):** 16

### P76-F19: Inconsistent error handling for empty/null access_level
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Uses `.equalsIgnoreCase("")` to check for empty access_level, which will throw NullPointerException if access_level is null rather than empty string. The session.getAttribute() can return null.
**Fix:** Change condition to handle null safely: `if(access_level == null || access_level.isEmpty())` or use StringUtils.isBlank() if available.
**File:** All reviewed files with access control
**Line(s):** 28 (print_monthly_online.jsp), 42 (print_per_driver_summary.jsp), and similar in other files

### File 1: reports/print_serv_maintenance.jsp

## Findings

### P77-F01: Commented-out debug code
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Line 123 contains a commented-out System.out.println debug statement "//System.out.println("THIS: " + user_cd);" which serves no purpose in production code and clutters the codebase. Commented debug code should be removed.
**Fix:** Delete line 123 entirely.
**File:** reports/print_serv_maintenance.jsp
**Line(s):** 123

### P77-F02: Commented-out HTML body tag
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Line 56 contains a commented-out body tag "<!--  <body>  -->" which appears to be leftover from development or debugging. This provides no value and should be removed.
**Fix:** Delete line 56 entirely.
**File:** reports/print_shock_summary.jsp
**Line(s):** 56

### P77-F03: Commented-out table header code
**Severity:** MEDIUM
**Description:** Lines 66 and 87 contain commented-out JSP code for displaying "Service Hour From" column. If this functionality is no longer needed, the commented code should be removed. If it might be reinstated, it should be documented why it's commented.
**Fix:** Either remove the commented code entirely or add a comment explaining why it's preserved.
**File:** reports/print_super_master_auth_report.jsp
**Line(s):** 66, 87

### P77-F04: Commented-out HTML body tag
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Line 94 contains a commented-out body tag "<!--  <body>  -->" which appears to be leftover from development or debugging. This provides no value and should be removed.
**Fix:** Delete line 94 entirely.
**File:** reports/print_unit_unlock.jsp
**Line(s):** 94

### P77-F05: Commented-out HTML body tag
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Line 78 contains a commented-out body tag "<!--  <body>  -->" which appears to be leftover from development or debugging. This provides no value and should be removed.
**Fix:** Delete line 78 entirely.
**File:** reports/print_unit_unlock_impact.jsp
**Line(s):** 78

### P77-F06: HTML syntax error
**Severity:** HIGH
**Description:** Line 224 contains a malformed closing tag "/td>" instead of the proper closing tag "</td>". This is invalid HTML syntax that could cause rendering issues or break HTML parsers.
**Fix:** Change "/td>" to "</td>" on line 224.
**File:** reports/print_unit_unlock_impact.jsp
**Line(s):** 224

### P77-F07: Commented-out HTML body tag
**Severity:** LOW
**Description:** Line 66 contains a commented-out body tag "<!--  <body>  -->" which appears to be leftover from development or debugging. This provides no value and should be removed.
**Fix:** Delete line 66 entirely.
**File:** reports/print_unit_unlock_question.jsp
**Line(s):** 66

### P77-F08: Commented-out HTML body tag
**Severity:** LOW
